definition.en: The mind is the tool which must be used.
definition.zh: 心智是必须使用的工具。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[1]

definition.en: The mind is the connective. It is here that the secrets of the universe will be discovered, and the mind itself is the tool of discovery.
definition.zh: 心智是连接体。宇宙的秘密将在此被发现，而心智本身就是发现的工具。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[2]

definition.en: A mind is a psychic pattern through which you interpret and form reality.
definition.zh: 心智是一种心灵模式，你通过它诠释并形成实相。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[5]

definition.en: The mind does not take up space, and yet the mind is the value that gives power to the brain.
definition.zh: 心智不占据空间，然而心智是赋予大脑力量的价值。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[8]

definition.en: The mind is distributed throughout the whole physical body.
definition.zh: 心智遍布整个物质身体。
definition.source: Session 未明确标注，但出自摘录[1]

explanation:
心智是遍布全身的感知与创造工具，它不局限于大脑，而是通过整个身体接收内在感官的信息，并将这些无形能量转化为你在物质世界能体验到的具体现实。大脑只处理物质伪装模式，可以被物理仪器探测；而心智处理所有层面的基本原则，无法被任何物理手段找到或测量。心智是连接内在与外在的桥梁，是探索宇宙秘密的工具。

心智拥有自己的“文明”、地理和历史，它紧密关联于“广袤当下”，不占据空间，也不以时间为基本存在方式。意识心智专注于物质现实，但容易误以为物质现象是原因而非结果。当意识心智接受太多错误信念，特别是将内在自我视为威胁时，就会关闭来自深层自我的提醒，失去应有的安全感。

心智是心理模式，

quotes.text: Einstein used the miraculous aspects of his mind. Parts of the mind are almost completely undistorted. The mind is distributed throughout the whole physical body. The mind builds up about it the physical camouflages necessary for existence on your physical plane. The mind receives data from the inner senses and forms the camouflage necessary. The mind unconsciously or unselfconsciously deals with the basic laws according to the camouflage effect that is vital for survival on your physical plane. The mind is the tool which must be used.
quotes.translation: 爱因斯坦运用了他心智的神奇面向。心智的某些部分几乎完全未被扭曲。心智遍布整个物质身体。心智在其周围构建出你们物质层面生存所必需的物质伪装。心智接收来自内在感官的数据，并形成必要的伪装。心智无意识或不自觉地根据对你们物质层面生存至关重要的伪装效应来处理基本法则。心智是必须使用的工具。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The brain deals exclusively with camouflage patterns, transforming vitality into physical environmental camouflage patterns. The mind deals with basic principles inherent on all planes. The brain is itself part of the camouflage pattern, and can be interpreted and probed by physical instruments. The mind cannot be probed by physical instruments. It cannot even be found by physical instruments. The mind is the connective. It is here that the secrets of the universe will be discovered, and the mind itself is the tool of discovery.
quotes.translation: 大脑专门处理伪装模式，将生命力转化为物质环境伪装模式。心智处理所有层面固有的基本原则。大脑本身就是伪装模式的一部分，可以用物质仪器来解读和探测。心智无法用物质仪器探测。它甚至无法被物质仪器找到。心智是连接体。正是在这里，宇宙的秘密将被发现，而心智本身就是发现的工具。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The inner self is embarked upon an exciting endeavor, in which it learns how to translate its reality into physical terms. The conscious mind is brilliantly attuned to physical reality, then, and often so dazzled by what it perceives that it is tempted to think physical phenomena is a cause, rather than a result. Deeper portions of the self always serve to remind it that this is not the case. When the conscious mind accepts too many false beliefs, particularly if it sees that inner self as a danger, then it closes out these constant reminders. When this situation arises the conscious mind feels itself assailed by a reality that seems greater than itself, over which it has no control. The deep feeling of security in which it should be anchored is lost.
quotes.translation: 内在自体正投身于一项激动人心的事业，在其中它学习如何将其实相转化为物质语言。那么，有意识心智与物质实相完美协调，常常被它所感知的事物如此炫目，以至于它倾向于认为物质现象是原因而非结果。自体的更深部分总是提醒它事实并非如此。当有意识心智接受了太多错误信念，特别是当它视内在自体为危险时，它就会关闭这些持续的提醒。当这种情况发生时，有意识心智会感到被一个似乎比它自身更强大、它无法控制的实相所攻击。它本应扎根其中的深层安全感便丧失了。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... (Long pause, eyes closed.) There are lands of the mind.5 That is, the mind has its own "civilizations," its own personal culture and geography, its own history and inclinations. But the mind is connected with the physical brain, and so hidden in its [the brain's] folds there is an archaeological memory. To some extent what you know now is dependent upon what will be known, and what has been known, in your terms. The "past" races of men live to that extent within your Now, as do those who will seemingly come after. So, ideally speaking, the history of your species can be discovered quite clearly within the psyche; and true archaeological events are found not only by uncovering rocks and relics, but by bringing to light, so to speak, the memories that dwell within the psyche.
quotes.translation: 请给我们一点时间……（长时间停顿，闭着眼睛。）存在着心智的国度。也就是说，心智有它自己的"文明"，它自己的个人文化和地理，它自己的历史和倾向。但心智与物质大脑相连，因此隐藏在大脑的褶皱中存在着一种考古学记忆。在某种程度上，你们现在所知道的取决于在你们的时间概念中将会被知道的和已经被知道的。人类的"过去"种族就在那个程度上活在你们的当下之中，那些看似后来者也是如此。所以，理想地说，你们物种的历史可以在心灵中相当清晰地被发现；而真正的考古学事件不仅通过发掘岩石和遗迹来发现，而且可以说，是通过揭示居住在心灵中的记忆来发现的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Earlier this evening I reminded Jane of the conversation about cells, versus their components, that we'd had because of Seth's material at 9:38 in Session 705. Some of tonight's book work refers to the questions growing out of our talk I think, as does Seth's brief clarification near the end of the session.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It seems evident to you that one person has one mind. You identify with the mind you use. If you had another, then it would seem as if you must be someone else. A mind is a psychic pattern through which you interpret and form reality. You have physical limbs that you can see. You have minds that are invisible. Each one can organize reality in a different fashion. Each one deals with its own kind of knowledge.
quotes.translation: 在你们看来，一个人只有一个心智似乎是显而易见的。你们认同于你们所使用的心智。如果你们有另一个心智，那么似乎你们必定是另一个人。心智是一种心灵模式，通过它你们诠释并形成实相。你们有看得见的物质肢体。你们有看不见的心智。每一个心智都能以不同的方式组织实相。每一个心智都处理它自己种类的知识。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The conscious mind was [therefore] expected to perform alone, so to speak, ignoring the highly intuitive inner information that is also available to it. It was not supposed to be aware of such data. Yet any individual knows quite well that intuitive hunches, inspiration, precognitive information or clairvoyant material has often risen to conscious knowledge. Usually it is shoved away and disregarded because you have been taught that the conscious mind should not hold with such "nonsense." So you have been told to trust your conscious mind, while at the same time you were led to believe it could only be aware of stimuli that came to it from the outside physical world.
quotes.translation: 因此，有意识心智被期望独自运作，可以说，忽略同样可供它使用的高度直觉的内在信息。它本不应意识到这类数据。然而，任何个体都很清楚，直觉预感、灵感、预知信息或千里眼材料常常上升到有意识知识层面。通常它被推开并忽视，因为你们被教导有意识心智不应持有这种"胡言乱语"。所以你们被告知要信任你们的有意识心智，而同时你们又被引导相信它只能意识到来自外部物质世界的刺激。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The mind will be found to be closely allied with the spacious present. The mind's capabilities, if studied, would lead man into a realization of these other fields of actuality of which I have spoken. The mind deals with intangibles, but it does not deal with unrealities. Again, the validity of an actuality is not to be determined by its appearance within matter alone. The ingredients of matter are first of all intangible ingredients, and the study of the mind and a study of the processes by which the mind creates its dream images could lead to a basic understanding of the manner in which man subconsciously produces the physical images of his own material universe.
quotes.translation: 心灵将被发现与广袤当下紧密相连。心灵的能力若经研究，将引导人类认识到我所说的那些实相领域的其他面向。心灵处理无形之物，但并非处理虚幻之物。再次强调，实相的有效性不能仅凭其在物质中的显现来判定。物质的成分首先是无形的成分，对心灵的研究以及对心灵创造梦境意象过程的研究，可引导人基本理解人类潜意识如何产生自身物质宇宙的物理影像。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: A flower cannot write a poem about itself. You can, and in so doing your own consciousness turns around about itself. It literally becomes more than it was. Existing in such diversified, rich environment-possibilities, the human psyche needed and developed a conscious mind that could make fairly concise and accurate "minute by minute" judgments and evaluations. As the conscious mind grew, now, so did the range of imagination. The conscious mind is a vehicle for the imagination in many ways. The greater its knowledge the further the reach of imagination. In return imagination enriches conscious reasoning and emotional experience.
quotes.translation: 花朵无法为自己写诗。你能做到，而在这样做时，你自己的意识会围绕自身旋转。它确实变得比原来更多。存在于如此多样化、丰富的环境可能性中，人类心灵需要并发展出一个能够做出相当简洁准确"每分钟"判断和评估的有意识心智。随着有意识心智的成长，想象力的范围也随之扩展。有意识心智在许多方面是想象力的载体。其知识越广博，想象力所能触及的范围就越远。反过来，想象力又丰富了有意识推理和情感体验。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I have said that the mind cannot be detected by your instruments at present. The mind does not take up space, and yet the mind is the value that gives power to the brain. The mind expands continuously, both in individual terms and in terms of the species as a whole, and yet (with amusement) the mind takes up neither more nor less space, whether it be the mind of a flea or a man.
quotes.translation: 我曾说过，你们目前的仪器无法检测到心灵。心灵不占据空间，然而心灵是赋予大脑力量的价值。心灵持续扩展，无论从个体角度还是从物种整体角度而言，然而（带着笑意）无论是一只跳蚤的心灵还是一个人的心灵，心灵既不占据更多也不占据更少的空间。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 705)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: An individual can tune into spacious-mind operation two or three times in a lifetime without realizing it, and have experiences that he finds difficult to interpret later. The affirmation involved is one of transcendence, in which for a time a person affirms his reality in flesh and at the same time states his independence from it (smile) -- and realizes that both of these conditions exist simultaneously. A dual perception takes place in which the spacious mind is activated. By "activated" I mean that the physical organism is suddenly aware of [the spacious mind's] existence.
quotes.translation: 个体在一生中可能有两三次在不自知的情况下进入广袤心灵运作状态，并经历后来难以解释的体验。所涉及的肯定是一种超越，在其中一个人暂时肯定了自己在肉身中的实相，同时声明自己独立于肉身（微笑）——并意识到这两种状态同时存在。一种双重感知发生，广袤心灵被激活。所谓"激活"，我指的是物理有机体突然意识到[广袤心灵]的存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In certain terms, the ego is the eye through which the conscious mind perceives, or the focus through which it views physical reality. But the conscious mind automatically changes its focus throughout life. The ego, while appearing the same to itself, ever changes. It is only when the conscious mind becomes rigid in its direction, or allows the ego to take on some of its own functions, that difficulties arise. Then the ego allows the conscious mind to work in certain directions and blocks its awareness in others.
quotes.translation: 从某种角度说，自我是眼睛，有意识心智通过它感知，或是它聚焦物理实相的焦点。但有意识心智在一生中会自动改变其焦点。自我，虽然对自己而言看似相同，却不断变化。只有当有意识心智在其方向上变得僵化，或允许自我承担其某些功能时，困难才会出现。那时自我允许有意识心智在某些方向上运作，同时阻碍其在其他方向上的觉知。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If the dream world, the mind, and the inner universe do exist, but not in space, and if they do not exist basically in time, though they may be glimpsed through time, then your question will be: In what medium or in what manner do they exist, and without time, how can they be said to exist in duration? I am telling you that the basic universe exists behind all camouflage universes in the same manner, and taking up no space, that the mind exists behind the brain. The brain is a camouflage pattern. It takes up space. It exists in time, but the mind takes up no space and does not have its basic existence in time. Your camouflage universe, on the other hand, takes up space and exists in time.
quotes.translation: 如果梦境世界、心灵和内在宇宙确实存在，但不在空间中，并且它们基本上不存在于时间中，尽管可能通过时间瞥见它们，那么你的问题将是：它们存在于何种媒介或何种方式中？没有时间，又如何能说它们存在于持续中？我告诉你，基本宇宙以同样的方式存在于所有伪装宇宙之后，不占据空间，就像心灵存在于大脑之后一样。大脑是一个伪装模式。它占据空间，存在于时间中，但心灵不占据空间，其基本存在不在时间中。而你们的伪装宇宙则占据空间并存在于时间中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: That is the analogy. In actuality the mind is but a which looks out for the personality on the camouflage plane. Your guardian angel legends and such refer to this part of the entity, which is the mind and which is attached to the present personality during this particular existence. The mind helps to keep the personality from going too far astray. I use the term personality to include the whole person. I use it to express the entirety manifested in physical form, in one life.
quotes.translation: 这只是一个类比。实际上，心智只是人格在伪装层上的一个瞭望者。你们关于守护天使的传说等，指的就是存有的这一部分——即心智，它在这次特定存在期间与当前人格相连。心智帮助防止人格过度偏离正轨。我使用"人格"一词来指代完整的人，用以表达在某一世中以物质形态显现的整体性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: There are more than your thought forms in other words. Your thought forms however can be used as definite aids when you are in the proper mental condition, and they can impede your progress if you are not. In physical reality a man in a desperate frame of mind is more apt to emphasize horrible aspects in the news, for example, and to see desperation rather than joy in the faces of those he meets. He will ignore the contented playful child on one side of the street, and notice instead a dirty ragged boy even though he be further away. So your frame of mind when projecting will largely determine the sort of experiences you have, and the environments you visit.
quotes.translation: 换句话说，你们的思想形态之外还有更多存在。不过，当你们处于适当的心智状态时，思想形态可以作为明确的助力；若状态不佳，它们则会阻碍你们的进展。在物质实相中，一个处于绝望心境的人更容易强调新闻中可怕的一面，例如，在遇见的人脸上看到绝望而非喜悦。他会忽略街对面那个满足嬉戏的孩子，反而注意到更远处一个肮脏褴褛的男孩。因此，你们投射时的心境将在很大程度上决定你们所经历的体验类型和所造访的环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The physical body, in other words, exists as an electrical body that is not material, that has a peculiar mass but no weight, whose characteristics are apparent in terms of not varying shapes, but varying intensities and concentrations of electric force. In some respects there are differences. The mind, which is not physically represented in the material body, does exist electrically. The brain, which exists physically, is a part of the mind in the electric structure. The organs exist electrically. The skin does not exist within this electric counterpart, although the physical skin does contain electric force.
quotes.translation: 换言之，物质身体以电性身体的形式存在——它并非物质，具有特殊质量但无重量，其特性表现为电力强度的变化与集中，而非形状的变化。在某些方面存在差异。心智在物质身体中没有物理表征，却以电性方式存在。物质存在的脑部，是心智电性结构的一部分。器官以电性方式存在。皮肤在这个电性对应物中并不存在，尽管物质皮肤确实含有电力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: ("It is a universal truth that whatever you impress on your subconscious mind is expressed on the screen of space as condition, experience, and event. Thought is incipient action. The reaction is a response from your subconscious mind which corresponds with the nature of your thought. Your subconscious mind can be likened to the soil which can grow all kinds of seeds, good or bad. Every thought is a cause and every condition an effect."
quotes.translation: "这是一条普遍真理：无论你在潜意识中铭刻什么，都会在空间的银幕上显现为状况、体验和事件。思想是初期的行动。反应来自你的潜意识，其性质与你的思想相对应。你的潜意识可被比作土壤，能生长各种种子——无论好坏。每个思想都是因，每个状况都是果。"
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: When you are certain that you feel at least reasonably secure, where you are, then we shall take you to where you are not and you will find yourself. You are already there, but in order to go there, you must start from somewhere. You must start with, you see, a balance and a degree of security.... You must start with confidence. And if you travel too quickly and too far, you will not have confidence, for in the back of your mind you will think: if I do not feel secure in physical reality, then why should I feel secure here... and you will not have the daring that is necessary, nor will you have the peace of mind... and the peace of mind is the key to the door.
quotes.translation: 当你确信自己在所处之地至少感到合理安全时，我们便会带你前往你不在之处，而你将找到自己。你早已在那里，但为了抵达那里，你必须从某处启程。你必须带着平衡与一定程度的安全感出发……你必须带着信心启程。若你行进过快过远，便会失去信心，因为在你脑海深处会想：若我在物质实相中都不觉安全，为何在此处就该感到安全……于是你将缺乏必要的勇气，也得不到心灵的平静——而心灵的平静正是开启那扇门的钥匙。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Some of this has to do with distorted ideas of both the conscious and unconscious minds, using your terms now. Generally speaking, in Western society the conscious mind is seen as coming into its own in early adulthood, as the self rises from the bed of childhood unconsciousness into its critical awareness and differentiation. The appreciation of distinctions and differences is considered one of the greatest characteristics of consciousness, and so those aspects of it are valued. On the other hand the equally significant assimilating, combining, correlating characteristics of consciousness are overlooked. In scholarly circles, and many that are not scholarly at all, the intellect is equated only with the critical faculties, so that the more diagnostic you are the more intellectual you are considered.
quotes.translation: 其中部分涉及对显意识与潜意识两者的扭曲认知——此处沿用你们的术语。一般而言，在西方社会，显意识被视为在成年早期臻于成熟，如同自我从童年无意识的床榻中升起，进入批判性觉察与分化。对区别与差异的辨识被视为意识最伟大的特征之一，因此这些方面备受珍视。然而，意识同样重要的同化、整合、关联特性却被忽视。在学术界及许多非学术领域，智力仅等同于批判能力，因此你越擅长诊断，就被认为越有智慧。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your conscious mind tells you where you are in time and space, and directs your activity in a world of human action. That world has its own kind of rich complication, that is as unknown to the animals as is much of their acute realization unknown to you. Because you have a conscious mind, then, other portions of your being rely upon it to give them an adequate picture of your situation, and to give the conscious orders for action. These orders will then be carried out. To do this, you must use that mind as completely as possible. The picture of reality in time and space that you give to your cells must be accurate. They must act on a minute-to-minute, second-to-second, microsecond-to-microsecond basis, even though their own orientation is not familiar with your time concept.
quotes.translation: 你的意识心告诉你你在时空中的位置，并指引你在人类行动世界中的活动。那个世界有其自身丰富的复杂性，如同动物们敏锐的觉知对你而言是未知的，那个世界对动物们来说也是未知的。正因你拥有意识心，你存在的其他部分便依赖它来提供关于你处境的充分图像，并下达行动的意识指令。这些指令随后将被执行。为此，你必须尽可能充分地运用那个心智。你给予细胞的关于时空现实的图像必须准确。它们必须基于分分秒秒、秒秒、微秒微秒地行动，即便它们自身的取向并不熟悉你的时间概念。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 691
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The people count upon the government to realistically define the conditions of the world, to have proper intelligence so that the activities in foreign lands are known, to keep up proper communication with other governments, and so forth. Now in some important respects the reasoning mind is like the government in this analogy. If the people in power are paranoid, then they overestimate the dangers of any given world situation. They overreact, or overmobilize, using a disproportionate amount of energy and time for defense, and taking energies away from other projects. The reasoning mind acts in the same fashion when paranoid beliefs are in power. It therefore tells all of the citizens -- or cells of the body -- to mobilize for action, to be on the alert, to pare down all but necessary activities, and so forth.
quotes.translation: 人民依赖政府来现实地定义世界的状况，拥有适当的情报以了解外国的活动，与其他政府保持适当的沟通，等等。现在，在某些重要方面，推理心智就像这个比喻中的政府。如果当权者偏执多疑，那么他们就会高估任何特定世界局势的危险。他们反应过度，或过度动员，使用不成比例的能量和时间用于防御，并从其他项目中抽走能量。当偏执信念掌权时，推理心智也以同样的方式行动。因此，它告诉所有公民——或身体的细胞——动员起来采取行动，保持警惕，削减所有非必要的活动，等等。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: (11:37.) There is a give-and-take chemical reaction, or rather chemical rhythms of reactions, that are far more effective in the shorter sleep periods. Many of you sleep through periods that should be those of your greatest creativity and alertness, in which the conscious and unconscious are most beautifully focused and at one. The conscious mind is often drugged with sleep just when it could be deriving its greatest benefits from the unconscious, and be able to poise most meaningfully in the reality that you know. In these instances the beauty and illumination of your dream state can be clear in the conscious mind, and used to enrich your physical life. Contrasts in your experience will appear to you in their united clarity.
quotes.translation: (11:37。) 有一种给予与获取的化学反应，或者更确切地说，是化学反应的节律，在较短的睡眠期间内要有效得多。你们许多人睡过了那些本应是你们最具创造力和警觉性的时期，在这些时期里，意识与无意识最为美妙地聚焦并合一。意识心常常被睡眠麻醉，而恰恰在此时它本可以从无意识中获得最大益处，并能够最富有意义地立足于你所知的现实中。在这些情况下，你梦境状态的美与启迪可以在意识心中清晰显现，并被用来丰富你的物质生活。你经验中的对比将以它们统一的清晰性向你呈现。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ours is the voice that whispers through the corridors. The very matter of your physical brain has its greater reality in the mind, and behind that mind is another mind, and behind that another, and yet all of this is your mind. The mind of the larger self you do not know.
quotes.translation: 我们的声音是那在走廊中低语的声音。你物质大脑的实质本身在心智中拥有其更大的实相，而在那个心智背后是另一个心智，在那个心智背后又是另一个，然而所有这些都是你的心智。你所不知的更大自我的心智。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The conscious mind can, for instance, see a rose as a symbol of life or death, or joy or sadness, and under certain conditions its interpretation of a simple flower can trigger deep experiences that call up power and strength from the inner resources of being. Since the attributes of egotistical consciousness have been so misinterpreted, you usually consider it only in its analytical breaking-down functions. These are very important as it separates larger fields of perception into smaller ones that can be physically understood. But the conscious mind is also a great synthesizer. It brings together diverse elements from your experience and unites them in new patterns.
quotes.translation: 例如，意识心可以将一朵玫瑰视为生命或死亡、喜悦或悲伤的象征，并且在某些条件下，它对一朵简单花朵的诠释可以触发深刻的体验，从存在的内在资源中唤起力量与强度。由于自我意识的性质被如此误解，你通常只考虑其分析拆解的功能。这些功能非常重要，因为它将较大的感知领域分割成较小的、可以在物质上理解的领域。但意识心也是一个伟大的综合者。它将来自你经验的不同元素汇集在一起，并将它们结合成新的模式。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your painting was meant to bring out from the recesses of your being the accumulation of your knowledge in the form of images -- not of people you might meet now on the street, but portraits of the residents of the mind. The residents of the mind are very real. In a certain fashion, they are your parents more than your parents were, and when you express their realities, they are also expressing yours. All time is simultaneous. Only the illusion of time on each of your parts keeps you from greeting each other. To some extent, when you paint such portraits you are forming psychic bridges between yourself and those other selves: Your own identity as yourself grows.
quotes.translation: 你的画作旨在从你存在的深处，以图像的形式引出你知识的积累——不是你现在可能在街上遇到的人，而是心智居民的肖像。心智的居民非常真实。从某种方式来说，他们比你的父母更像是你的父母，当你表达他们的实相时，他们也在表达你的实相。所有时间都是同时的。只有你们每个人身上的时间幻象阻止了你们彼此问候。在某种程度上，当你绘制这样的肖像时，你正在你自己与那些其他自我之间形成心灵桥梁：你作为自己的身份得以成长。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 834, FEBRUARY 5, 1979
8:59 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I'm personally intrigued, of course, that this book was written through me, without my conscious mind there at every point, anxiously checking, organizing, and criticizing, as it does in my own work. Then, while my creative and intuitional abilities are given a good deal of freedom, the conscious mind is definitely in control. Yet this book was not written "by itself," in the same way that some poems seem to be. Often a writer will say that a certain book "wrote itself," and I know what that means. In this case, however, the book came from a specific source, not just from "out there," and it is colored by the author's personality, which is not mine.
quotes.translation: 我本人自然感到好奇，这本书是通过我写成的，而我的意识心并未在每一处都焦虑地检查、组织和批评——就像它在我自己的作品中那样。然后，当我的创造力和直觉能力被给予相当多的自由时，意识心却明确地掌控着。然而，这本书并非像某些诗歌那样“自行”写成。作家常说某本书“自己写成了”，我明白那是什么意思。但在这个案例中，这本书来自一个特定的源头，并非仅仅来自“外面”，而且它被作者的个性所渲染，而那个个性并非我的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth began dictating the book in our next Session 511, January 21, 1970, and finished it in Session 591, August 11, 1971. The intervening sessions did not all involve book dictation, however. Some were devoted to personal matters, some given for specific people who needed help, and some were in answer to philosophical questions not connected with the book. I also took several "little vacations." Despite such layoffs, Seth always picked right up, precisely where he left off dictation.

quotes.text: The conscious mind is endlessly creative. This applies to all areas of conscious-mind thinking. It is also the organizer of physical data, so natural guilt became the basis for all kinds of variations. These closely followed man's religious and social groupings. The latter are also the result of the aware mind's capacity to play upon, mix and merge, and rearrange perception and experience.
quotes.translation: 意识心是无限创造性的。这适用于意识心思维的所有领域。它也是物质数据的组织者，因此自然罪恶感成为各种变体的基础。这些变体紧密跟随人类的宗教和社会群体。后者也是觉察心玩弄、混合、融合和重新排列感知与经验的能力的结果。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now: Ruburt's mind is far more aware of other realities than his brain is, but he consciously believes in the greater reality of himself and his perceptions. The brain also possesses this belief, and so it opens itself as much as possible to the mind's activities. Because it does, certain intuitive psychic and "intellectually spacious" experiences can be physically felt to some extent. The knowledge is interpreted through alterations in body sensation, which give it an important corporeal validity. In such cases high mental and psychic activity is reflected in the body's experience, providing a beneficial unity.
quotes.translation: 现在：鲁柏的心智远比他的大脑更觉察其他实相，但他有意识地相信自己和自己的感知具有更大的实相。大脑也拥有这种信念，因此它尽可能地向心智的活动敞开自己。正因为如此，某些直觉性的心灵和“智力上开阔”的体验可以在某种程度上被身体感受到。知识通过身体感觉的变化被诠释，这赋予了它重要的肉体有效性。在这种情况下，高度的心智和心灵活动反映在身体的体验中，提供了有益的统一。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Emotion and imagination are being considered as far superior. The displaced powers of consciousness are still being assigned to the unconscious, and great efforts are being made to reach what seem to be normally inaccessible areas of awareness. To this end drugs are utilized, cults set up, and there are methods and training manuals galore. Period. Yet there is nothing basically inaccessible about such "inner knowledge or experience." It can all be quite conscious, and utilized to enrich the reality that you know. The conscious mind is not some prodigal child or poor relative of the self. It can quite freely focus into inner reality when you understand that it can. You, again, have a conscious mind. You can change the focus of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 情感和想象力被认为远为优越。被取代的意识力量仍然被分配给无意识，并且人们正付出巨大努力去触及那些看似通常无法触及的意识领域。为此，药物被使用，邪教被建立，还有大量的方法和训练手册。句号。然而，这种“内在知识或体验”基本上没有什么不可触及的。它完全可以是有意识的，并用来丰富你所知的实相。意识心并非自我的某个浪子或穷亲戚。当你理解它可以时，它可以相当自由地聚焦于内在实相。你，再次，拥有一个意识心。你可以改变你自己意识的焦点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When large doses of chemicals are used, the conscious mind is confronted full blast with very potent experiences that it was not meant to handle, and by which it is purposely made to feel powerless. (Pause.) Faced with the exterior nightmares of wars and natural disasters, the conscious mind is still directed outward into that world with which it knows it was formed to cope. In periods of great physical stress it draws upon the powers of the body and inner self to perform remarkable feats of heroism -- that leave it wondering afterward at the power and energy of the self in crisis.
quotes.translation: 当使用大剂量的化学物质时，意识心会全盘面对非常强烈的体验，这些体验本不该由它处理，并且它被故意弄得感到无力。（停顿。）面对战争和自然灾害的外部噩梦，意识心仍然被导向外部世界，它知道自己被塑造来应对那个世界。在巨大的肉体压力时期，它汲取身体和内在自我的力量，执行非凡的英雄壮举——之后让它对危机中自我的力量和能量感到惊奇。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There are techniques for using cameras,1 and a camera left at home will do you little good abroad. So it is the conscious alert mind that must take these pictures if you hope to later make sense of your inner journeys. That conscious reasoning mind must therefore be taken along. There are many ways of doing this, methods not really difficult to follow. Certain techniques will help you pack your conscious mind for your journey as you would pack your camera. It will be there when you need it, to take the pictures that will be your conscious memories of your journey.
quotes.translation: 使用相机有技巧，1 而一台留在家的相机在国外对你没什么好处。所以，如果你希望后来理解你的内在旅程，必须是警觉的意识心拍摄这些照片。因此，那个有意识的推理心必须被带上。有许多方法可以做到这一点，这些方法并不难遵循。某些技巧会帮助你为旅程打包你的意识心，就像你打包相机一样。当你需要它时，它会在那里，拍摄那些将成为你旅程有意识记忆的照片。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 710
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The ego is a very specialized portion of your greater identity. It is a portion of you that arises to deal directly with the life that the larger You is living. The ego can feel cut off, lonely and frightened, however, if the conscious mind lets the ego run away with it. The ego and the conscious mind are not the same thing. The ego is composed of various portions of the personality -- it is a combination of characteristics, ever-changing, that act in unitary fashion -- the portion of the personality that deals most directly with the world.
quotes.translation: 自我是你更大身份中一个非常专门化的部分。它是你的一部分，用来直接处理更大的你所经历的生活。然而，如果意识心让自我牵着鼻子走，自我可能会感到孤立、孤独和恐惧。自我和意识心并非同一回事。自我由人格的各种部分构成——它是各种特征的组合，不断变化，以统一的方式运作——是人格中最直接处理世界的部分。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The entity in this case would be compared to the mind. The brain would be more or less what it is, that is, the brain of the present personality existing on a camouflaged plane. As the brain gives orders and communicates messages to the various parts of the physical body, so would the mind or entity do in like manner. The mind would contain all data having to do with past existences and intertwining purposes, problems and relationships, but it would only give such data to the brain as was necessary for its present existence.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，存有可以被比作心智。大脑或多或少就是它本身，即存在于伪装层面上的当前人格的大脑。正如大脑向身体各部位下达指令并传递信息，心智或存有也会以类似方式运作。心智将包含与过去存在、交织的目的、问题和关系有关的所有数据，但它只会将当前存在所必需的数据传递给大脑。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: These minds all work together to keep you alive through the physical structure of the brain. When you use all of these minds, then and only then do you become fully aware of your surroundings: You perceive reality more clearly than you do now, more sharply, brilliantly, and concisely. At the same time, however, you comprehend it directly. You comprehend what it is apart from your physical perception of it. You accept as yourself those other states of consciousness native to your other minds. You achieve true personhood.
quotes.translation: 这些心智共同协作，通过大脑的物理结构维持你的生命。当你运用所有这些心智时，只有那时你才能完全意识到你的环境：你比现在更清晰、更锐利、更明亮、更简洁地感知实相。同时，你直接理解它。你理解它在你物理感知之外的本质。你接受那些属于你其他心智的、与生俱来的其他意识状态作为自己的一部分。你实现了真正的人格。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Left alone, the self acts spontaneously as a unit, but as an ever-changing one. Listening to voices both within and without, the conscious mind is able to form beliefs that are in league with the self's knowledge as received from material and nonmaterial sources. Then examination of beliefs takes its place along with other activities -- naturally, easily, without effort. Once the conscious mind has accepted a collection of conflicting beliefs, however, a definite attempt is necessary to sort these out.
quotes.translation: 当独处时，自我会自发地作为一个整体运作，但这是一个不断变化的整体。倾听内在和外在的声音，意识心能够形成与自我从物质和非物质来源获得的知识相一致的信念。然后，对信念的审视与其他活动一起自然、轻松、毫不费力地进行。然而，一旦意识心接受了一组相互冲突的信念，就必须做出明确的努力来理清它们。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In periods of play the child actually often continues some games initiated quite naturally in the dream state. These include role-playing, and also games that quite simply involve physical muscular activity. All of this teaches a specification. In dreams the mind is free to play with events, and with their formation. The actualization of those events, however, requires certain practical circumstances. In play the children try out events initiated in the dream state, and "judge" these against the practical conditions. In such a way the child juggles probabilities, and also brings his physical structure precisely into line with a given niche of probability. Basically (underlined twice) in dreaming the brain is not limited to physically encountered experience.
quotes.translation: 在玩耍期间，孩子实际上经常继续一些在梦境状态中自然开始的游戏。这些包括角色扮演，以及简单涉及身体肌肉活动的游戏。所有这些都教会了具体化。在梦中，心智可以自由地玩弄事件及其形成过程。然而，这些事件的实际化需要特定的实际环境。在玩耍中，孩子们尝试在梦境状态中开始的事件，并对照实际条件“判断”它们。通过这种方式，孩子权衡可能性，并将他的身体结构精确地调整到特定的可能性空间。基本上（双重下划线强调），在梦中，大脑不受限于物理经历的经验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Memory was so perfected that men at one time were indeed living histories, and carried within their minds their genealogies and backgrounds and the knowledge of their peoples, which were then passed on to their children. It is true that reading and writing have certain advantages over such procedures, but it is also true that knowledge possessed in that old fashion became a part of a man, and a society, in a much more personal, meaningful manner. It was, of course, a different kind of knowing. At its best it did not lead to rote renditions of remembered material, but to dramatic renderings of it through music, poetry, dancing. In other words, its rendition was accompanied by creative physical expression. It is true that, practically speaking, a man's mind, or a woman's, could not hold all of the information available now in your world--but much of that information does not deal with basic knowledge about the universe or man's place within it. It is a kind of secondary information--interesting, but not life-giving.
quotes.translation: 记忆曾经如此完美，以至于人们一度确实是活生生的历史，他们的心智中承载着他们的家谱、背景和民族的知识，然后这些被传给他们的孩子。诚然，阅读和写作相比这些过程有某些优势，但同样真实的是，以那种古老方式拥有的知识，以更个人化、更有意义的方式成为一个人和一个社会的一部分。当然，这是一种不同的认知方式。在最佳状态下，它不会导致对记忆材料的机械复述，而是通过音乐、诗歌、舞蹈对其进行戏剧性的演绎。换句话说，它的演绎伴随着创造性的身体表达。诚然，从实际角度讲，一个人的心智无法容纳你们世界中所有可用的信息——但其中许多信息并不涉及关于宇宙或人类在宇宙中位置的基本知识。它是一种次要信息——有趣，但并非赋予生命。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As you apprehend them through association you come quite close to examining the contents of your mind in a free fashion. But if you drop the time concept and then view the conscious content of your mind through other core ideas, you are still structuring. I am not saying that you should never organize those contents. I am saying that you must become aware of your own structures. Build them up or tear them down, but do not allow yourself to become blind to the furniture of your own mind.
quotes.translation: 当你通过联想来理解它们时，你几乎可以自由地检视你心智的内容。但如果你放下时间概念，再透过其他核心观念来审视你心智的意识内容，你仍然在进行结构化的过程。我不是说你永远不该组织那些内容。我是说你必须觉察到自己的结构。你可以建立它们，也可以拆解它们，但不要让自己对你心智中的家具视而不见。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The same kind of development can occur in almost any physical category. Usually more than one belief is involved. Parallel with the belief that vision will fail, you may have the before-mentioned belief that hearing will dim, and these two ideas may be reinforced by a belief that age automatically makes you less a person, turning you into an individual who can no longer relate in the daily pattern of environment. The belief, you see, would work to insure the materialization of that state. (Pause.) On the other hand you may believe that wisdom grows with age, that self-understanding brings a peace of mind not earlier known, that the keen mind is actually far better able to assess the environment, and that the physical senses are much more appreciative of all stimuli. And so those conditions will be physically met in your experience. The physical apparatus itself, following your beliefs, will continue in health.
quotes.translation: 类似的发展几乎可以在任何生理类别中发生。通常涉及的不止一个信念。与视力会衰退的信念并行，你可能还有前面提到的听力会变差的信念，而这两个想法可能被一个信念强化——即年龄会自动让你变得不那么完整，把你变成一个无法再与日常环境模式互动的人。你看，这个信念会确保那个状态的具体化。（停顿）另一方面，你可能相信智慧随年龄增长，自我理解带来前所未有的内心平静，敏锐的心智实际上更能评估环境，而肉体感官对所有刺激也更能欣赏。于是这些条件将在你的经验中具体实现。你的身体机制，遵循你的信念，将继续保持健康。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: To study the human body from only the physical standpoint, or to consider it as exclusively a physical phenomenon, is to severely limit your perception of it, and of reality as a whole. To study psychology exclusively in terms of the brain's effect upon the physical body is likewise hampering and limiting, for the brain is merely that portion of, that very small portion, of the mind which is apparent within matter. As such within your physical field the brain is subject to the laws of your field. The mind, having its existence within the scope of the physical field but independent of it, is a much more fruitful subject for study; and not of course study through means of physical instruments or of operations performed. In any case the mind cannot be found by such procedures.
quotes.translation: 仅从物理角度研究人体，或将其视为纯粹的物理现象，会严重限制你对它以及整个实相的看法。仅从大脑对肉体的影响来研究心理学，同样是有阻碍和局限的，因为大脑只是心智在物质中显现的那一部分——非常小的一部分。因此，在你的物理领域内，大脑受制于该领域的法则。而心智，其存在虽在物理领域范围内却独立于它，是一个更有成果的研究主题；当然，不能通过物理仪器或手术操作来研究。无论如何，心智无法通过这类程序找到。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You should desire good health because it is a natural state of your being. You should trust in the innate intelligence of your own being... which produces good health. Health is a natural state of your being. Through your physical image the energy of the universe expresses itself. You as an individualized consciousness are a part of this, and you cannot express yourself fully nor fulfill your purpose as an identity, as an individual, if you are not in good health... for the effects of the body are felt in the mind... and the effects of the mind are felt in the body. You distort the picture. Now I did not intend a question and answer service this evening, but I seem to be involved in one. And some evening I will turn the tables. And when I ask you questions, I will expectat some answers. And they will not be easy questions... and they will not be general questions. And I will make you search for the answers... and the answers will form steps upon which you can walk and will serve as foundations within which you can travel within yourselves. But they will be your answers and not my answers.
quotes.translation: 你应该渴望健康，因为它是你存在的自然状态。你应该信任你自身内在的固有智能……它产生健康。健康是你存在的自然状态。宇宙的能量通过你的物质形象表达自己。你作为一个个体化的意识是其中的一部分，如果你不健康，你就无法充分表达自己，也无法实现你作为身份、作为个体的目的……因为身体的影响会在心智中感受到……而心智的影响也会在身体中感受到。你扭曲了这幅画面。我今晚本不打算进行问答服务，但我似乎卷入其中了。某个晚上我会反过来。当我问你们问题时，我会期待一些答案。那些问题不会容易……也不会笼统。我会让你们去寻找答案……这些答案将形成你们可以行走的阶梯，并作为你们可以在自身之内旅行的基础。但它们将是你们的答案，而不是我的答案。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: "Man is therefore set against his nature in his own mind, and he thinks he must control it. The fact is that man's consciousness can indeed become aware of -- aware of -- those spontaneous processes. But he himself has largely closed the door of comprehension, so that he only identifies with what he thinks of as his rational mind, and tries to forget as best he can those spontaneous processes upon which the mind rides so triumphantly.
quotes.translation: 因此，人在自己的心智中与自己的本性对立，他认为必须控制它。事实上，人的意识确实可以觉察到——觉察到——那些自发的过程。但他自己很大程度上关闭了理解之门，以至于他只认同他认为是理性心智的部分，并尽量忘记那些心智如此得意洋洋地驾驭其上的自发过程。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: NOTES: SESSION 845
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: What happens to a thought when it leaves your conscious mind? It does not simply disappear. You can learn to follow it, but you are usually frightened of turning your attention away from its intense focus in three-dimensional existence. Therefore, it seems that the thought disappears. It seems also that your subjectivity has a mysterious unknown quality about it, and that even your mental life has a sort of insidious dropping-off point, a subjective cliff over which thoughts and memories fall, to disappear into nothingness. Therefore to protect yourself, to protect your subjectivity from drifting, you erect various psychological barriers at what you suppose to be the danger points. Instead, you see, you can follow these thoughts and emotions simply by realizing that your own reality continues in another direction, beside the one with which you mainly identify. For these thoughts and emotions that have left your conscious mind will lead you into other environments.
quotes.translation: 当一个想法离开你的意识心智时，它会怎样？它不会简单地消失。你可以学会追随它，但你通常害怕将注意力从它在三维存在中的强烈焦点上移开。因此，这个想法似乎消失了。你的主观性似乎也具有一种神秘未知的特质，甚至你的精神生活也有某种阴险的跌落点，一个主观的悬崖，思想和记忆从那里坠落，消失于虚无。因此，为了保护自己，保护你的主观性不漂移，你在你认为的危险点处建立了各种心理屏障。相反，你看，你可以通过认识到你的实相在另一个方向继续存在——除了你主要认同的那个方向之外——来追随这些思想和情感。因为这些离开你意识心智的思想和情感将引领你进入其他环境。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: Take another photograph of yourself at a different age than the first one you chose. Ask yourself simply: "Am I looking at the same person?" How familiar or how strange is this second photograph? How does it differ from the first one you picked this evening? What similarities are there that unite both photographs in your mind? What experiences did you have when each photograph was taken? What ways did you think of following in one picture that were not followed in the other one? Those directions were pursued. If they were not pursued by the self you recognize, then they were by a self that is probable in your terms. In your mind follow what directions that self would have taken, as you think of such events. If you find a line of development that you now wish you had pursued, but had not, then think deeply about the ways in which those activities could now fit into the framework of your officially accepted life.2 Such musings, with desire -- backed up by common sense -- can bring about intersection points in probabilities that cause a fresh realignment of the deep elements of the psyche. In such ways probable events can be attracted to your current living structure.
quotes.translation: 现在：再拿一张你不同年龄的照片，与你选的第一张对比。简单问自己："我看的是同一个人吗？"第二张照片有多熟悉或多陌生？它与你今晚选的第一张有何不同？在你的心智中，有哪些相似之处将两张照片联系起来？每张照片拍摄时你经历了什么？一张照片中你曾考虑走但未走的方向是什么？那些方向其实已被追寻。如果未被你认知的自我追寻，那么它们已被你所谓的可能自我追寻。在你的心智中，跟随那个自我本会采取的方向，如同你思考这类事件。如果你发现一条你希望当初追寻却未追寻的发展路线，那么深入思考这些活动现在如何能融入你官方接受的生活框架。这种带着欲望的沉思——辅以常识——能在可能性中创造交汇点，引发心灵深层元素的重新整合。如此，可能事件能被吸引到你当前的生活结构中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 695
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: The inner lands have not been as well explored. To say the least, they lie in virgin territory as far as your conscious mind is concerned. Others have journeyed to some of these interior locales, but since they were indeed explorers they had to learn as they went along. Some, returning, provided guidebooks or travel folders, telling us what could be expected. You make your own reality. If you were from a foreign land and asked one person to give you a description of New York City, you might take his or her description for reality. The person might say "New York City is a frightful place in which crime is rampant, gangs roam the streets, murders and rapes are the norm, and people are not only impolite but ready to attack you at a moment's notice. There are no trees. The air is polluted, and you can expect only violence." If you asked someone else, this individual might say instead: "New York City has the finest of museums, open-air concerts in some of the parks, fine sculpture, theater, and probably the greatest collection of books outside of the Vatican. It has a good overall climate, a great mixture of cultures. In it, millions of people go their way daily in freedom." Period. Both people would be speaking about the same locale. Their descriptions would vary because of their private beliefs, and would be colored by the individual focus from which each of them viewed that city.
quotes.translation: 内在领域尚未被充分探索。至少可以说，就你的意识心智而言，它们处于处女地。其他人曾游历过其中一些内在地点，但由于他们确实是探索者，必须边行边学。有些人回来后提供了指南或旅行手册，告诉我们能期待什么。你创造自己的实相。如果你来自异国，请一个人描述纽约市，你可能会把他的描述当作实相。那人可能说："纽约市是个可怕的地方，犯罪猖獗，帮派横行街头，谋杀和强奸是常态，人们不仅不礼貌，而且随时准备攻击你。没有树木。空气污染，你只能期待暴力。"如果你问另一个人，这个人可能反而说："纽约市有最好的博物馆、公园里的露天音乐会、精美的雕塑、戏剧，以及可能是梵蒂冈以外最大的藏书。气候总体良好，文化大融合。数百万人每天自由地各行其道。"句号。两人都在谈论同一个地点。他们的描述因个人信念而异，并被各自看待那座城市的个人焦点所着色。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Information like this is sifted through many layers of subconscious conception, and is subsequently colored. People believing in your organized religions color it in a manner that is highly disadvantageous, and that unfortunately adds to existing superstitions. Ruburt's mind, believe it or not, is much like my own -- though, if you'll forgive me, in a very limited fashion. Therefore the distortions are much less distortive, much less harmful, and more easily discovered and cleared ... Others less perfectionist than myself are content with more distortion. I am not. Ruburt's Idea Construction9 was rather amazing. The inner senses provided him with much, but nevertheless the ideas contained in it represented an achievement of the conscious mind. I was drawn by this to realize that he was ready for me.
quotes.translation: 这类信息经过潜意识概念的许多层次筛选，随后被着色。相信你们组织化宗教的人以一种极为不利的方式着色，不幸地增加了现有的迷信。信不信由你，鲁柏的心智与我的非常相似——尽管，请原谅我，以一种非常有限的方式。因此扭曲的扭曲程度小得多，危害小得多，更容易被发现和清除……其他不如我完美主义的人满足于更多扭曲。我不满足。鲁柏的《观念构建》相当惊人。内在感官为他提供了许多，但其中包含的观念代表了意识心智的成就。我被此吸引，意识到他已准备好接受我。

quotes.text: Many of my readers are familiar with private meditation, when concentration is focused in one particular area. There are many methods and schools of thought here, but a highly suggestive state of mind results, in which spiritual, mental, and physical goals are sought. It is impossible to meditate without a goal, for that intent is itself a purpose. Unfortunately, many of your public health programs, and commercial statements through the various media, provide you with mass meditations of a most deplorable kind. I refer to those in which the specific symptoms of various diseases are given, in which the individual is further told to examine the body with those symptoms in mind. I also refer to those statements that just as unfortunately specify diseases for which the individual may experience no symptoms of an observable kind, but is cautioned that these disastrous physical events may be happening despite his or her feelings of good health. Here the generalized fears fostered by religious, scientific, and cultural beliefs are often given as blueprints of diseases in which a person can find a specific focus -- the individual can say: "Of course, I feel listless, or panicky, or unsafe since I have such-and-such a disease."
quotes.translation: 我的许多读者熟悉私人冥想，即注意力集中在一个特定领域。这里有许多方法和思想流派，但会产生一种高度暗示性的心智状态，在其中寻求灵性、心智和身体的目标。没有目标就无法冥想，因为那个意图本身就是目的。不幸的是，你们的许多公共卫生项目和通过各种媒体的商业声明，提供了最可悲的大众冥想。我指的是那些给出各种疾病具体症状的声明，进一步告诉个体带着这些症状检查身体。我也指那些同样不幸的声明，指定个体可能没有可观察症状的疾病，但警告说尽管他或她感觉健康，这些灾难性的身体事件可能正在发生。在这里，由宗教、科学和文化信念培养的普遍恐惧常被作为疾病蓝图，个体可以找到具体焦点——个体可以说："当然，我感到无精打采、恐慌或不安全，因为我患有某某疾病。"

quotes.text: It is not enough to meditate, or to imagine in your mind some desired goal being accomplished, if you are afraid to act upon the very impulses to which your meditations and imaginings give rise. When you do not take any steps toward an ideal position, then your life does lack excitement. You become depressed. You might become an idealist in reverse, so that you find a certain excitement in contemplating the occurrence of natural disasters, such as earthquakes. (Pause.) You may begin to concentrate your attention on such activities. You may contemplate the end of the world instead, but in either case you are propelled by a sense of personal frustration, and perhaps by some degree of vengeance, seeing in your mind the destruction of a world that fell so far beneath your idealized expectations.
quotes.translation: 如果你害怕按照冥想和想象所引发的冲动行动，那么仅仅冥想或在心智中想象某个期望目标的实现是不够的。当你不采取任何步骤走向理想状态时，你的生活确实缺乏兴奋。你会变得沮丧。你可能成为反向的理想主义者，以至于在思考自然灾害（如地震）的发生中找到某种兴奋。（停顿。）你可能开始将注意力集中在这种活动上。你可能反而思考世界末日，但无论哪种情况，你都被个人挫败感驱动，或许还有某种程度的报复，在心智中看到一个远低于你理想化期望的世界的毁灭。
