source_raw.title: (Over two years ago, in Note 2 for Session 801, and in the opening notes for Session 805, I described our decision to add the writing room to the house. All four of us involved in the moving were quite amused, then -- for the friends who helped Jane and me carry the furniture yesterday are the father-and-son contracting team that built the room in the first place.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: This tale, I admit, is far more difficult to understand than a simple story of God's creation of the world, or its actual production in a meaningless universe through the slippery hands of chance--and yet my story is more magnificent because elements of its truth will find resonance in the minds and hearts of those open enough to listen. For men's minds themselves are alive with the desire to read properly, and they are aware of their own vast heritage. It is not simply that man has a soul that is somehow blessed while the rest of him is not, but that in those terms everything [he knows], regardless of size or degree, is made of "soul stuff." Each portion has its own identity and validity--and no portion is ever annihilated or destroyed. The form may change.
quotes.translation: 我承认，这个故事比上帝创造世界的简单故事，或世界在无意义宇宙中通过偶然的滑手实际产生要难理解得多——然而我的故事更宏伟，因为其真理的元素会在足够开放倾听的心智和心灵中找到共鸣。因为人类的心智本身充满正确解读的渴望，他们意识到自己庞大的遗产。并非简单地说人有一个以某种方式被祝福的灵魂而其余部分则没有，而是以那些术语来说，他所知道的一切，无论大小或程度，都由"灵魂材料"构成。每个部分都有其自身的身份和有效性——没有任何部分被消灭或摧毁。形式可能改变。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The reasoning mind, as you have used it thus far, roughly (underlined) since the birth of Christianity, has used--instead of used, confined--has confined its reasoning abilities to a very narrow spectrum of reality. It has seen the value of life largely only as that life conforms to its own standards. (Pause.) That is, the reasoning mind, as you have used it, considers that only reasoning creatures are capable of understanding life's values. Other forms of life have almost seemed beside the point, their value considered only insofar as they were of service to man. But man's life is obviously dependent upon the existence of life's other species, and with him those species share certain values. Life is sacred--all life--and again, all life seeks value fulfillment, not simply physical survival.
quotes.translation: 你们迄今所使用的理性心智，大致上（划线强调）自基督教诞生以来，非但未能善用其推理能力，反而将其局限在极其狭隘的现实光谱中。它几乎只将生命价值视为符合自身标准的存在。（停顿）也就是说，你们所运用的理性心智，认为唯有具备推理能力的生物才能理解生命价值。其他生命形式几乎被视为无关紧要，其价值仅在于对人类的服务。但人类的生命显然依赖于其他物种的存在，并与它们共享某些价值。生命是神圣的——所有生命皆是——再次强调，所有生命都在寻求价值实现，而不仅仅是肉体存续。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They are the dreams that warn of famines or of wars. Such dreams, however, can also be triggered often, as in your own times, when the conscious mind is convinced that the survival of the species is threatened--and in such cases the dreams then actually represent man's fears. Overanxiety, then, can confuse the genetic system, and in a variety of ways. The existence of each of the species is dependent upon trust, indeed a biological optimism, in which each species feels the freedom to develop the potentials of its members in relative safety, within the natural frameworks of existence. Each species comes into being not merely feeling a natural built-in trust in its own validity, but is literally propelled by exuberance in its ability to cope with its environment. It knows that it is uniquely suited to its place within life's framework. The young of all species exhibit an unquenchable rambunctiousness. That rambunctiousness is built in.
quotes.translation: 它们是预告饥荒或战争的梦境。然而在你们这个时代，当意识心确信物种存续受到威胁时，这类梦境也常被触发——此时这些梦境实则代表人类的恐惧。过度焦虑会以多种方式扰乱基因系统。每个物种的存续都依赖于信任，确切说是生物性乐观，使每个物种在相对安全的自然存在框架内，自由发展其成员的潜能。每个物种诞生时不仅对自身有效性怀有与生俱来的信任，更被应对环境的丰沛能力所驱动。它深知自己独特地适应生命框架中的位置。所有物种的幼崽都展现出不可遏制的活力，这种活力是与生俱来的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.

quotes.text: The mind is always with you. The particular brain is the physical mechanism that translates the thoughts of the mind. It goes without saying then, that the brain belongs to the physical system, and here in clear terms you see the smooth cooperation that exists. Thoughts are initially psych electric patterns in pure form, productions of the inner self that must be translated in order to be used by the physical self.
quotes.translation: 心智始终与你同在。特定大脑是将心智思想转译的物理机制。不言而喻，大脑属于物理系统，在此你们能清晰看到两者间的顺畅协作。思想最初是纯粹形式的心理电模式，是内在自我的产物，必须经过转译才能被物质自我运用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Another thing. It is in some ways convenient that you are not consciously aware of each breath that you take. But it is sheer stupidity to ignore the inner self which does the breathing, and is aware of the mechanics involved. What you almost get here is that some little unknown self performs these necessary functions, and that is not the case. I have said that the mind is a part of the inner world, but you have access to your own minds which you ignore, and this access would lead you inevitably to the truths about the physical world. Working inward you could understand the outward so much more clearly.
quotes.translation: 另一点。你们未能有意识地觉察每次呼吸，这在某些方面是便利的。但忽视那个进行呼吸、知晓其中机制的内在自我，则是十足的愚昧。你们几乎要认为某个无名小我执行着这些必要功能，实则不然。我曾说过心智是内在世界的一部分，但你们能够触及却被忽视的自身心智，这种触及必将引领你们发现物质世界的真相。向内探索，你们将更清晰地理解外在世界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I know you are rather concerned with the differentiation between planets and planes. I also know your mind is filled with ponderous questions, like an endless chasm filled to the brim with heavy rocks. Certainly you didn't expect those huge radio stars to be cute little RCA transmitters? They are not quite that. I am in a quandary, and I admit it, as to which matters you would prefer I discussed first. I gather all sorts of weird projectiles thrown at me with vibrant force. Dreams, yours and Ruburt's, your flying saucers, some of your visions, planes, planets and radio stars. You'd think surely that my first name was Encyclopedia. Just call me Psych for short, and spell it P-s-y-c-h.
quotes.translation: 我知道你们相当关注行星与层面的区别。我也知道你们的心智充满沉重疑问，如同无底深渊填满巨石。你们难道真以为那些巨大的射电星是可爱的RCA发射器？它们并非如此。我陷入两难，承认这点，不知你们希望我先讨论哪些议题。我接收到各种奇异投射物以鲜活力量向我袭来：梦境、你和鲁柏的梦、你们的飞碟、某些幻象、层面、行星与射电星。你们准以为我的名字叫百科全书。就简称我为Psych，拼写为P-s-y-c-h。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I risk repeating myself, but I want these steps to be plain. This vital data is sent to the mind by the inner senses. Any material that is important for the body's contact with outer camouflage patterns is given to the brain. The subconscious, so-called, is a connective between mind and brain, between the inner senses and the outer senses. It is actually partly on your plane and partly on other planes. Portions of it do deal with camouflage patterns, with the personal past of the present personality, with racial camouflage memories; and the greater portion belongs to the inner world, and as data comes into it from the inner world, so can it reach far into the inner world itself.
quotes.translation: 我甘冒重复之险，但希望这些步骤清晰明了。内在感官将关键数据传送至心智。任何对肉体接触外在伪装模式重要的资料都交给大脑。所谓潜意识是心智与大脑、内在感官与外在感官之间的连接体。它实际上部分存在于你们的层面，部分存在于其他层面。其部分确实处理伪装模式、当前人格的个人过往、种族伪装记忆；而更大部分属于内在世界，当数据从内在世界进入时，它便能深入内在世界本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: All of this material applies to your lives in general and to Ruburt's physical condition, because you must be clear in your minds as to your own status in that regard, and much of this material will clear the air and dissolve lingering doubts; doubts that cause both of you -- but Ruburt in particular -- to hold on to the rational approach in a misguided effort to maintain what he thinks of as a balanced viewpoint and open mind. It seems, because of the definitions you have been taught, that there is only one narrow kind of rationality, and that if you forsake the boundary of that narrow definition, then you become irrational, fanatic, mad, or whatever (all very emphatically).
quotes.translation: 所有这些资料都普遍适用于你们的生活，也适用于鲁柏的身体状况，因为你们必须清楚自己在这一点上的立场，而许多资料将澄清气氛、消除挥之不去的疑虑——那些导致你们两人（尤其是鲁柏）错误地坚持理性方法、试图维持他所谓的平衡观点和开放心态的疑虑。由于你们所受的教育定义，似乎只有一种狭隘的理性，一旦你放弃那狭隘定义的边界，就会变得非理性、狂热、疯狂或其他什么（非常强调地说）。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: The fact that he is now thinking of walking after dinner is an obvious advance. His irritability is somewhat natural -- but also based on the idea, still, that when he is laying down that is dead time (with amusement), or useless time, enforced inactivity. It would help, of course, if he reminded himself that his creative mind is at work whether or not he is aware of it, and regardless of what he is doing, and that such periods have the potential, at least, of accelerating creativity, if he allows his intellect to go into a kind of free drive at such times. You might have him become more aware of when he actually becomes tired, or uncomfortable, so that he does lay down then.
quotes.translation: 他现在考虑饭后散步的事实，是一个明显的进步。他的烦躁有些自然——但也基于这样的观念：当他躺下时，那就是死时间（带着戏谑），或是无用时间，被迫的静止。当然，如果他提醒自己，无论他是否意识到，他的创造性心智都在运作，无论他在做什么，而且如果他在那些时刻允许自己的智力进入一种自由驱动状态，这样的时期至少具有加速创造力的潜力，那会有所帮助。你可以让他更清楚地意识到自己何时真正感到疲倦或不适，这样他那时就会躺下。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: It certainly seems that the best way to get specific answers is to ask specific questions, and the rational mind thinks first of all of something like a list of questions. In that regard, Ruburt's response before such a session is natural, and to an extent magical, because he knows that no matter what he has been taught, he must to some degree (underlined) forget the questions and the mood that accompanies them with one level of his consciousness, in order to create the proper kind of atmosphere at another level of consciousness -- an atmosphere that allows the answers to come even though they may be presented in a different way than that expected by the rational mind.
quotes.translation: 当然，获得具体答案的最佳方式似乎是提出具体问题，而理性心智首先想到的是一系列问题。在这方面，鲁柏在这样一节课前的反应是自然的，并且在某种程度上是神奇的，因为他知道，无论他受过怎样的教育，他必须在某个程度上（加下划线）忘记那些问题以及伴随它们的心境，用他意识的一个层面，以便在另一个意识层面创造适当的气氛——一种允许答案到来的气氛，即使这些答案可能以与理性心智预期不同的方式呈现。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: My remark has nothing to do with your accepted concepts of the unconscious portions of the self. Your ideas of the unconscious are so linked to your limited ideas of personhood as to be meaningless in this discussion. It is as if you used only one finger of one hand, and then said: "This is the proper expression of my personhood." It is not just that there are other functions of the mind, unused, but that in those terms you have other minds. You have one brain, it is true, but you allow it to use only one station, or to identify itself with only one mind of many.
quotes.translation: 我的评论与你们对自我无意识部分的接受概念无关。你们对无意识的观念与你们对人格的有限观念如此紧密相连，以至于在这次讨论中毫无意义。就好像你们只用一只手的一根手指，然后说：“这是我人格的恰当表达。”不仅仅是心智的其他功能未被使用，而是按照那些术语，你们拥有其他心智。你们确实有一个大脑，但你们只允许它使用一个频道，或只让它认同于众多心智中的一个。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Probabilities can be juggled, tried out without physical consequences. The mind follows its natural bents. It has far more energy than you allow it to use, and it releases this in great "fantasies" -- fantasies from which you will choose facts that you will experience. At the same time dreaming is an art of the highest nature, in which all are proficient. There are structured dreams as there are structured games in waking life. There are mass dreams "attended by many." There are themes, both mass and private, that serve as a basis or framework. Yet overall, the mind's spontaneous activity continues because it enjoys its own activities.
quotes.translation: 概率可以被操纵、尝试，而无需物理后果。心智遵循其自然倾向。它拥有的能量远超你们允许它使用的，它将这些能量释放到巨大的“幻想”中——你们将从这些幻想中选择你们将体验的事实。同时，做梦是一种最高性质的艺术，所有人都精通于此。有结构化的梦，就像清醒生活中有结构化的游戏。有“许多人参加”的集体梦。有主题，既有集体也有私人的，作为基础或框架。然而，总体而言，心智的自发活动持续进行，因为它享受自己的活动。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The core belief, because of its intensity and because of your habits, will often tend to attract to itself others of a like nature. They will hang on. If you are not accustomed to examining your own mind, then you can allow separate growths of this kind to form about a belief until you cannot distinguish one from the other. This can develop to such an extent that all of your experience is seen only in relationship to this idea-growth. (Seth called for the hyphen.) Data that seems unrelated to this core belief is then not assimilated but thrown into the corners of your mind, unused, and you are denied the value of the information.
quotes.translation: 核心信念，由于它的强度以及你们的习惯，往往会吸引其他类似性质的信念。它们会附着不去。如果你们不习惯审视自己的心智，那么你们可能会让这种分离的增长围绕一个信念形成，直到你们无法区分彼此。这可以发展到这样的程度：你们的所有经验都只在与这个观念增长的关系中被看待。（赛斯要求加连字符。）与这个核心信念看似无关的数据，则不被吸收，而是被扔进你们心智的角落，未被使用，你们被剥夺了这些信息的价值。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The dream world and the mind are touched by time, and exist in it only in so far as these realities dip into the camouflage universe. Basically both the dream world and the ideas of the inner mind do not have their existence in time, although they may be visible from the perspective of time, viewed from the physical form.
quotes.translation: 梦境世界与心智虽受时间触及，却仅在这些实相潜入伪装宇宙时才存在于时间之中。本质上，梦境世界与内在心智的理念并不存在于时间之内，尽管从时间视角、透过物质形体观之，它们或许可见。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: As I also mentioned (in the 614th session in Chapter Two), the conscious mind is not basically cut off from the inner self or from those deep inner sources of knowledge available to it. The aware mind is not any one event, for that matter; it represents various portions of the inner self that "surface" at any given time.
quotes.translation: 正如我先前（第二章第614节）所提，意识心基本上并未与内在自体或那些可触及的深层内在知识源头隔绝。就此而言，觉知的心并非单一事件；它代表内在自体在不同时刻"浮现"的各个部分。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In the first place, as often mentioned lately, the reasoning mind is spontaneously fired. The species contains within itself all of the necessary spontaneous attributes that are necessary to form a civilization, for example. (Pause.) All of your reasoned activities -- your governments, societies, arts, religions and sciences -- are the physical realization, of course, of inner capacities, capacities that are inherent in man's structure. Take your theaters' moving picture dramas. These are the materialization in your time of man's natural acting ability -- a characteristic highly important in the behavior of the species.
quotes.translation: 首先，如近期常提的，推理心是自发激发的。物种自身便包含所有形成文明所必需的自发属性，例如。（停顿）你们所有理性活动——政府、社会、艺术、宗教与科学——当然都是内在能力的物质实现，这些能力是人类结构固有的。以你们的剧院动态影像戏剧为例，这是人类天生表演能力在你们时间中的物质化——此能力在物种行为中极为重要。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: There are also what I will call body dreams. No consciousness, to whatever degree, is fully manifested in matter. There is always constant communication between all portions of the body, but when the conscious mind is diverted that activity often increases. Cellular consciousness at its own level then forms a body dream. These do not involve pictures or words, but are rather like the formations of electromagnetic intent, anticipating action to be taken, and these may then serve as initiators of therapeutic dreams, in which "higher" levels of consciousness are psychologically made aware of certain conditions.
quotes.translation: 此外还有我称之为身体梦的现象。无论何种程度的意识，都无法完全显化于物质中。身体各部分之间始终存在持续沟通，但当意识心被转移时，这种活动往往增强。细胞意识在其自身层面便形成身体梦。这些不涉及图像或文字，而更像是电磁意图的成形，预判即将采取的行动，继而可能成为疗愈梦的启动者——在此过程中，"更高"层次的意识会在心理上觉察到某些状况。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Later, Jane suggested that I ask Seth to comment upon the ideas in a session. There would also be a number of other reasons for double dreams, of course. I briefly wrote about them in Volume I of "Unknown" Reality; see the notes for Session 692.)

quotes.text: In a manner of speaking, the conscious mind is a window through which you look outward -- and looking outward, perceive the fruits of your inner mind. Often you let false beliefs blur that great vision. Your joy, vitality and accomplishment do not come from the outside to you as the result of events that "happen to you." They spring from inner events that are the result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 打个比方，意识心是一扇窗，你透过它向外看——而向外看时，你感知到内在心智的果实。你常让虚假信念模糊那伟大的视野。你的喜悦、活力与成就并非来自外界"发生在你身上"的事件，而是源自你信念所引发的内在事件。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Once more, it is extremely important that Ruburt keep his mind on his goals, and not burden his conscious mind by trying to figure out circumstances and conditions that are best handled by the infinite intelligence that is within his own subconscious mind. The way and the means will be taken care of. They will indeed appear almost effortlessly -- but he must let the burden of worry go.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，鲁柏必须将心智专注在目标上，切勿试图盘算那些最好交由他潜意识中无限智慧处理的情况与条件，以免加重意识心的负担。方法与途径自会得到照料。它们确实会几乎不费力地显现——但他必须放下忧虑的重担。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Jane went to hydro as usual this morning, but wasn't too happy about the system. While she was eating a good lunch I thought of telling her the typewriter repair service had called this morning, citing a bill for $90.00 for the repair and a box of a dozen cartridges, but I forgot to mention it as we talked about other things. I'd also called our optometrist and asked his secretary to have him return my call so I could ask him a couple of technical questions about a note I'm writing for Session 901 for Dreams. Since I had to wait for his call -- which never came -- I couldn't leave the house to get Jane's typewriter. I want to try it out to make sure it's okay before I turn my own in for repair, which it needs.

quotes.text: Using your conscious mind as a threshold, however, you can discover still more. Figuratively speaking, stand where you are. Think of that moment of conscious awareness as a path. Imagine many other such paths, all converging; again, imaginatively take one of them in your mind and follow it. Accept what you experience uncritically. To some small extent you are "altering" your consciousness. (Half humorously:) Of course, you are not "altering" it at all. You are simply using it in a different fashion, and focusing it -- however briefly -- in another direction. This is the simplest of exercises.
quotes.translation: 然而，运用你的意识心智作为门槛，你可以发现更多。打个比方说，站在你所在之处。将那个意识觉知的时刻视为一条路径。想象许多其他这样的路径，全部汇聚在一起；再次，在想象中选取其中一条，用你的心智跟随它。不加评判地接受你所体验的一切。在某种程度上，你是在“改变”你的意识。（半开玩笑地说：）当然，你根本没有“改变”它。你只是以不同的方式使用它，并将它聚焦——无论多么短暂——于另一个方向。这是最简单的练习。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 701
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Much information does not even reach the brain (the mind is aware of such data, however). In man, the psychic-physical structure has at every moment a complete up-to-date picture of pertinent information about all events that will in any way affect the organism. All actions are taken with this information available. In the dream state such data becomes transformed, again, into pseudophysical pictures -- reflections of events that might occur, previews of probable sequences. These are flashed before a consciousness that momentarily focuses upon the inner rather than the outer arena of reality.
quotes.translation: 许多信息甚至没有到达大脑（然而，心智是知道这些数据的）。在人类中，灵性-物理结构每时每刻都拥有关于所有将以任何方式影响有机体的事件的相关信息的完整最新图景。所有行动都是在这些信息可用的情况下采取的。在梦境状态中，这些数据再次转化为伪物理画面——可能发生的事件的反映，可能序列的预览。这些画面闪现在一个暂时聚焦于内在而非外在实相舞台的意识之前。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The intensity is the core about which the electromagnetic energy units form. In your terms, the more intense the core, the sooner the physical materialization. This would apply whether the mental image was a fearful one or a joyful one. Now there is a very important problem here: If your turn of mind is highly intense and you think in vivid mental emotional images, these will be swiftly formed into physical events. If you are also of a highly pessimistic nature, given to thoughts and feelings of potential disaster, then these thoughts will be quite faithfully reproduced in experience.
quotes.translation: 强度是电磁能量单位围绕其形成的核心。用你们的话说，核心越强烈，物理物质化就越快。无论心智图像是恐惧的还是喜悦的，这一点都适用。这里有一个非常重要的问题：如果你的心智倾向高度强烈，并且你以生动的心理情感图像思考，这些将迅速形成物理事件。如果你也高度悲观，倾向于潜在灾难的想法和感受，那么这些想法将相当忠实地在经验中再现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Physical play is pleasant, and accompanied by high imaginative activity. Muscles and mind are both exercised. The same kind of activity occurs in the child's dream state as it learns to handle events before they are physically encountered. Intense dream activity is involved. Some dream events are more real to the child than some waking events are -- not because the child does not understand the nature of experience, but because he or she is still so close to the emotional basis behind events. Some of the exercises I will suggest will put you in touch with the way events are formed.
quotes.translation: 身体游戏是愉快的，并伴随着高度的想象活动。肌肉和心智都得到锻炼。同样的活动发生在儿童的梦境状态中，因为他们学习在物理上遭遇事件之前处理它们。涉及强烈的梦境活动。对儿童来说，有些梦境事件比某些清醒事件更真实——不是因为儿童不理解经验的本质，而是因为他或她仍然如此接近事件背后的情感基础。我将建议的一些练习将使你接触到事件形成的方式。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Because of the brain's necessary specifications, large portions of your own greater reality cannot appear through its auspices. The brain might consider such extracurricular activity as background noise or clutter that it could not decipher. It is the mind, then, as the brain's nonphysical counterpart, that decides what data will activate the brain in that regard. The so-called ancient portions of the brain (among them the brainstem -- limbic system) contain "the mind's memories." Generally speaking, this means important data to which, however, no conscious attention need be given.
quotes.translation: 由于大脑的必要规格，你更大实相的很大一部分无法通过它的中介显现。大脑可能将这种课外活动视为它无法解读的背景噪音或杂乱信息。因此，是心智——作为大脑的非物理对应物——决定哪些数据将在那方面激活大脑。大脑所谓的古老部分（其中包括脑干-边缘系统）包含“心智的记忆”。一般来说，这意味着重要的数据，然而，这些数据不需要有意识的关注。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Later, Jane suggested that I ask Seth to comment upon the ideas in a session. There would also be a number of other reasons for double dreams, of course. I briefly wrote about them in Volume I of "Unknown" Reality; see the notes for Session 692.)

quotes.text: Your conscious mind is meant to give your body an assessment of what I will call cultural conditions, for there are sophistications and specifications that in your terms consciousness alone can assess. If, under conditions naturally safe in the terms of primary experience, you become overwhelmed by unsafe signals from secondary experience -- that is, from your reading or whatever -- you show a lack of discrimination. You are not able to differentiate between the physically safe present situation, and the imagined, which is perhaps unsafe, calling forth the alarms of danger.
quotes.translation: 你的意识心智旨在为你的身体提供我称之为文化条件的评估，因为存在只有意识（用你们的话说）才能评估的复杂性和规格。如果在主要经验中自然安全的条件下，你被来自次要经验（即来自你的阅读或其他）的不安全信号所淹没，你就表现出缺乏辨别力。你无法区分物理上安全的当下情境和想象的（可能不安全）情境，从而引发危险的警报。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: One symbol will be meaningful to many portions of the personality. The symbol will be the same. That is, any given symbol will be the same, but it will be so chosen by the mind that it will have definite meanings to various portions of the self, and the meanings may be quite different. In the breaking-down process the intensities are separated into the most minute values, each value an electrical impulse representing any one of many references. And all of these references are contained within a given impulse, electrical impulse, that will be decoded by the mind.
quotes.translation: 一个象征对人格的许多部分都具有意义。这个象征本身是相同的。也就是说，任何给定的象征都是相同的，但心灵会选择它，使其对自我的各个部分具有明确的意义，而这些意义可能大相径庭。在分解过程中，强度被分离成最细微的值，每个值都是一个电脉冲，代表众多参照中的任何一个。所有这些参照都包含在一个给定的脉冲——电脉冲中，而心灵将解码这个脉冲。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Catastrophies, such as earthquakes or floods, are not perpetuated by certain elements of nature against other portions of itself. Your feelings have as much natural validity as the tides, and they have their own kind of attraction -- mind does move matter. A ring manipulated at a seance under controlled conditions is but the most simple kind of demonstration of the great ability of mind to interact with matter. You each participate in the creation of each thunderstorm, each new spring, each flood, earthquake, and summer rain.
quotes.translation: 灾难，如地震或洪水，并非由自然的某些元素对其自身其他部分所造成。你的情感与潮汐一样具有自然的有效性，它们有自己的吸引力——心灵确实能移动物质。在受控条件下，降神会上被操控的戒指，只是心灵与物质互动伟大能力的最简单演示。你们每个人都参与了每一场雷雨、每一个新春、每一场洪水、地震和夏雨的创造。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "Thus far, however, the old habits have returned, and for all of your joint good intents the idea of bringing things to a crisis point is still far less beneficial than it might appear. This does not mean such a method cannot work at times. It does mean that on the whole it is a difficult method, and in utmost honesty and clarity in that regard, I can only tell you what I have said before: Regardless of how ill-founded it may seem on certain occasions, basically speaking the situation becomes less as you pare it down in your mind, rid it of significance in your mind, say things like: 'Well, after all it is not that bad yet,' or in other ways turn your attention otherwise.
quotes.translation: “然而，到目前为止，旧习惯又回来了，尽管你们共同怀有良好的意图，但将事情推向危机点的想法，其益处远不如表面看起来那么大。这并不意味着这种方法有时不起作用。它确实意味着总体而言这是一种困难的方法，并且在此方面，以最大的诚实和清晰度，我只能告诉你们我之前说过的话：无论在某些场合看起来多么没有根据，基本上，当你在心中削减它、在心中剥离其重要性、说些像‘好吧，毕竟还没那么糟’之类的话，或以其他方式转移你的注意力时，情况就会变得不那么严重。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 3. In Chapter 9 of Dreams, see the opening notes for Session 920 at superscript number 5.

quotes.text: In the dreaming state the characteristics of the reasoning mind become altered, and from a waking viewpoint it might seem distorted in its activity. What actually happens, however, is that in the dreaming state you are presented with certain kinds of immediate knowledge. It often appears out of context in usual terms. It is not organized according to the frameworks understood by the reasoning portions of your mind, and so to some extent in dreams you encounter large amounts of information that you cannot categorize.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，理性心灵的特征会发生改变，从清醒的视角来看，其活动可能显得扭曲。然而，实际发生的是，在梦境状态中，你被呈现某种直接的知识。它通常以常规术语来看显得不合上下文。它并非根据你心灵理性部分所理解的框架来组织，因此在某种程度上，你在梦中会遇到大量你无法分类的信息。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: We have mentioned reincarnation hardly at all (but see the 631st session in Chapter Seven), yet here let me state that the theory is a conscious-mind interpretation in linear terms. On the one hand it is highly distorted. On the other hand it is a creative interpretation, as the conscious mind plays with reality as it understands it. But in the terms used there is no karma to be paid off as punishment unless you believe that there are crimes for which you must pay (as indicated in the 614th session in Chapter Two).
quotes.translation: 我们几乎从未提及转世（但见第七章第631节），然而在此让我说明，这个理论是意识心灵以线性术语进行的诠释。一方面，它高度扭曲。另一方面，它是一种创造性的诠释，因为意识心灵在它理解的方式下与实相嬉戏。但按照所使用的术语，没有业力需要作为惩罚来偿还，除非你相信有些罪行你必须为此付出代价（如第二章第614节所示）。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your conscious mind is always trying to give you a clear picture, but you often allow preconceived ideas to block out this intelligence. It has been fashionable to blame the subconscious for personality problems and difficulties, the idea being that early events, charged and mysterious, lodged there. In this country several generations grew up believing that the subconscious portions of the personality were unreliable, filled with negative energy, and contained only locked-up unpleasant episodes best forgotten.
quotes.translation: 你的意识心灵总是试图给你清晰的画面，但你常常让先入为主的观念阻挡这种智慧。将人格问题和困难归咎于潜意识曾是一种时尚，认为早期事件充满能量且神秘，被安置在那里。在这个国家，有几代人成长起来，相信人格的潜意识部分不可靠，充满负面能量，只包含最好被遗忘的、锁闭的不愉快事件。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Such states lead to a condition of mental, psychic and physical health and efficiency. The aware mind's great leeway through the intellect, and its connection with the senses, makes it possible for any singly insignificant event to trigger such experience. Intense focus is a characteristic of the conscious mind, and you can call it narrow because it includes only the physical dimension; but within the scope of that corporeal field it has great freedom to interpret the given dimension in any way it chooses.
quotes.translation: 这种状态会导向心智、心灵与身体的健康及效能。觉察心智能通过智力获得极大的自由空间，并与感官相连，这使得任何看似微不足道的事件都可能触发此类体验。强烈专注是意识心智的特性，你可以称其为狭隘，因为它只包含物质维度；但在那具身领域的范围内，它拥有极大的自由，能以任何它选择的方式诠释该维度。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The person might feel indisposed, but in such ways the body assimilates and uses properties that would otherwise be called alien ones. It immunizes itself through such methods. The body, however, exists with the mind to contend with -- and the mind produces an inner environment of concepts. The cells that compose the body do not try to make sense of the cultural world. They rely upon your interpretation, therefore, for the existence of threats of a nonbiological nature. So they depend upon your assessment.
quotes.translation: 此人或许会感到不适，但身体正是通过这种方式吸收并运用那些本可称为异质的属性。它通过此类方法进行自我免疫。然而，身体必须与心智共存——而心智则产生一个概念的内在环境。构成身体的细胞并不试图理解文化世界。因此，它们依赖你的诠释来认知非生物性威胁的存在。所以它们仰赖你的评估。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 804, MAY 9, 1977
9:44 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The mind is also equipped to see its own beliefs, reflect upon them and evaluate their results, so using this tool as it was meant to be used would automatically help man in recognizing both his beliefs and their effects. Part of this great permissiveness has to do with the fact that man is to realize that he creates his own reality. Free will is a necessity. The leeway given allows him to materialize his ideas, meet them in physical experience, and evaluate for himself their particular kind of validity.
quotes.translation: 心智同样具备审视自身信念、反思并评估其结果的能力，因此按此工具的本意来运用它，自然会帮助人类认清其信念及其影响。这种极大宽容的部分原因在于，人类必须意识到自己创造了自己的实相。自由意志是必要的。所给予的自由空间使他能够将理念物质化，在物质体验中与之相遇，并自行评估其特定有效性。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your own kind of conscious mind is splendid and unique. It causes you, however, to interpret all other kinds of life according to your own specifications and experience. The complex nature of other animal consciousness escapes you completely. And when you compare your technologies, learning, logical thought, cultures and arts with what you understand of animal experience, there seems no doubt that you are superior and "the Flower of Evolution" -- that all other kinds of life are topped by your existence.
quotes.translation: 你们这种意识心智是卓越而独特的。然而，它却导致你按照自己的规格与经验去诠释所有其他生命形态。你完全无法理解其他动物意识的复杂本质。当你将自身的技术、学识、逻辑思维、文化与艺术与你所理解的动物体验相比较时，似乎毫无疑问你更优越，是"进化的花朵"——所有其他生命形态都因你的存在而相形见绌。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I do not define bad. When I use the term it is according to your own definition. You have an idea that good is gentle and bad is violent. This is because in your mind violence and destruction are the same thing. By this analogy, you see, the soft voice is the holy voice and the loud voice is the wicked one, and a strong desire is the bad desire and a weak desire the good one. You become afraid of projecting ideas or desires outward, for in the back of your mind you think that what is powerful is evil.
quotes.translation: 我不定义"恶"。当我使用这个术语时，是依据你们自己的定义。你们认为善是温和的，恶是暴烈的。这是因为在你们心中，暴力与破坏是同一回事。依此类推，你看，轻柔的声音是神圣的声音，响亮的声音是邪恶的声音，强烈的欲望是坏的欲望，而微弱的欲望是好的欲望。你变得害怕将理念或欲望向外投射，因为在你脑海深处，你认为强大的东西就是邪恶的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those that do are chosen with great discrimination, dreams serving as one of the methods by which you ascertain the desirability of any given probable act. There is basically no difference at these other levels of existence between waking and dream events. Creatively, then, you organize your experience in such a fashion, with the conscious mind as you think of it also carrying its own responsibility. Those events that you do not accept as physical ones, however, also exist and join their own organizations. They do not simply fall away from your experience, but serve as focus points for events that do not concern you directly, while indirectly they form a definite psychological background. To a certain extent they become the invisible medium of experience from which your own specialized activities emerge, so that their nature is implied in your own life -- and so that your life is implied in those other frameworks.
quotes.translation: 那些被采纳的意念是经过高度辨别的选择，梦境作为方法之一，让你能确定任何特定可能行动的合意性。在这些其他存在层面上，清醒事件与梦境事件之间基本上没有区别。因此，在创造层面上，你以这种方式组织你的体验，而你所认为的意识心智也承担着自身的责任。然而，那些你不接受为物质事件的事件，同样存在并形成它们自己的组织。它们并非简单地从你的体验中消失，而是作为与你无直接关联的事件的焦点，同时间接地构成一个明确的心理背景。在某种程度上，它们成为你特定活动从中浮现的不可见体验媒介，因此它们的本质隐含在你的生命之中——而你的生命也隐含在那些其他框架之中。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now as you will see, all creatures, regardless of their degree, can and do choose, within their spheres of reality, those sensations that they will experience--but to one extent or another (underlined) all sensations are felt. We will later discuss the part of the mind and its interpretation, for example, of painful stimuli, but I want to make the point that those attracted to physical life are first and foremost tasters of sensation. Outside of that, basically, there are all kinds of mental distinctions made [among] stimuli. The body is made to react. It is made to feel life and vitality by reacting to an environment that is not itself, by encountering what you might call natural stress. The body maintains its equilibrium by reacting against gravity, by coming in contact with other bodies, by changing its own sensations, by glorifying in the balance between balance and off-balance.
quotes.translation: 现在你会明白，所有生物，无论其进化程度如何，都能且确实会在其现实领域中选择自己将要体验的感受——但所有感受都会在某种程度上（划线强调）被感知。我们稍后会讨论心智对痛苦刺激等信息的解读作用，但我想强调：那些被物质生命吸引者，首先是感受的品尝者。除此之外，刺激物之间本质上存在各种心智层面的区分。身体被设计成会做出反应，它通过接触非自身的环境、通过遭遇你所谓的自然压力来感受生命与活力。身体通过对抗重力、接触其他物体、改变自身感受、在平衡与失衡的平衡中欢庆来维持平衡。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Events are processed in dreams, put in the necessary perspective, sorted and arranged. This is done when the conscious mind is separated from direct involvement with physical events. Dreams serve to dull the impact of the day's events just past, while the meaning of those activities sifts through the various levels of the personality, settling into compartments of intent and belief. Often the true impact of an event does not occur until it has been interpreted or reexperienced through a dream.
quotes.translation: 事件在梦境中被处理，被置于必要的视角中，被分类整理。当意识心智与物质事件的直接参与分离时，这个过程便得以完成。梦境的作用是缓冲刚过去一天的事件冲击，同时让那些活动的意义渗透过人格的各个层面，沉淀到意图与信念的隔间中。通常，一个事件的真正影响要等到通过梦境被解读或重新体验后才会显现。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your mind's eye however imagine a run-down, shabby, deteriorating shamble of a house with rotting floorboards and sagging porches. Then imagine that it is burned to the ground and the remaining rubble carted away and burned. Imagine the land now free beneath it, open to the blessing of wind, rain and sun. Then imagine a new house being built there, of your preferred choice, with all new materials, of splendid design, and see this always in your mind where before you saw the previous image.
quotes.translation: 然而，在你心灵之眼中，想象一栋破败、肮脏、摇摇欲坠的房屋，地板腐朽，门廊塌陷。然后想象它被烧成灰烬，残骸被运走焚烧。想象那片土地重获自由，敞开接受风、雨和阳光的祝福。然后想象在那里建起一栋新房子，按你心仪的选择，用全新材料，设计精美，并在你心中始终看到这个画面，取代之前看到的旧影像。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Some of those abilities show themselves in those classified as mentally deficient simply because all of the powers of the reasoning mind are not activated. In children under such conditions, the reasoning mind has not yet developed in all of its aspects sufficiently, so that in a certain area direct cognition shines through with its brilliant capacity.
quotes.translation: 其中一些能力会显现在那些被归类为心智缺陷者身上，仅仅是因为推理心智的所有能力未被激活。在这样的儿童身上，推理心智尚未充分发展其所有面向，因此在某个领域，直接认知以其璀璨的能力穿透而出。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You used up an unwarranted amount of energy at your mother's. Symbolically you did not like to put on the storm windows, feeling that perhaps it would be the last time that you did so, and that you were sealing up the house. The symbolism in your mind was connected with your visit. You did not want to be reminded particularly of your fathers condition, and subconsciously you transposed the image of a casket upon the house, so that in sealing up the windows you were sealing up a casket.
quotes.translation: 你在母亲家消耗了过多不必要的能量。象征性地，你并不喜欢安装防风窗，感觉这可能是你最后一次这样做，仿佛你在封存那栋房子。你心中的象征意义与你的探访有关。你特别不想被提醒父亲的状况，于是潜意识里将棺材的意象投射到房子上，以至于当你封窗时，你是在封存一口棺材。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The meaning of the light will normally become unfolded as he is ready to fully perceive it. While the event has happened, therefore, like any event it is not completed. In the drug experience mentioned before (in the last session), startling, enforced symbols and occurrences are suddenly thrust upon the conscious mind; and more, within a context in which time as it knows it has little meaning. It [the conscious mind] cannot reflect upon phenomena subjectively. They happen too quickly.
quotes.translation: 光的含义通常会在人准备好完全感知时逐渐展开。因此，尽管事件已经发生，但如同任何事件一样，它并未完成。在之前（上次课）提到的药物体验中，令人震惊的、强制的符号和事件突然被抛向意识心智；更甚的是，这些发生在其所认知的时间几乎没有意义的背景中。它（意识心智）无法主观地反思这些现象。它们发生得太快了。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The conscious mind is basically curious, open. It is also equipped to examine its own contents. Because of the psychological theories of the last century, many Western people believed that the primary purpose of the conscious mind was to inhibit "unconscious" material.
quotes.translation: 意识心基本上好奇而开放，它也具备检视自身内容的能力。由于上个世纪心理学理论的影响，许多西方人相信意识心的主要功能是抑制"无意识"材料。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Latently, your consciousness is capable of performing these feats, but the work cannot be done with the part of your consciousness that is strongly attached to the space-time relationship. What you think of as your conscious mind is given the task of assessing the "facts" of daily living. It then forms beliefs about reality, and these are used in the dream state as one of the main yardsticks, so to speak, that activate the emergence of certain probable events rather than others.
quotes.translation: 你的意识潜在地能够完成这些壮举，但这项工作不能由你意识中强烈依附于时空关系的那部分来完成。你认为的意识心被赋予评估日常生活的"事实"的任务，然后形成关于实相信念，这些信念在梦境状态中被用作主要衡量标准之一——可以说，它们激活了某些可能事件而非其他事件的显现。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You are silly sometimes, Joseph. For shame. From the information you have been given before, and from the above paragraph, you should know that you are in contact with your own entities, in that the mind is a portion of the entity to begin with. I have told you that communication between a present personality and the entity is carried on by means of the inner senses, and I have told you that in these sessions you use the inner senses. Surely then the question answers itself.
quotes.translation: 约瑟，你有时真傻。真不害臊。根据之前给你的信息以及上述段落，你应该明白你正在与自己的存有接触，因为心本来就是存有的一部分。我曾告诉你，当前人格与存有之间的沟通是通过内在感官进行的，我也告诉过你，在这些课中你使用了内在感官。那么这个问题自然就回答了自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You did not want to project, per se. You wanted to be here to offer reassurance. The desire got you here. Your conscious mind was taken up fully with your activities, giving the inner self full rein. In the same way do you travel in other realities without being aware of your journeys. You were not frightened, either of you, because of the familiar surroundings, and you were unusually free to express your feelings for each other, released from restraints of time.
quotes.translation: 你根本不想投射。你想待在这里提供安慰。这个愿望让你来到这里。你的意识心完全专注于你的活动，让内在自我自由驰骋。同样地，你在其他实相中旅行时也未曾察觉自己的旅程。你们俩都没有害怕，因为熟悉的环境让你们异常自由地表达对彼此的情感，摆脱了时间的束缚。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: It may be another version of yourself. We will discuss ways of distinguishing between these. A man may suddenly disappear, and to be replaced by a small girl, a nonsensical development to the logical mind. Yet the girl could be the form of the man's previous or future reincarnated self. The unity, you see, is different. Basically perception of the spacious present is naturally available. It is your nervous and physical mechanism which acts as a limiting device, and also as a focusing device. By limiting many otherwise available perceptions it forces you to focus upon what you can perceive with greater intensity.
quotes.translation: 那可能是你自己的另一个版本。我们将讨论区分它们的方法。一个人可能突然消失，取而代之的是一个小女孩——这对逻辑心来说是荒谬的发展。然而这个女孩可能是这个男人前世或来世转世自我的形态。你看，统一性是不同的。基本上，对广袤当下的感知是自然可得的。是你的神经和生理机制充当了限制装置，同时也充当了聚焦装置。通过限制许多原本可得的感知，它迫使你以更强的强度聚焦于你能感知到的事物。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Keep in mind here, though a discussion will wait for quite a while, that a like relationship occurs between the dream universe and the world of negative matter. There is surrounding this threefold system an envelope, so to speak, of uncamouflaged psychic phenomena. In other words, psychic energy gestalts, of which we will speak much later. They are important however; and from what you now know you realize that these energy forms represent mental enclosure units, operating in the same manner as our earlier ones of which we spoke when discussing the early development of energy within the physical field.
quotes.translation: 请记住——尽管讨论还要等很久——梦境宇宙与负物质世界之间也存在类似的关系。可以说，这个三重系统周围包裹着一层未经伪装的灵性现象外膜。换言之，那是灵性能量完形，我们将在很久以后讨论它们。但它们很重要；根据你现在的了解，你会意识到这些能量形式代表心智包覆单元，其运作方式与我们之前讨论物理场中能量早期发展时提到的那些单元相同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In those normal, generally accepted terms, the images in photographs do not change, move, or alter their relationships. The living subjective photography of dreams, however, provides a framework in which these "images" have their own mobility. They represent creativity in far different terms than you usually understand. You know what physical issue is (intently), because you see the children of your loins, but you do not experience the children of your dreams in the same physical way, nor understand that your dream life is continuous. It has organization on its own levels that you do not comprehend, and from its rich source you draw much of the energy with which you form your daily experience. Your conscious mind is the director of that experience.
quotes.translation: 在那些正常且普遍接受的说法里，照片中的影像不会改变、移动或改变彼此的关系。然而，梦的活生生主观摄影术却提供了一个框架，让这些"影像"拥有自身的流动性。它们所代表的创造力，与你通常理解的方式截然不同。你清楚知道物质实相是什么（专注地），因为你看到了自己亲生的孩子，但你却无法以同样的物质方式体验梦中的孩子，也不明白你的梦境生活是持续的。它在自身的层面上有着你不理解的组织，而从这个丰富的源头中，你汲取了形成日常体验所需的大量能量。你的意识心正是那体验的导演。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 699
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: It is obvious to most others that such paranoid views are not based on mass fact. (Pause.) Your President at that time, however, had at his command vast information, so that he was aware of many groups and organizations that did not agree with his policies. He used those as in other circumstances a paranoid might use the sight of a police car to convince himself that he was being pursued by the police, or the FBI or whatever. The President felt threatened -- and not only personally threatened, for he felt that the good for which he stood in his own mind was in peril (intently). And again, since the idealized good seemed too remote and difficult to achieve, any means was justified. Those who followed him, in the Cabinet and so forth, possessed the same kinds of characteristics to some degree or another.
quotes.translation: 对大多数人而言，这种偏执的观点显然并非基于大众事实。（停顿）然而，你当时的那位总统掌握着大量信息，因此他清楚许多不赞同他政策的团体和组织。他利用这些，就像在其他情况下，一个偏执狂可能利用看到警车来说服自己正被警察、联邦调查局或其他机构追捕一样。总统感到受威胁——不仅是个人受威胁，因为他觉得自己所代表的美好事物正岌岌可危（专注地）。而且，既然理想化的美好似乎太过遥远且难以实现，任何手段都变得合理。那些追随他的人，在内阁中等等，也都或多或少具备同样的特质。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 856, MAY 24, 1979
8:23 P.M. THURSDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: All of this reminds me of my old idea of a projected book: The Contents of the Mind, or the Unofficial Contents of the Mind. After reading this first page of notes about Peg G. and the paintings, I remember my old ideas ... that we organize the contents of our minds along certain lines that then become habitual.
quotes.translation: 这一切让我想起自己曾计划写的一本书：《心灵的内容》，或《心灵的非官方内容》。在读完关于佩格·G和画作的第一页笔记后，我记起了自己旧有的想法……我们按照某些后来成为习惯的路线来组织心灵的内容。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: You have always been then in contact with me, but you were only able to see a portion of me. (Pause.) Keep in mind that all names are arbitrary, and we use them merely for your convenience, as we use words. (Still speaking carefully) Basically, Seth's name or mine isn't important. Individuality is important, and continues in ways you barely suspect. (Long pause; voice light, not like Seth.) In one way, in the most important way, and in the only basic way, I am Seth, dispensing with certain (pause) characteristics which are mine (leaning forward) which I used to contact you.
quotes.translation: 你一直与我保持联系，但你只能看到我的一部分。（停顿）请记住，所有名称都是随意的，我们使用它们只是为了方便你，就像我们使用词语一样。（仍谨慎地说）基本上，赛斯的名字或我的名字并不重要。个体性才是重要的，并且以你几乎无法察觉的方式持续存在。（长停顿；声音轻快，不像赛斯。）从某方面来说，在最重要的方面，也是唯一根本的方面，我就是赛斯，舍弃了某些（停顿）属于我的特质（前倾），那些我曾用来与你联系的特质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Again, the writer or the artist also brings more into his work than the simple ability to write or paint. In one way or another all of his experience is involved. When you pay attention to Framework 1 primarily, it is as if you have learned to write simple sentences with one word neatly before the other. You have not really learned true expression. In your life you are writing sentences like "See Tommy run." Your mind is not really dealing with concepts but with the simple perception of objects, so that little imagination is involved. You can express the location of objects in space, and you can communicate to others in a similar fashion, confirming the physical, obvious properties that others also perceive.
quotes.translation: 再说一次，作家或艺术家带入作品中的，也不仅仅是写作或绘画的简单能力。无论以何种方式，他所有的经验都参与其中。当你主要关注框架一时，就好像你学会了写简单的句子，一个词整齐地排在另一个词前面。你并没有真正学会真实的表达。在你的生活中，你写的句子就像"看汤米跑"。你的心灵并非真正在处理概念，而是在处理对物体的简单感知，因此很少涉及想象力。你可以表达物体在空间中的位置，并以类似的方式与他人交流，确认他人也能感知到的物理、明显的属性。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 819, which was held last Saturday evening, had nothing to do with Mass Events. In our opinion tonight's session did, however, and for a number of reasons that will become obvious as it's read. Yet Seth didn't call it dictation.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You are not at the mercy of your emotions, either, for they are meant to follow the flow of your reasoning. Your mind is meant to perceive the physical environment clearly, and its judgments about the environment then activate the body's mechanisms to bring about proper response. If your beliefs about existence are fearful, then the emotional reactions will be those leading to stress. Your own value judgments need examination in such a case.
quotes.translation: 你也不受情绪的摆布，因为情绪本应跟随你推理的流动。你的心灵本应清晰地感知物质环境，然后对环境做出的判断会激活身体的机制，以产生适当的反应。如果你对存在的信念充满恐惧，那么情绪反应就会导致压力。在这种情况下，你需要审视自己的价值判断。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (12:02.) In a very limited and fumbling manner this is hinted at through the use of computers, where you try to assess "future probabilities" and act accordingly in your present. The mind can do this far better than any computer. If it believed this, then certain portions of the brain would be activated. The brain would become aware of more of the mind's knowledge, and the probabilities of future events would be made consciously available.
quotes.translation: （12:02分。）通过使用电脑，你们试图评估"未来可能性"并在当下采取相应行动——这种极其有限且笨拙的方式隐约暗示了这一点。心智比任何电脑都更擅长此事。若它相信这一点，大脑的某些部分就会被激活。大脑将能意识到更多心智的知识，未来事件的概率也会被有意识地获取。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now. We will try to have a session for you (WL), our friend William the Bull, shortly... and clear up some of your difficulties... for some of them are because you are bull-headed. You will not let the inner self speak clearly enough to you... so we will give it the voice that you can hear... And, you will know when we have the session, you will intuitively realize that what we say is true... And there is no need to fight what you are fighting... And the secret that you try so hard to hide from your conscious mind is not that terrible a secret. There is some past life that operated in your case that has caused a block, and we hope that we can help you remove it. Bulls can rip down fences.
quotes.translation: 现在。我们很快会为你（WL），我们的朋友"公牛"威廉安排一场课……并澄清你的一些困难……因为其中部分源于你的固执。你不让内在自我足够清晰地对你说话……所以我们会赋予它你能听见的声音……当你参加这场课时，你会直觉地意识到我们所说的是真实的……无需与你正在对抗的事物对抗……你极力向意识心智隐藏的秘密，其实并没有那么可怕。你个案中运作的某个前世造成了障碍，我们希望帮你移除它。公牛也能撞倒围栏。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The "unconscious" simply contains great portions of your own experience in which you have been taught not to believe. Again, your conscious mind is meant to look into the exterior world and into the interior one. The conscious mind is a vehicle for the expression of the soul in corporeal terms.
quotes.translation: "无意识"仅仅包含你被教导不应相信的、大量自身经验。再次强调，你的意识心智本应同时审视外在世界与内在世界。意识心智是灵魂以肉身形式表达的工具。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Creativity connects waking and dreaming reality, and is in itself a threshold in which the waking and dreaming selves merge to form constructs that belong equally to each reality. You cannot begin to understand how you form the physical events of your lives unless you understand the connections between creativity, dreams, play, and those events that form your waking hours. In one respect dreams are a kind of structured unconscious play. Your mind dreams in joyful pleasure at using itself, freed from the concerns of practical living. Dreams are the mind's free play. The spontaneous activity, however, is at the same time training in the art of forming practical events.
quotes.translation: 创造力连接清醒与梦境现实，它本身就是一个阈限，在此清醒与做梦的自我融合成同时属于两种现实的构造。除非你理解创造力、梦境、游戏与构成清醒时光的事件之间的关联，否则你无法开始理解自己如何形成生命中的物质事件。从某方面说，梦境是一种结构化的无意识游戏。你的心智在运用自身时充满喜悦的欢愉，摆脱了现实生活的顾虑。梦境是心智的自由游戏。然而，这种自发活动同时也是形成实际事件的艺术训练。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: No apple tree tries to grow violets. Quite automatically it knows what it is, and the framework of its own identity and existence. (Pause.) You have a conscious mind, but this is only the "topmost" portion of your mind. Much more of "it" is available to you. Much more of your knowledge can be conscious, therefore; but a false belief, a limiting one, is as ambiguous to your nature as any apple tree's idea that it was a violet plant.
quotes.translation: 没有苹果树试图生长紫罗兰。它完全自动知晓自己的本质，以及自身身份与存在的框架。（停顿。）你拥有意识心智，但这只是你心智的"最表层"部分。更多"它"的部分可供你取用。因此，你更多的知识可以成为有意识的；但一个虚假信念、限制性信念，对你的本性而言，就像任何苹果树认为自己是紫罗兰那样荒谬。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I have said that the brain is the mind in camouflage, and so it is. It is the portion of the mind that is more or less observable to the surgeon, and the part of the mind that reacts to camouflage pattern, and the part that can be explored and tampered with, though this is dangerous indeed.
quotes.translation: 我曾说过大脑是伪装中的心智，事实正是如此。它是心智中外科医生或多或少可观察到的部分，是心智中对伪装模式做出反应的部分，也是可以被探索和篡改的部分——尽管这确实危险。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Part of the subconscious for example deals with camouflage, but the deeper portions are in direct contact with the basic vitality of the universe. When you or Ruburt wonder if this material comes from the subconscious, you often take it for granted that the subconscious is personal, exclusively dealing with matters of your egos' personal past. You are sometimes willing to concede that perhaps an element of racial memory might enter in. Racial memory is of course a conglomeration of camouflage data. The subconscious also contains the undistorted material of the mind, which is un-camouflaged, and which operates between planes, knowing no boundaries.
quotes.translation: 潜意识的一部分处理伪装，但更深层的部分直接与宇宙的基本生命力相连。当你或鲁柏怀疑这些资料是否来自潜意识时，你们常理所当然地认为潜意识是个人的，只处理与你们自我个人过去相关的事务。你们有时愿意承认或许有种族记忆的元素介入。种族记忆当然是伪装资料的集合体。潜意识也包含心智未扭曲的资料，这些资料未经伪装，在层面间运作，不受界限限制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You may feel that you want to "relive" certain episodes of your life so that you can understand them better. Your life's experience, therefore, is your own. Such conditions certainly are not alien. In ordinary living, you often imagine yourself behaving in a different manner than you did, or in your mind reexperiencing events in order to gain greater understanding from them. Your life is your own personal experience- perspective, and when at death you take it out of the mass physical time context, then you can experience it in many ways. Events and objects are not absolute, remember, but plastic. Events can be changed both before and after their occurrence. They are never stable or permanent, even though within the context of three-dimensional reality they may appear so.
quotes.translation: 你或许觉得想"重历"生命中的某些片段，以便更好地理解它们。因此，你的人生经历是你自己的。这样的状况当然并不陌生。在日常生活中，你常想象自己以不同于实际的方式行事，或在心中重新体验事件，以从中获得更深的理解。你的人生是你个人的经验视角，当死亡时你将其从集体物理时间背景中取出，便能以多种方式体验它。记住，事件和物体并非绝对，而是可塑的。事件在发生前后都可以被改变。它们从不稳定或永恒，即使在三维实相的背景下看似如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There were countless other events that happened to you on different afternoons at the same hour, both before and after that one. The cells within your hand contain within themselves memories your conscious mind would be dazzled to behold. Yet remember that the cells in your twenty-seven-year-old hand are in no physical way the same cells that experienced any of those events. In some underground of sensation, however, the buried evidences of stimuli and reaction experienced during those numberless "past" afternoons still exist. Some of those memories will certainly be played back, to affect what you think of as your current experience at twenty-seven. Your conscious thoughts and habits regulate which of them will intermix into the maelstrom of the present.
quotes.translation: 在那个下午之前和之后，同一时刻还有无数其他事件发生在你身上。你手中的细胞包含着连你有意识心智都会惊叹的记忆。然而，记住你二十七岁手中的细胞在物理上已不再是经历那些事件的同一批细胞。但在某种感觉的地下深处，那些无数"过去"下午所经历的刺激与反应的埋藏证据依然存在。其中一些记忆必定会回放，影响你视为二十七岁当前体验的内容。你的有意识思想和习惯调节着哪些记忆会融入当下的漩涡。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You must look within yourself for evidences of what you want in terms of positive experience. Examine your past with that in mind. Imagine your future from the power point of the present. In such a way at least you are not using the past to reinforce your limitations, or projecting them into the future. It is only natural to contrast what you want with what you have, and it is very easy to become discouraged in so doing, but looking for errors in the past will not help you. A correctly utilized five-minute period of time can be of great benefit, however. In this period concentrate upon the fact that the point of power is now. Feel and dwell upon the certainty that your emotional, spiritual and psychic abilities are focused through the flesh, and for five minutes only direct all of your attention toward what you want. Use visualization or verbal thought -- whatever comes most naturally to you; but for that period do not concentrate upon any lacks, just upon your desire.
quotes.translation: 你必须向内寻找积极体验的证据。带着这个目的审视你的过去。从当下的力量点想象你的未来。这样至少你不会用过去强化你的局限，或将它们投射到未来。将你想要的与已有的对比是自然的，这样做时很容易气馁，但寻找过去的错误无济于事。然而，正确利用五分钟的时间段可以带来巨大益处。在这段时间里，专注于力量点就在当下这一事实。感受并沉浸于你的情感、灵性和心灵能力通过肉体聚焦的确定性，仅用五分钟将所有注意力导向你想要的。使用视觉化或言语思维——任何你最自然的方式；但在这段时间内，不要专注于任何匮乏，只专注于你的渴望。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

related_concepts.name_en: The point of power is in the present

quotes.text: Because you have in the past convinced yourselves that the conscious mind must of necessity be cut off from inner reality, you think that it must be alienated from the dream state. Following such beliefs, you find yourselves thinking of dreaming as chaotic, unreasonable, and as completely divorced from normal conscious direction, purpose, or function. It often seems that sleep is almost a small death, and psychologists have compared dreaming with controlled insanity.5 You have so divorced your waking and dreaming experience that it seems you have separate "lives," and that there is little connection between your waking and dreaming hours. The rich tapestry of probable actions from which you choose your official life becomes just as invisible. This is quite needless.
quotes.translation: 因为你们过去说服自己有意识心智必须与内在实相隔绝，你们认为它必须与梦境状态疏离。遵循这样的信念，你们发现自己将做梦视为混乱、不合理、完全脱离正常有意识方向、目的或功能。睡眠常被看作一种小死亡，心理学家将做梦比作可控的疯狂。你们如此将清醒与做梦体验分离，以至于似乎拥有分离的"生命"，清醒与做梦时刻之间几乎没有联系。你们从中选择官方实相的可能行动之丰富织锦也变得不可见。这完全没必要。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 698
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: A remembered dream is a product of several things, but often it is your conscious interpretation of events that initially may have been quite different from your memory of them. To that extent the dream that you remember is a snapshot of a larger event, taken by your conscious mind. There are many kinds or varieties of dreams, some more and some less faithful to your memories of them -- but as you remember a dream you automatically snatch certain portions of subjective events away from others, and try to "frame" these in space and time in ways that will make sense to your usual orientation. Even then, however, dream events are so multidimensional that this attempt is often a failure. It might be easier here, perhaps, if you compare a scene from a dream with a scene in a photograph. A photograph will show certain events natural to the time in which it was taken. It will not show, for example, a picture of a Turk at the time of the Crusades. A dream scene might portray just such a motif, however.
quotes.translation: 一个被记住的梦是多种事物的产物，但通常是你对事件的意识解读，而这些事件最初可能与你记忆中的大相径庭。就此而言，你记住的梦是更大事件的一张快照，由你有意识心智拍摄。梦有多种类型，有些更忠实于你的记忆，有些则不然——但当你记住一个梦时，你自动从主观事件中抓取某些部分，并试图以符合你通常取向的方式在时空里"框定"它们。即便如此，梦的事件仍是如此多维，以至于这种尝试常常失败。或许，如果你将梦中的场景与照片中的场景相比较，会更容易理解。一张照片会显示拍摄时自然发生的某些事件。例如，它不会显示十字军东征时期土耳其人的画面。然而，一个梦的场景却可能描绘这样的主题。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 699
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: What is required is more than a shedding of clothes, however. To plunge into this ocean you also leave the physical body at the shore. It will be there when you get back. Your camouflage patterns can be likened to those cast by sun and shadow upon the ever-moving waves. As long as you keep the pattern in mind, you create it, and it is there. If you turn your head away for a moment and then look quickly back you can see only the wave. Your camouflage and your world are created by conscious focusing and unconscious concentration. Only by turning your head aside momentarily can you see what is beneath the seemingly solid pattern. By plunging into our ocean of value climate you can dive beneath your camouflage system and look up to see it, relatively foundationless, floating above you, moved, formed, and directed by the shifting illusions caused by the wind of will, and the force of subconscious concentration and demand.
quotes.translation: 然而，所需的远不止是脱去衣物。要跃入这片海洋，你还得将肉体留在岸边——当你归来时它仍在原处。你的伪装模式可比作阳光与阴影投射在永不停歇的波浪上的图案。只要你心中存有这图案，你便创造它，它便存在。若你稍一转头又迅速回望，便只能看见波浪。你的伪装与你的世界，是由有意识的聚焦与无意识的专注共同创造的。唯有暂时侧首，你才能看见那看似坚固的图案之下隐藏之物。跃入我们价值气候的海洋，你便能潜入你的伪装系统之下，仰头望去，它相对无根地漂浮在你上方，被意志之风、潜意识专注与需求之力所驱动的变幻幻象所移动、塑造与引导。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (12:21.) These characters become portions of the inner literature of the mind. Suppose an inhabitant from another reality saw [one of those three programs] and realized that people were watching it. Pretend he wanted to add more depth to the show. He might then come on himself in the guise of [the hero detective], but enlarging upon the characterization, adding more dimension to the plot. So, often when some personality from another station wants to help change the programming, he comes on in the form of a personality already known in fact or fiction. However, you must realize that that personality is larger than fact or fiction. "It" is independent at its own level, yet it is also a part of the portion of the private and mass psyche that is so represented.
quotes.translation: （12:21）这些角色成为心灵内在文学的一部分。假设来自另一实相的一位居民看到了[那三个节目之一]，并意识到人们正在观看它。假设他想为这个节目增添更多深度。那么他可能会以[那位英雄侦探]的伪装亲自登场，但扩展角色的刻画，为剧情增加更多维度。因此，当来自另一站台的某个人格想要帮助改变节目内容时，他常常以已知的事实或虚构人物的形式出现。然而，你必须认识到，那个人格超越了事实与虚构。"它"在其自身层面是独立的，但它同时也是以这种方式被代表的私人与大众心灵的一部分。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: When you awaken with a dream photograph in mind, it may appear meaningless because it does not seem to correlate with the official order of activities you recognize. You may make one particular decision in physical and waking consciousness, and that decision may bring forth certain events. Using your dream camera, you can with practice discover the history of your own psyche, and find the many probable decisions experienced in dreams. These served as a basis from which you made your physical decision. There is some finesse required as you learn to interpret the individual pictures within your dream album. This should be easy to grasp, for if you tried to understand physical life having only a group of snapshots taken at different places and in different times, then it would be rather difficult to form a clear idea of the nature of the physical world.
quotes.translation: 当你带着一张梦中的照片醒来时，它可能显得毫无意义，因为它似乎与你所认知的官方活动秩序无关。你可能会在物质与清醒意识中做出一个特定的决定，而这个决定可能会引发某些事件。运用你的梦境相机，通过练习，你可以发现你自己心灵的历史，并找到在梦中经历过的许多可能决定。这些决定构成了你做出物质决定的基础。当你学习解读你梦境相册中的个别照片时，需要一些技巧。这应该很容易掌握，因为如果你试图仅凭一组在不同地点、不同时间拍摄的快照来理解物质生活，那么要形成关于物质世界本质的清晰概念将会相当困难。

quotes.text: Your thinking mind, as you consider it, is the top of your mountain. In certain terms you can see "more" than your cells can, though they are also conscious of their realities. Were it not for their lives you would not be at the top of your psychological mountain. Even the trees at the highest tip of the hillside send sturdy roots into the ground, and receive from it nourishment and vitality -- and there is a great give-and-take between the smallest sapling in the foothills and the most ancient pine. No single blade of grass dies but that it affects the entire mountain. The energy within the grass sinks into the earth, and in your terms is again reborn. Trees, rocks, and grass constantly exchange places as energy changes form (very forcefully, leaning forward, eyes wide and dark).
quotes.translation: 你所认为的思考之心，是你这座山的顶峰。从某种角度来说，你比你的细胞能"看到"更多，尽管它们也意识到自己的实相。若非它们的存在，你便无法站在你心理之山的顶峰。即便是山坡最高处的树木，也将强壮的根系扎入大地，从中汲取滋养与活力——而在山麓最小的树苗与最古老的松树之间，存在着巨大的给予与索取。没有一片草叶的死亡不会影响整座山。草叶内的能量沉入大地，用你们的话说，它再次重生。树木、岩石与草地随着能量的形式变化而不断交换位置（非常有力地，身体前倾，眼睛睁大而深邃）。

quotes.text: He picks, or she picks, victims as intuitively as the victim seeks out the slayer. On the other hand, Mary's experiences in life may make her change her mind, so to speak, so that at 17 she encounters a severe illness instead, from which she victoriously recovers. Or she might narrowly miss being murdered when a bullet from the killer's gun hits the person next to her. On an entirely different level and in a different way, she might have no such experiences but be a writer of murder mysteries, or a nurse in surgery. The particular variations that one person might play are endless. You cannot consciously begin to alter the framework of your life, however, unless you realize first of all that you form it. The melody is your own. It is not inevitable, nor is it the only tune that you can play.
quotes.translation: 他或她选择受害者，其直觉性正如受害者寻找凶手一样。另一方面，可以说，玛丽生活中的经历可能会让她改变主意，以至于她在17岁时反而遭遇了一场重病，并从中胜利康复。或者，她可能侥幸躲过谋杀，因为凶手的子弹击中了站在她旁边的人。在完全不同的层面和方式上，她可能根本没有这样的经历，而是成为一位谋杀悬疑小说作家，或是一名手术室护士。一个人可能扮演的特定变体是无穷无尽的。然而，除非你首先意识到是你自己塑造了你生命的框架，否则你无法有意识地开始改变它。旋律是你自己的。它并非不可避免，也不是你唯一能演奏的曲调。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Consciousness forms symbols. It is not the other way around. Symbols are great exuberant playthings. You can build with them as you can with children's blocks. You can learn from them, as once you piled alphabet blocks together in a stack at school. Symbols are as natural to your minds as trees are to the earth. There is a difference, however, between a story told to children about forests, and a real child in a real woods. Both the story and the woods are "real." But in your terms the child entering the real woods becomes involved in its life cycle, treads upon leaves that fell yesterday, rests beneath trees far older than his or her memory, and looks up at night to see a moon that will soon disappear. Looking at an illustration of the woods may give a child some excellent imaginative experiences, but they will be of a different kind, and the child knows the difference.
quotes.translation: 意识形成符号。而不是反过来。符号是极好的、充满活力的玩具。你可以像用儿童积木一样用它们来搭建。你可以从它们身上学习，就像你曾经在学校把字母积木堆成一摞一样。符号对你的心智来说，就像树木对大地一样自然。然而，讲给孩子们听的关于森林的故事，与一个真实的孩子置身于真实的树林中，两者之间是有区别的。故事和树林都是"真实的"。但用你们的话说，进入真实树林的孩子会参与到它的生命周期中，踩在昨天落下的树叶上，在比他或她的记忆还要古老的树下休息，并在夜晚仰望那即将消失的月亮。观看一幅树林的插图可能会给孩子带来一些极好的想象体验，但那是另一种体验，而孩子知道其中的区别。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now give us a moment ... As creatures dwelling in time and space, your senses provide you with highly specific data, and with a cohesive-enough physical reality. Each person may react to the seasons in a very personalized manner, and yet you all share those natural events. They provide a framework for experience. It is up to the conscious mind to interpret sense events as clearly and concisely as possible. This allows for the necessary freedom of action for psychological and physical mobility. You are an imaginative species, and so the physical world is colored, charged, by your own imaginative projections, and powered by the great sweep of the emotions. But when you are confused or upset, it is an excellent idea to return your attention to the natural world as it appears at any given moment -- to sense its effect upon you as separate from your own projections.
quotes.translation: 现在请给我们一点时间……作为时空中的生物，你们的感官提供高度具体的数据，以及足够连贯的物理实相。每个人可能以非常个人化的方式回应季节，但你们共享这些自然事件。它们为经验提供了框架。有意识的心智必须尽可能清晰简洁地解读感官事件。这为心理与生理的灵活性提供了必要的行动自由。你们是富有想象力的物种，因此物质世界被你们自己的想象投射所着色、充电，并被情感的宏大浪潮所驱动。但当你们困惑或不安时，将注意力回归到当下此刻的自然世界——感受它对你产生的影响，将其与你自己的投射区分开来——是一个绝佳的主意。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: hen he originally gave us the 812th session some 16 months ago, Seth told us it would "be part of a chapter later in the book...." [See my opening notes for Session 814.] The 812th session was held following an encounter Jane and I had with an unexpected visitor, although these excerpts aren't personal at all. They fit in well with material in Mass Events, however -- indeed, as soon as Seth confirmed Jane's headings for Part 3 and Chapter 6, we knew that we'd found the place to present this material. We made our decision without consulting Seth, by the way.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Your body is the physical mirror of your psychological state (quietly intent). It is powered by the energy of the universe. It actually springs into being in each moment. Your mind and your body come from the same source, from universal energy. You are powered with vitality. You must seek meaning in your lives. When you lose the sense of life's meaning, for whatever reason, this is reflected in your body. (Pause.) It is very difficult to separate all of this from the many connotations placed about disease, and I do not want the material to be misread (still intently). Cancer, for example, has become the symbol for the body's vulnerability, in current years -- the proof of man's susceptibility to the body. It is a disease that people have when they want to die -- when they are ashamed to admit that they want to die, because death seems to fly against sane behavior. If the species struggles to survive, then how can individuals want to die?
quotes.translation: 你的身体是你心理状态的物理镜像（安静而专注）。它由宇宙的能量驱动。实际上，它在每一刻都瞬间生成。你的心智和身体来自同一个源头，来自宇宙能量。你被生命力所驱动。你必须在生命中寻求意义。当你因任何原因失去生命意义感时，这会在你的身体中反映出来。（停顿。）很难将这一切与围绕疾病的各种含义区分开来，我不希望这些资料被误解（仍然专注地）。例如，癌症在当今已成为身体脆弱性的象征——证明人类易受身体影响。这是一种人们在想要死亡时患上的疾病——当他们羞于承认自己想要死亡时，因为死亡似乎违背了理智的行为。如果物种挣扎求生，那么个体又怎能想要死亡呢？
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (According to him, tonight's session after 9:52 isn't book material either, but Jane and I are presenting it here because in it Seth returns to questions I'd asked earlier in Mass Events: What about the roles played in human affairs by viruses like smallpox?
