quotes.text: For some time, in your terms, the sleepwalkers remained more or less at that level of activity, and for many centuries they used the surface of the earth as a kind of background for other activity.Their real life was what you would now call the dreaming one. They worked mentally while asleep, constructing in their individual minds and in their joint mental endeavors (long pause) all of the dazzling images that would later become a mental reservoir from which men could draw. In that multidimensional array, consciousness mentally learned to form itself into EE units, atoms and molecules, electrons and chromosomes. It mentally formed the patterns through which all physical life could flow. The world then came into physical existence. Those units of consciousness are indestructible and vitalized, regardless of the forms they take, and while men's forms were dream images, consciousness spun forms into physical material.
quotes.translation: 在你们的时间概念中，梦游者在一段时间内或多或少保持在这种活动水平，许多世纪以来，他们将地球表面用作其他活动的背景。他们真实的生活是你们现在所谓的梦境生活。他们在睡眠中精神工作，在个体心智和共同的精神努力中构建（长停顿）所有那些令人眼花缭乱的图像，这些图像后来成为人类可以汲取的精神宝库。在那个多维阵列中，意识在精神上学会了将自己形成为EE单位、原子和分子、电子和染色体。它在精神上形成了所有物理生命可以流经的模式。世界随后进入物理存在。无论采取何种形式，那些意识单位都是不可毁灭且充满活力的，而当人类的形式是梦境图像时，意识将形式编织成物理物质。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The body learned to maintain its stability, its strength and agility, to achieve a state of balance in complementary response to the weather and elements, to dream computations that the conscious mind alone could not hold. The body learned to heal itself in sleep in its dreams--and at certain levels in that state even now each portion of consciousness contributes to the health and stability of all other portions. Far from the claw-and-dagger universe, you have one whose very foundation is based upon the loving cooperation of all of its parts. That is given--the gift of life brings along with it the actualization of that cooperation, for the body's parts exist as a unit because of inner relationships of a cooperative nature; and those exist at your birth (most emphatically), when you are innocent of any cultural beliefs that may be to the contrary.
quotes.translation: 身体学会了维持其稳定性、力量和敏捷性，在对天气和元素的互补反应中达到平衡状态，并执行有意识心智无法单独处理的梦境计算。身体学会了在睡眠和梦境中自我疗愈——在那个状态的某些层面上，即使现在，意识的每个部分都贡献于所有其他部分的健康和稳定。远非利爪与匕首的宇宙，你们拥有的宇宙其基础建立在所有部分之间的爱的合作之上。这是给定的——生命的礼物带来了这种合作的实现，因为身体的各个部分之所以作为一个单位存在，是由于合作性质的内在关系；而这些关系在你们出生时（最强调地）就已存在，那时你们对任何可能相反的文化信念一无所知。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Direct cognition is an inner sense. In physical terms you might call it remote sensing. Your physical body, and your physical existence, are based upon certain kinds of direct cognition, and it is responsible for the very functioning of the reasoning mind itself. Scientists like to say that animals operate through simple instinctive behavior, without will or volition: It is no accomplishment for a spider to make its web, a beaver its dam, a bird its nest, because according to such reasoning, such creatures cannot perform otherwise. The spider must spin his web. If he chooses not to, he will not survive. But by that same reasoning--to which, of course, I do not subscribe--you should also add that man can take no credit either for his intellect, since man must think, and cannot help doing so.
quotes.translation: 直接认知是一种内在感官。用物理术语来说，你们可以称之为遥感。你们的物理身体和物理存在基于某种直接认知，它负责推理心智本身的运作。科学家喜欢说动物通过简单的本能行为运作，没有意志或意愿：蜘蛛织网、河狸筑坝、鸟儿筑巢并非成就，因为根据这种推理，这些生物无法不这样做。蜘蛛必须织网。如果它选择不织，它将无法生存。但根据同样的推理——当然，我并不赞同——你们也应该补充说，人类也不能因其智力而居功，因为人类必须思考，且无法不这样做。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The intellect is extremely important in the manipulation of camouflage patterns once they are created. You have made your world, and your intellect should help you deal with what you have created. It has other vital functions which I shall enumerate at a later date. However, I cannot say this too often: You are more than your conscious mind, much more, and the self which you do not admit happens to be the portion of yourself which not only insures your own survival in the physical universe which it has made, but which is also the connective portion of yourself with inner reality. Which is, when all is said and done, the only basic reality; and which also continually enables you to create these camouflage patterns, and which contains knowledge and intuitions and memories which you need in a most desperate manner if you are ever to understand yourselves, and if the race of mankind is ever to evolve to its fullest.
quotes.translation: 智力在操控伪装模式方面极为重要，一旦这些模式被创造出来。你们创造了你们的世界，你们的智力应该帮助你们处理你们所创造的事物。它还有其他重要功能，我将在日后列举。然而，我再怎么强调也不为过：你们不仅仅是你们的有意识心智，远远不止，而你们不承认的那个自我恰好是你们自身的一部分，它不仅确保你们在它所创造的物理宇宙中的生存，而且是你们与内在实相连接的部分。当一切尘埃落定，这是唯一的基本实相；并且它持续使你们能够创造这些伪装模式，它包含你们最迫切需要理解自身、以及人类种族若要充分进化所需的知识、直觉和记忆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: You must become more aware of your inner selves.... They are not all that horrible. You still fear, as our Lady of Venice, that there is a cellar door... a cellar door to your mind--to your inner self. And, if you open it, all sorts of demons will emerge. And if there are any angels, the demons will gobble them up before you get to see them. Instead, I tell you, as I have told you before, you are more than you know. And it is up to you to find your own reality. I cannot give it to you. I can point you in the direction... but the experience is personal and the experience is subjective and the journey is one that you must make and that you must make alone. I cannot make it for you. I have my own journeys to make... and detours here. And any problems that you have I have had them... so look at me and know how indestructible you are!
quotes.translation: 你必须更加觉察自己的内在自我……它们并没有那么可怕。你仍然像威尼斯女士那样，害怕有一扇地窖门——通往你心智、通往你内在自我的地窖门。如果你打开它，各种恶魔就会蜂拥而出。即使有天使存在，恶魔也会在你见到它们之前就把它们吞噬殆尽。相反，我告诉你，正如我之前告诉过你的那样，你远比你所知道的要伟大。找到你自己的实相是你的责任。我无法把它交给你。我可以为你指明方向……但体验是个人的，体验是主观的，这段旅程必须由你亲自完成，而且必须独自完成。我无法替你走。我有自己的旅程要走……还有这里的迂回曲折。你所遇到的任何问题，我都曾经历过……所以看着我，你就会明白你是多么坚不可摧！
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The material is delivered to the body, as I have said, from the inner world by means of these inner senses. This inner reality data is received by the mind. This is extremely important. The mind, being un-camouflaged, is the receiving station for the data brought to it by the inner senses. What you almost have here is an inner nervous and communication system closely resembling the outer systems with which you are familiar.
quotes.translation: 正如我所说，物质是通过这些内在感官从内在世界传递给身体的。这种内在实相数据由心智接收。这一点极其重要。心智，由于没有伪装，是内在感官所传递数据的接收站。这里几乎存在一个内在的神经系统和通讯系统，与你所熟悉的外在系统非常相似。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The personalities possess a keen resiliency of both body and mind, and serve as a strong earth stock. It goes without saying that members of one family often marry into other families. Of course the same thing happens here. When this occurs new stability is inserted, for this particular family acts as a source-stock, providing physical and mental strength. Period. Physically speaking, these people often have many children, and usually the offspring do well in whatever area of life is chosen. (Pause.) Biologically speaking, they possess certain qualities that nullify "negative" codes in the genes.2 They are usually very healthy people, and marriage into this group can automatically end generations of so-called inherited weaknesses.3
quotes.translation: 这些人格拥有身心两方面的敏锐韧性，并作为强大的地球根基。不言而喻，一个家族的成员常常会与其他家族通婚。这里当然也发生同样的事情。当这种情况发生时，新的稳定性就被注入，因为这个特定的家族充当了根源库，提供身心力量。句号。从生理上讲，这些人通常子女众多，而且后代无论选择哪个生活领域，通常都表现出色。（停顿。）从生物学上讲，他们拥有某些能抵消基因中"负面"编码的特质。他们通常是非常健康的人，嫁入这个群体可以自动终结数代所谓的遗传弱点。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Now these notes hark back to the end of the 732nd session, when I wrote a paragraph concerning Sue Watkins, our longtime friend who attends class as often as she can these days from the small town where she now lives, some 35 miles north of Elmira. Jane listed Seth's families of consciousness last month in Session 732, but wound up the evening's work thinking that several years ago, soon after she'd initiated the Sumari breakthrough, Sue had psychically tuned in on the name of a second family of consciousness -- one that Seth didn't give in the 732nd session. Jane thought the family name was similar to the "Gramada" that Seth had described; at session's end I wrote that I intended to check our records for the missing name, and to ask Seth about it -- but I neglected to do either of those things. One of the reasons for my failure to settle the matter right away was the lack of any immediate pressure to do so, for we hadn't seen Sue since before the 729th session was held; that's over five weeks ago now; newspaper work has often kept her too busy to make the trip to Elmira.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It is no coincidence that Ruburt does not possess a scientific vocabulary, though he does possess a scientific as well as intuitive mind. The very attempt to describe reality in scientific terms, as they are currently understood, pays, my dear friend, undue tribute to a vocabulary that automatically scales down greater concepts to fit its rigors. In other words, such attempts further compound the problem of considering a seemingly objective universe, and describing it in an objective fashion.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏不具备科学词汇并非巧合，尽管他确实拥有科学和直觉的心智。用当前理解的科学术语来描述实相的努力，我亲爱的朋友，是对一种词汇的不当推崇，这种词汇会自动缩减更伟大的概念以适应其严谨性。换句话说，这种尝试进一步加剧了将看似客观的宇宙视为客观并用客观方式描述它的问题。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 855, MAY 21, 1979
9:15 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: This is just a short private little session. I have never completely trusted the written word half as much as I trust the spoken word, and on your plane it is difficult to trust either. But I do not feel that I could be myself as easily if you tried automatic writing, for example. I do not mind speaking through Ruburt's mouth. Somehow the sound of the words is rather pleasant. But seeing myself transformed more or less into plain black and white words on a page of paper seems dull and uninteresting. And I have always enjoyed conversation, which is the liveliest of all arts. And I am speaking now of the social arts, dear Joseph. Already I can see you yelling foul. I did not mean that conversation was on an equal par with painting.
quotes.translation: 这只是一个小小的私人课。我从未像信任口头语言那样完全信任书面语言，而在你们层面上，两者都难以信任。但我不认为，例如，如果你尝试自动书写，我能如此轻易地做我自己。我不介意通过鲁柏的嘴说话。不知怎的，话语的声音相当悦耳。但看到自己或多或少被转化成纸上平淡的黑白文字，似乎乏味无趣。我一直喜欢交谈，这是所有艺术中最生动的一种。我现在说的是社交艺术，亲爱的约瑟夫。我已经能看见你在大喊犯规。我并不是说交谈与绘画同等重要。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The very fact that you breathe and dream and perform countless other activities without any aid from the conscious ego should of itself convince even the most stubborn scientific skull that more is involved than science is willing to admit. The idea of the subconscious mind is merely a grudging, hedging, partial admission that man is more than the conscious ego, more than the sum of his parts, and more than a mechanism.
quotes.translation: 你呼吸、做梦以及进行无数其他活动，却无需意识自我的任何帮助，这一事实本身就足以说服即使是最顽固的科学头脑：其中涉及的东西比科学愿意承认的要多得多。潜意识心智的概念，不过是一种勉强的、闪烁其词的、部分的承认：人比意识自我更伟大，比其各部分的总和更伟大，也比一台机器更伟大。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: If you sell yourselves, this is what you will do. You will say, I am a physical organism and I live within the boundaries cast upon me by space and time. I am at the mercy of my environment. If you do not sell yourselves, you will say, I am an individual. I form my physical environment. I change and I make my world. I am free of space and time. I am part of the good mind. I am more than I know that I am, and there is no place within me that creativity does not exist. I will form the physical universe according to the picture that exists within my mind. I cannot kill, for there is only life, and life cannot be killed.
quotes.translation: 如果你出卖自己，你就会这样做。你会说：我是一个物理有机体，生活在时空强加给我的界限之内。我受环境的摆布。如果你不出卖自己，你会说：我是一个个体。我塑造我的物理环境。我改变并创造我的世界。我超越时空。我是善心智的一部分。我比我所知道的自己更伟大，我内在没有一处不存在创造力。我将根据我心智中的画面来塑造物质宇宙。我无法杀戮，因为只有生命存在，而生命无法被杀害。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The brain is a camouflage pattern. It takes up space. It exists in time. The mind takes up no space, it does not have its basic existence in time. The reality of the inner universe does not take up space, nor does it have its basic existence in time. Your camouflage universe, on the other hand, takes up space and has an existence in time, but it is not the real and basic universe, any more than the brain is the mind.
quotes.translation: 大脑是一种伪装模式。它占据空间，存在于时间中。心智不占据空间，它的基本存在不在时间中。内在宇宙的真实性不占据空间，其基本存在也不在时间中。而你们的伪装宇宙则占据空间，存在于时间中，但它并非真实和基本的宇宙，正如大脑并非心智。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: （In the 54th session Seth went into considerable detail in explaining the construction of entities. He could not give us this information earlier, he said, because we would have leaped to the conclusion that he was Ruburt's [Janes] subconscious mind; this is not so. As everything else does, entities constantly change. To quote Seth: "Ruburt is not myself now, in his present life; he is nevertheless an extension and materialization of the Seth that I was at one time... He grew, evolved and expanded in terms of a particular, personal set of value fulfillments... He is now an actual gestalt, a personality that was one of the probable personalities into which Seth could grow. I represent another, I am another. "
quotes.translation: （在第54节课中，赛斯详细解释了存有的构建。他说之前无法告诉我们这些信息，因为我们会贸然断定他就是鲁柏（珍）的潜意识心智；事实并非如此。如同万物一样，存有也在不断变化。引用赛斯的话："鲁柏现在并非我本人，在他此生中；但他是我曾是的赛斯的一个延伸和物质化……他根据一套特定的、个人的价值完成而成长、演化并扩展……他现在是一个实际完形，一个曾是赛斯可能发展成的人格之一。我代表另一个，我是另一个。"）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: When I say that a person misinterprets sense data, I mean that the fine balance between mind and matter becomes overstrained in one direction. There are, then, certain events that connect the world. Though when everything is said and done these events come from outside of the world's order, nevertheless they appear as constants within it. Their reality is the result of the most precise balancing of forces so that certain mental events appear quite real, and others are peripheral. You have dusk and dawn. If in the middle of the night, and fully awake, you believe it is sunrise in physical terms, and cannot differentiate between your personal reality and the physical one, then that balance is disturbed.
quotes.translation: 当我说一个人误解感官资料时，我的意思是心智与物质之间的精妙平衡在某方向上过度紧张。于是，存在某些连接世界的事件。尽管归根结底这些事件来自世界秩序之外，但它们在其中表现为恒常。它们的实相是力量最精确平衡的结果，使得某些心智事件显得相当真实，而其他则处于边缘。你们有黄昏和黎明。如果在深夜完全清醒时，你从物理角度相信是日出，且无法区分个人实相与物理实相，那么那种平衡就被扰乱了。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: hen he originally gave us the 812th session some 16 months ago, Seth told us it would "be part of a chapter later in the book...." [See my opening notes for Session 814.] The 812th session was held following an encounter Jane and I had with an unexpected visitor, although these excerpts aren't personal at all. They fit in well with material in Mass Events, however -- indeed, as soon as Seth confirmed Jane's headings for Part 3 and Chapter 6, we knew that we'd found the place to present this material. We made our decision without consulting Seth, by the way.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: "You wanted some affirmation of your body's vitality, of its resilience and recuperative energies. You also wanted some reassurance that you could operate as an artist as long as you chose in this life. You used the incident of the optometrist's notice to give yourself a very fine lesson, for in the back of your mind you did indeed worry and wonder that your eyes were becoming tired. Under usual circumstances, those "symptoms" would be interpreted as signs of difficulty. You discovered instead that the so-called symptoms are signs that your glasses have become too strong because your eyesight has not simply held its own, but most remarkably improved, and in a way this is medically demonstrable.
quotes.translation: "你希望确认你身体的活力、韧性和恢复能量。你也希望得到保证，在此生中只要选择就能以艺术家身份运作。你利用验光师通知的事件给自己上了一堂极好的课，因为你内心深处确实担忧并怀疑眼睛是否变得疲劳。在通常情况下，那些'症状'会被解读为困难的迹象。但你发现，所谓的症状恰恰表明你的眼镜度数过高，因为你的视力不仅没有衰退，反而显著改善，这在某种程度上是医学可证实的。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your conscious mind can only accept a certain sequence of probabilities as recognized experience. As I have said, the choices among probabilities go on constantly, both on conscious and unconscious levels. Events that you do not perceive as conscious experience are (pause) a part of your unconscious experience, however, to some extent. This applies to the individual, and of course en masse the same applies to world events. Each action seeks all of its own possible fulfillments. All That Is seeks all possible experience, but in such a larger framework in this case that questions of, say, pain or death simply do not apply, though [certainly] they do on the physical level (all quite forcefully).
quotes.translation: 你的意识心智只能接受特定序列的概率作为被认知的经验。如我所说，概率的选择持续进行，既在意识层面也在无意识层面。你未感知为意识经验的事件（停顿）在某种程度上是你无意识经验的一部分。这适用于个人，当然也适用于世界事件。每个行动都寻求其所有可能的完成。一切万有寻求所有可能的经验，但在此类更大框架中，诸如痛苦或死亡的问题根本不适用，尽管（相当有力地）它们在物理层面确实存在。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Very slowly at 11:18:) The conscious mind is an excellent perceiving attribute, a function that belongs to inner awareness but in this case is turned outward toward the world of events. Through the conscious mind the soul looks outward. Left alone, it perceives clearly.
quotes.translation: （11:18分，非常缓慢地：）意识心是一个卓越的感知属性，一种属于内在觉知的功能，但在这种情况下它转向外在，朝向事件的世界。通过意识心，灵魂向外观看。若不加干预，它便能清晰感知。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your present methods will simply bring you pat, manufactured results and answers. They will satisfy neither the intellect nor the soul. Since your universe springs from an inner one, and since that inner one pervades each nook and cranny of your own existence, you must look where you have not before -- into the reality of your own minds and emotions. You must look to the natural universe that you know. You must look with your intuitions and creative instincts at the creatures about you, seeing them not as other species with certain habits, not as inferior properties of the earth, to be dissected, but as living examples of the nature of the universe, in constant being and transformation.
quotes.translation: 你们目前的方法只会带来现成的、机械化的结果和答案。它们既无法满足理智，也无法满足灵魂。既然你们的宇宙源自一个内在宇宙，而那个内在宇宙又渗透到你们自身存在的每一个角落，你们必须看向从未看过的地方——进入你们自己心智和情绪的真实。你们必须看向你们所知的自然宇宙。你们必须用你们的直觉和创造本能，去观察周围的生物，不再将它们视为具有特定习性的其他物种，或视为地球上可被解剖的低等财产，而是视为宇宙本质的活生生例证，处于持续的存在与转化之中。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I would like to add a note concerning the importance and dangers of belief. An open mind is by far one of the best advantages that you can possess. Belief is a two way door, although it does not have to be. One belief, for example, may seem to negate another belief. Close examination will often prove this is not the case. Simply for example, a belief in the color red does not negate belief in the color green.
quotes.translation: 我想补充一点关于信念的重要性和危险性的说明。开放的心智是你们所能拥有的最佳优势之一。信念是一扇双向门，尽管它不必如此。例如，一个信念似乎可能否定另一个信念。但仔细审视往往会证明并非如此。简单举例：对红色的信念并不否定对绿色的信念。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The past exists as a series of electromagnetic connections, held in the physical brain on the one hand, but it also consists of the same sort of realities retained in the nonphysical mind. These electromagnetic connections can be changed. The present exists as a series of electromagnetic connections in both the brain and the mind, and this is the only reality which you are justified in giving to your present.
quotes.translation: 过去作为一系列电磁连接而存在，一方面存于物质大脑中，但它也由存于非物质心智中的同类实相构成。这些电磁连接可以被改变。现在作为一系列电磁连接存在于大脑和心智两者之中，而这是你们唯一有理由赋予你们现在的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: No matter what science says about certain values being outside of its frame of reference, science implies that those values are therefore without basis. The reasoning qualities of the mind are directed away from any exploration that might bring about any acceptable scientific evidence for such values, therefore. The fact is that man lives by those values that science ignores (quietly emphatic, and repeated).
quotes.translation: 无论科学如何声称某些价值在其参考框架之外，科学都暗示这些价值因此毫无根据。因此，心智的推理能力被引导远离任何可能为这类价值带来可接受的科学证据的探索。事实是，人类正是依靠科学所忽视的那些价值而活（平静而强调地，并重复）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your inner self adopts the physically conscious, physically focused mind as a method of allowing it to manipulate in the world that you know. The conscious mind is particularly equipped to direct outward activity, to handle waking experience and oversee physical work.
quotes.translation: 你们的内在自我采用物质上有意识、物质上聚焦的心智，作为允许它在你们所知的世界上运作的方法。意识心特别配备来指导外在活动，处理清醒体验，并监督物质工作。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The concept of nirvana (see the 637th session in Chapter Nine) and the idea of heaven are two versions of the same picture, the former being one in which individuality is lost in the bliss of undifferentiated consciousness, and the latter one in which still-conscious individuals perform mindless adoration. Neither theory contains an understanding of the functions of the conscious mind, or the evolution of consciousness -- or, for that matter, certain aspects of greater physics. No energy is ever lost. The expanding universe theory* applies to the mind as well as to the universe.
quotes.translation: 涅槃的概念（见第九章第637节）与天堂的观念是同一画面的两个版本：前者是个体性消融于无分别意识的极乐中，后者则是仍有意识的个体进行无心的崇拜。这两种理论都未能理解有意识心智的功能、意识的演化——就此而言，也未能理解更宏大物理学的某些面向。能量永不消失。宇宙膨胀理论*既适用于宇宙，也适用于心智。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: No negative beliefs were thrust upon you, therefore, despite your will. Period. None can be inflicted upon you that you do not consciously accept. In formal hypnosis, the hypnotist and the subject play a game. If the hypnotist orders the subject to forget what happened, that individual will pretend to do so. In that context both hold the belief in the resulting forgetfulness, and it is the power of belief that is being demonstrated. But instead this is taken as an indication that the conscious mind is helpless under such conditions, generally speaking, and this is not the case.
quotes.translation: 因此，没有任何负面信念曾违背你的意志强加于你。句号。凡是你未自觉接受的信念，无人能强加于你。在正式催眠中，催眠师与受试者玩着一种游戏。若催眠师命令受试者忘记所发生之事，此人会假装如此行事。在此情境中，双方都相信随之而来的失忆，这正是信念力量在展现。但人们却将此视为有意识心智在此类条件下无能为力的证据——一般而言，事实并非如此。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ideas originate always from individuals on any plane, and under any circumstance. The ideas once originated, however, are given energy reality by the individual and therefore attain a vitality of their own. The ideas are picked up by the subconscious through a telepathic communication. They do not however have to be picked up directly from one mind, that is, there does not have to be direct communication.
quotes.translation: 观念始终源于任何层面、任何环境中的个体。然而，观念一旦被个体赋予能量实相，便获得自身生命力。这些观念通过心灵感应沟通被潜意识接收。但它们未必需要直接从某个心智接收——也就是说，未必需要直接的沟通。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: It is however usually operating at subconscious levels, and without either knowledge or intent as far as the conscious mind is concerned. To be able to bring these natural but subconscious forces at all under any domination by the conscious mind is a terrific task.
quotes.translation: 然而它通常运作于潜意识层面，就有意识心智而言，既无认知也无意图。要让有意识心智能对这些自然却潜意识的能量施加任何掌控，实为艰巨任务。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Man automatically began to form culture. He did not start with the rudiments of culture, as is thought. He did not learn (pause) through trial and error to think clear thoughts. He thought quite clearly from the beginning. He did learn through trial and error various ways of best translating those thoughts into physical action. The first cultures were as rich as your own. In your terms, reading and writing are great advantages, but it is also true that in the past the mind was also used to record information, and transmit it with an artistry that you do not now use.
quotes.translation: 人类自动开始形成文化。他并非如人们所想，从文化的雏形起步。他并非通过试错法学习清晰思考。他从一开始就思考得相当清晰。他确实通过试错法学会了如何以最佳方式将这些思想转化为物理行动。最早的文化与你们的文化同样丰富。以你们的说法，读写能力是巨大优势，但同样真实的是，过去心智也被用于记录信息，并以你们现今不再使用的艺术性方式传递信息。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Psychic sympathy can be expanded in some space of the mind, of any mind, of any inner mind. All things are known, for in the depths of the inner self emotional sympathy is endless. By sympathy I refer to an inner connection, an attraction, not necessarily a pity.
quotes.translation: 心灵共鸣可在心智的某些空间——任何心智、任何内在心智——中扩展。万有皆被知晓，因为内在自我深处的情感共鸣永无止境。我所说的共鸣，指的是内在连接、吸引力，未必是怜悯。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The tree makes adjustments as you make adjustments. The tree listens to its growth up from the earth and listens also to the murmur of the growth of its roots beneath. It adjusts each root ending according to what impediments might lie in its way. Without the so-called mind of man, it nevertheless retains this inner consciousness of all its parts above and below the ground, and adjusts them constantly.
quotes.translation: 树木会随着你的调整而调整。它倾听自己从大地向上生长的声音，也倾听地下根系生长的低语。它根据前方可能遇到的障碍调整每条根系的末端。虽然没有人类所谓的心智，它却保持着对地上地下所有部分的内在觉知，并持续调整它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: In certain terms, and in certain terms only, and speaking now as the psychological bridge personality, then what you perceive in me and these abilities represents a portion of Ruburt that is utterly free in those directions -- a portion of the human mind, as you understand it, goes beyond the threshold of itself into other dimensions of actuality; then, as best it can, it translates what it learns, sees, and experiences. It goes out of itself -- it launches itself on paths that it does not understand, taking journeys that even Ruburt does not understand; and yet, that one portion of Ruburt's human personality is that free. And so you can see what happens!
quotes.translation: 在某种层面上——且仅限于某种层面——作为心理桥梁人格的我现在要说：你们在我身上感知到的这些能力，代表鲁柏人格中在那些方向上完全自由的部分——人类心智的一部分，如你们所理解的，跨越了自身的门槛进入其他维度的实相；然后尽其所能地翻译它所学习、看见和体验的一切。它走出自身——将自己投射到连鲁柏自己都不理解的路径上，进行连鲁柏都不理解的旅程；然而，鲁柏人类人格的那一部分就是如此自由。所以你们可以看到发生了什么！
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Now I'll refer the reader to Note 6 for Session 711, which is the session for which I'm assembling this chronology to begin with. In that note I presented some material on ancient connections with the name, Seth, then quoted Seth on the subject of names from the ESP class session for April 17, 1973. For instance:)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now: For Ruburt, I want him to remember the idea of effortlessness, because with the best of intentions he has been trying too hard. (Pause.) I want him to remember that relaxation is one of creativity's greatest champions -- not its enemy. He is naturally gifted with the quickness of body and mind. Remind him that it is safe to express his natural (underlined) rhythms, to remember the natural person. Your most vital inspirations are effortlessly yours. I want you to see how many of your beliefs are the result of the old framework, for in that way you will find yourselves releasing yourselves more and more, so that your own strengths come to your support.
quotes.translation: 现在：对于鲁柏，我要他记住"毫不费力"这个概念，因为他怀着最好的意图却过于努力了。（停顿）我要他记住，放松是创造力最伟大的盟友——而非敌人。他天生就拥有身心敏捷的天赋。提醒他：表达他自然的（强调）节奏是安全的，要记住那个自然的人。你们最关键的灵感会毫不费力地属于你们。我要你们看清有多少信念是旧框架的产物，因为这样你们会发现自己越来越释放自己，让自身的力量来支持你们。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: It has been truthfully said that the "unconscious" is intimately aware of the most minute details of your health, state of mind, and relationships. "It" is also aware of the state of the earth's health -- even of the environmental conditions on the other side of the planet. It is familiar with the cultural climate also. Your recognized consciousness operates as it does because of immense information-gathering procedures -- procedures that unite all species. Biologically such information is coded, but that physical information, such as in the genes and chromosomes, can be altered through experience and mental activity in other species as well as your own.
quotes.translation: 诚然，"无意识"密切知晓你们健康、心态和人际关系最细微的细节。"它"也知晓地球的健康状态——甚至地球另一端的环境状况。它同样熟悉文化气候。你们所认知的意识之所以如此运作，是因为庞大的信息收集程序——这些程序联合了所有物种。生物学上这类信息被编码，但那些物理信息——如基因和染色体中的——可以通过经验和其他物种（包括你们自身）的心智活动而改变。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Whatever your language, you perceive trees, mountains, people, oceans. You never see a man merge with a tree, for example. This would be considered an hallucinatory image. Your visual data are learned and interpreted so that they appear as the only possible results of those data. Inner vision can confound you, because in your mind you often see images quite clearly that you would dismiss if your eyes were open. In the terms of which we are speaking, however, the young species utilized what I have called the "inner senses" to a far greater degree than you do. Visually, early man did not perceive the physical world in the way that seems natural to you.
quotes.translation: 无论你们使用何种语言，你们感知树木、山脉、人类、海洋。例如，你们从未见过一个人与一棵树融合。这会被视为幻觉影像。你们的视觉数据经过学习和解读，以至于它们看起来是这些数据的唯一可能结果。内在视觉会令你们困惑，因为在你们的心智中，你们常常清晰地看到那些如果睁开眼睛就会否定的影像。然而，就我们正在讨论的层面而言，年轻物种使用我所谓的"内在感官"的程度远高于你们。视觉上，早期人类并不以你们看来自然的方式感知物质世界。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The new director does indeed have a fussy, almost womanish love of detail for detail's sake, and this sort of mind is one with which Ruburt finds it difficult to attune when closely involved. And here is a pretty point: Ruburts insistence upon the term gallery secretary left room for a certain independence and impersonality and leeway, that the term Mr. Soandso's secretary does not leave room for.
quotes.translation: 这位新主管确实有一种吹毛求疵、近乎女性化的对细节本身的爱好，而鲁柏发现当密切接触时，很难与这种心智调谐。这里有个精妙之处：鲁柏坚持使用"画廊秘书"这个称谓，为的是保留一定的独立性、非个人性和回旋余地，而"某某先生的秘书"这个称谓则没有这种空间。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: "Being your own natural and magical self when you dream, you utilize information that is outside of the time context experienced by the so-called rational mind. The creative abilities operate in the same fashion, appearing within consecutive time, but with the main work done outside of it entirely.... When you were both working on your projects, your cultural time was taken up in a way you found acceptable. When the projects were done, particularly with Ruburt, there was still the cultural belief that time should be so used (underlined), that creativity must be directed and disciplined to fall into the proper assembly-line time slots.
quotes.translation: 当你做梦时，作为自然且充满魔力的自己，你会运用那些被所谓理性心智所体验的时间脉络之外的信息。创造能力以同样的方式运作，它们显现在连续的时间中，但主要工作却完全在时间之外完成……当你们两人都在进行自己的项目时，你们的文化时间以你们可接受的方式被占用。当项目完成后，特别是对鲁柏而言，仍然存在这样的文化信念：时间应该被如此使用（下划线强调），创造力必须被引导和约束，以落入适当的流水线时间槽中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 4. In Chapter 2 for Volume 1 of Dreams, see the opening notes for Session 885.

quotes.text: Now in your terms only, these other counterparts are like latent patterns within your mind. Echoes. How many of you have actually thought of what the unconscious may be? Or, the voices that you hear within your mind or heart? Are they yours? To what counterparts do they belong? And yet each of you, in your own identity, has the right to do precisely as you wish, and to form your own reality....
quotes.translation: 现在，仅就你们的角度而言，这些其他对应体就像你心智中的潜在模式。回响。你们中有多少人真正思考过无意识可能是什么？或者，你在心智或心中听到的声音？它们是你的吗？它们属于哪些对应体？然而，你们每个人，在自己的身份中，都有权利完全按照自己的意愿行事，并形成自己的实相……
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (The material in these recent excerpts rather prepared us for Seth's introduction of counterparts, then, in Session 721. In ESP class the next evening [on November 26], Seth began contending with some of the questions that instantly arose as a result of his new material. I'd just read aloud portions of the 721st session when one longtime student, whom I'll call Florence, commented that there "has to be a balance between each of us and our counterparts." Speaking strongly and humorously, Seth immediately took over the discussion.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Examine the event as you know it. Then try to trace its emergence from the thread of your own past life as you understand it, and project outward in your mind what other events might emerge from that one to become action in your probable future. This exercise has another part: When you have finished the procedure just given, then change your viewpoint; see the event from the standpoint of someone else who is also involved. No matter how private the experience seems, someone else will have a connection with it. See the episode through his or her eyes, then continue with the procedure just given, only using this altered viewpoint.
quotes.translation: 审视你所知的事件。然后，试着追溯它从你理解中的过去生活线索中浮现的过程，并在你的心智中向外投射，看看哪些其他事件可能从那个事件中浮现，成为你可能的未来中的行动。这个练习还有另一部分：当你完成刚刚给出的步骤后，改变你的视角；从同样参与其中的另一个人的立场来看待这个事件。无论经历看起来多么私密，总会有另一个人与之相关。通过他或她的眼睛来看待这个片段，然后继续刚刚给出的步骤，只是使用这个改变的视角。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 695
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: As a civilization you fail to reap dreams' greater benefit, and the conscious mind is able to handle much more dream recall than you allow. Such training would add immeasurably to the dimensions of your life. Dreams educate you even in spatial relationships, and are far more related to the organism's stance in the environment than is realized. The child learns spatial relationships in dreams.
quotes.translation: 作为一个文明，你们未能收获梦境的更大益处，而意识心智能够处理比你们允许的更多的梦境回忆。这样的训练将无可估量地增加你们生命的维度。梦境甚至在空间关系上教育你，并且与有机体在环境中的姿态的关联远比人们意识到的更为密切。孩子在梦中学习空间关系。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When women give birth in a crowded world they also know, and with a portion of their conscious minds, that a violation is involved. When your species sees that it is destroying other species and disrupting the natural balance, then it is consciously aware of its violation. When such natural guilt is not faced there are other mechanisms that must be employed. Again at the risk of repeating myself: Many of your problems result from the fact that you do not accept the responsibility of your own consciousness. It is meant to assess the reality that is unconsciously formed in direct replica of your thoughts and expectations.
quotes.translation: 当女性在一个拥挤的世界中生育时，她们也知道，并且用她们意识心智的一部分，知道这涉及一种侵犯。当你们的物种看到自己在毁灭其他物种并破坏自然平衡时，那么它就有意识地意识到自己的侵犯。当这种自然的内疚不被面对时，就必须采用其他机制。再次冒着重复的风险：你们的许多问题源于这样一个事实：你们不接受自己意识的责任。它本应评估那些直接复制你们思想和期望而无意识形成的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Intellectual truth alone will not make you free, though it is certainly a necessary preliminary. If this were the case your walls would fall away, since intellectually you understand their rather dubious nature. Since feeling is so often the cohesive with which mind builds, it is feeling itself which must be changed if you would find freedom from your particular plane of existence at your particular time. That is, to some extent a change in feeling will allow you to see variants. Since feeling is a cohesive, to change it completely would hardly be of any advantage since your world of present existence would fall apart.
quotes.translation: 仅凭理智的真理不会让你自由，尽管它当然是必要的先决条件。如果情况如此，你们的墙壁就会倒塌，因为理智上你们理解它们相当可疑的本质。既然感觉常常是心智构建的粘合剂，如果你们想从特定时间特定存在层面获得自由，就必须改变感觉本身。也就是说，在某种程度上，感觉的改变会让你看到变体。既然感觉是一种粘合剂，完全改变它几乎没有任何好处，因为你们当前存在的世界会分崩离析。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: He was determined to find the kind of mate that would best suit him and his own unique characteristics. That intent was in his mind. When that challenge was met he used his will and mobilized all of his power to fulfill his abilities, and to bring about conditions in which he hoped Joseph (as Seth calls me) could also fulfill his. The will, again, operates according to the personality's beliefs about reality, so its desires are sometimes tempered as those beliefs change. In his own way Ruburt always concentrated upon one challenge at a time -- boring in, so to speak, and ignoring anything else that might distract him.
quotes.translation: 他决心找到最适合自己独特特质的伴侣。这个意图存在于他的心智中。当这个挑战被克服后，他运用意志调动全部力量来发挥自己的能力，并创造他希望约瑟（赛斯对我的称呼）也能发挥其能力的条件。意志再次根据人格对实相的看法运作，因此其欲望有时会随着这些信念的改变而受到调和。鲁柏总是以自己的方式一次专注于一个挑战——可以说，是钻进去，忽略任何可能分散他注意力的事物。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 713
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Dictation: Luckily the human mind and body are far more flexible, durable and creative than ever given credit for. Many cases like Augustus's never come to light. The individuals involved cure themselves. Sometimes this is done when such a person chooses to undergo a traumatic experience -- often one part of the personality will plan this quite deliberately while the other portion closes its eyes. These events can seem to be disasters or near disasters, and yet they can sufficiently mobilize the entire personality for survival's sake. In a moment of high critical tension the personality may put itself together again.
quotes.translation: 口授：幸运的是，人类的心智和身体远比人们所认为的更加灵活、持久和富有创造力。许多像奥古斯都这样的案例从未曝光。当事人自己治愈了自己。有时，当这样的人选择经历创伤性体验时——通常人格的一部分会相当刻意地计划这件事，而另一部分则闭上眼睛——这种情况就会发生。这些事件可能看起来像是灾难或近乎灾难，然而它们足以为了生存而调动整个人格。在高度危急的紧张时刻，人格可能会重新整合自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: As your cells have their own memories, so the conscious mind has a more overt kind of memory. Your conscious thoughts act as triggers, bringing both kinds of memory into activation. Within your physical being then each joyful, expanding, traumatic and tragic "past" event lies indelibly written. In your terms this is your working material, the memory of your physical being since the time of its conception in corporeal form. There are [in your memory] the most complex organizations and associative frameworks, that exist both in the depths of your cellular structure and in the highest reaches of your conscious activity.
quotes.translation: 正如你们的细胞拥有自己的记忆，意识心也有一种更显性的记忆。你们有意识的念头充当触发器，激活这两种记忆。在你们物质存在中，每一个快乐、扩展、创伤和悲剧的"过去"事件都不可磨灭地铭刻其中。用你们的话说，这是你们的工作素材，是你们物质存在从受孕成形以来的记忆。在你们的记忆中，存在着最复杂的组织和联想框架，既存在于你们细胞结构的深处，也存在于你们意识活动的最高层面。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You might even make a reasonable, and I mean reasonable, Ruburt, list of preferred circumstances such as shelves built, rooms painted and so forth, and carry them out yourselves. Whatever if anything now daily aggravates you, change these things and you will be amazed at your added peace of mind. I would have thought that you would have hit upon the entryway idea yourselves; something to keep your psychic energies contained, and again it is the feeling that the entryway gives you rather than the entryway itself, and the feeling that a desk and chair in another room gives Ruburt, rather than the desk and chair itself.
quotes.translation: 你甚至可以列出一份合理的——我指的是合理的，鲁柏——偏好环境的清单，比如搭好架子、粉刷房间等等，然后自己动手去做。无论现在每天有什么事情让你烦恼，改变这些事情，你会惊讶于内心增加的平静。我本以为你们自己会想到入口通道这个主意；一些能容纳你们心灵能量的东西，再说一遍，重要的是入口通道给你的感觉，而不是入口通道本身，以及另一个房间里的书桌和椅子给鲁柏的感觉，而不是书桌和椅子本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Too-conscious an attempt will not help, but this material should help. The physical organism does indeed require sleep, but only the physical organism with its brain. The mind is continually in vigilance. Nevertheless when you are more proficient you will, with the aid of automatic use of psychological time, be able to operate much more effectively, and be upheld by energy without strain.
quotes.translation: 过于刻意的尝试不会有帮助，但这些资料应该会有帮助。物质有机体确实需要睡眠，但只是带有大脑的物质有机体需要。心智则持续保持警觉。不过，当你们更加熟练时，借助对心理时间的自动运用，你们将能够更有效地运作，并在没有压力的情况下获得能量的支撑。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: You must realize, and tell yourself, that creativity is timeless in a basic manner. You must completely cease inner speculations and regrets and questions as to why your abilities have not come to fruition. This must positively be done. The negative suggestion works constantly against you. When such thoughts come to mind, instead tell yourself that that trend of thought will not help your painting, but hinder it, and that it is constricting. Then immediately imagine a time when you painted very well and spontaneously, and tell yourself that you are now free to use and develop these abilities. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 你必须认识到，并告诉自己，创造力在根本上是超越时间的。你必须完全停止内心的揣测、后悔和关于你的能力为何未能开花结果的疑问。这必须坚决做到。负面暗示持续地与你作对。当这样的念头出现时，反而要告诉自己，这种思路不会有助于你的绘画，反而会阻碍它，并且它是限制性的。然后立即想象一个你画得非常好且自发的时刻，并告诉自己，你现在可以自由地运用和发展这些能力。（停顿。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The idea, then, of the novel came from past and future events, though you were to catch up with those future events very quickly. Your mind intuitively organized all of that material, and put it together in a completely new fashion. Sometimes when such events occur, the precognitive trigger is not even recognized when it is encountered physically, because it happens too far ahead of time. (To me:) You organize mental and physical events in a creative manner. In this case a novel was involved because the concept, while strongly involving images, carried a time span that would make narrative necessary.
quotes.translation: 那么，这部小说的构思来自过去与未来的事件，尽管你很快就要赶上那些未来事件。你的心智直觉地组织了所有那些材料，并以全新的方式将它们整合在一起。有时当这类事件发生时，预知触发点甚至在物理层面遭遇时都未被识别，因为它发生的时间过于超前。（对我说：）你以创造性的方式组织心智与物理事件。在这个案例中涉及一部小说，因为其概念虽强烈包含意象，却承载着需要叙事的时间跨度。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Give us a moment.... That will be it for the evening. My heartiest regards to each of you. I have but one more point to make: Each person's experience of a painful nature is also registered on the part of what we will call the world's mind. Each, say, failure, or disappointment, or unresolved problem that results in suffering, becomes a part of the world's experience: This way or that way does not work, or this way or that way has been tried, with poor results. So in that way even weaknesses or failures of suffering are resolved, or rather redeemed as adjustments are made in the light of those data.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……今晚就到这里。我向各位致以最诚挚的问候。我只有一点要补充：每个人痛苦性质的体验也会被记录在我们所谓的世界心智中。每一次失败、失望或导致苦难的未解问题，都成为世界经验的一部分：这种方式或那种方式行不通，或这种方式或那种方式已被尝试却结果不佳。因此，即使苦难中的弱点或失败也会被解决，更确切地说，随着根据那些数据做出调整而被救赎。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "Ruburt's state of mind was in (telepathic) correspondence with your own state of mind, even as you are in some kind of correspondence with your old (childhood) environment, so in these cases you have a free flow of information at other levels.
quotes.translation: "鲁柏的心智状态与你的心智状态处于（心灵感应）对应中，正如你与旧日（童年）环境处于某种对应中一样，因此在这些情况下，你在其他层面拥有信息的自由流动。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 6. In Chapter 8 of Dreams, see the opening notes for Session 915.

quotes.text: "The 'Unknown' Reality itself is a product of the unknown reality of the mind, of course, since I produced it entirely in a trance state, as Seth. In a way the two volumes are the products of an inner psychic 'combustion' -- the spark that is lit in our world, as Seth's reality strikes mine -- or vice versa. For me, this is an accelerated state. I would compare it to a higher state of wakefulness rather than to the sleep usually associated with trance -- but a different kind of wakefulness, in which the usual world seems to be the one that is sleeping. My attention is not blunted. It is elsewhere.
quotes.translation: "'未知的实相'本身当然是心智未知实相的产物，因为我完全在出神状态中以赛斯的身份创作了它。从某种角度看，这两卷书是内在心灵'燃烧'的产物——那在我们世界点燃的火花，当赛斯的实相撞击我的实相时——反之亦然。对我来说，这是一种加速状态。我将其比作更高的清醒状态，而非通常与出神关联的睡眠——但那是另一种清醒，在其中平常世界似乎才是沉睡的那个。我的注意力并未迟钝，它只是去了别处。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Every so often I've thought of averaging Jane's dictation time for Seth Speaks and Personal Reality in the same way, but haven't done so. I'm somewhat puzzled to note, however, that her very short working times for the Seth books seem to be either ignored or taken for granted by practically everyone -- or, perhaps, those factors just aren't understood in terms of ordinary linear time. Maybe I'm alone in my interest here, for even Jane doesn't express any great curiosity about the time she has invested in the Seth material; she just delivers it. But given her abilities, I think her speed of production is a close physical approach to, or translation of, Seth's idea that basically all exists at once -- that really there is no time, and that the Seth books, for example, are "there" to be had in final form for just the tuning in. (In Section 3 of this volume, Note 2 for Session 692 contains information on another way by which we can move closer to Seth's idea of simultaneity from our physical reality, but that method grows out of material not discussed here.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Now: When you create a mental image in your mind it is composed of the same properties just mentioned. A mental image then is also a pattern of internal sound with electromagnetic properties imbued with certain light values. In a sense, and a very real one, the mental image is incipient matter; and any structure so composed, combining the electromagnetic sound and light values, will automatically try to reproduce itself in physical existence, or materialization. (Long pause.) There is a definite connection, then, with the nature of such images and the way in which your body itself is composed.
quotes.translation: 现在：当你在心智中创造心象时，它由刚才提到的相同属性构成。因此心象也是一种具有电磁特性的内在声音模式，并注入了某些光值。在某种意义上，且是非常真实的意义上，心象是初生的物质；任何如此构成的、结合电磁声音与光值的结构，都会自动试图在物理存在或物质化中复制自身。（长停顿。）因此，这类意象的性质与你身体本身的构成方式之间存在明确关联。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The first journey from one home station to another, unfamiliar one may bring you in contact with various kinds of bleed-throughs, distortions, or static. These can be expected. They are simply the result of not yet learning how to tune your own consciousness clearly in to other kinds of focus. Before you can pick up the "next" station, for example, you may see ghost images in your mind, or pick up distorted versions from your own home station. You have momentarily dispensed with the usual, habitual organizational process by which you unite regular physical sense perceptions, so while you are "between stations," you may well encounter mixed signals from each. When you alter your conscious focus in such a fashion, you are also moving away from the part of your psyche that you consider its center. You are journeying through your own psyche, in other words, for different realities are different states of the psyche -- materialized, projected outward and experienced. That applies to your home station or physical world as well.
quotes.translation: 从一处家园站台到另一处陌生站台的首次旅程，可能会让你接触到各种渗透、扭曲或静电干扰。这些是可以预期的。它们仅仅是因为尚未学会如何将自己的意识清晰调谐到其他焦点所致。例如，在你接收到"下一站"之前，你可能会在心智中看到鬼影般的意象，或从自己的家园站台接收到扭曲版本。你暂时放弃了通常惯常的组织过程——即你借以统合常规物理感官知觉的过程——因此当你处于"站台之间"时，你很可能会遇到来自各方的混合信号。当你以这种方式改变意识焦点时，你也在远离你视为自己心灵中心的部分。换句话说，你正在穿越自己的心灵，因为不同的实相就是心灵的不同状态——物质化、向外投射并被体验。这同样适用于你的家园站台或物理世界。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Man's physical world, with all of its civilizations and cultural aspects, and even with its technologies and sciences, basically represents the species' innate drive to communicate, to move outward, to create, and to objectify sensed inner realities. The most private life imaginable is a very social affair. The most secluded recluse must still depend upon the biological sociability of not only his own body cells, but of the natural world with all of its creatures. The body, then, no matter how private, is also a public, social, biological statement. A spoken sentence has a certain structure in any language. It presupposes a mouth and a tongue, the kind of physical organization necessary; a mind; a certain kind of world in which sounds have meaning; and a very precise, quite practical knowledge of the nature of sounds, the combination of their patterns, the use of repetition, and a knowledge of the nervous system. Few of my readers possess such conscious knowledge, yet the majority speak quite well.
quotes.translation: 人类的物质世界，连同其所有文明与文化面向，甚至包括科技与科学，基本上代表了该物种与生俱来的驱动力：沟通、向外拓展、创造，并将感知到的内在实相客观化。最私密的生活想象起来也是一件非常社会化的事务。最与世隔绝的隐士，仍然必须依赖不仅是他自身身体细胞的生物社会性，还有自然界及其所有造物的生物社会性。因此，身体无论多么私密，也是一种公开的、社会的、生物的声明。在任何语言中，一句说出的话都有其特定结构。它预设了嘴巴和舌头，必要的物理组织形态；一个心智；一个声音具有意义的特定世界；以及对声音本质、模式组合、重复运用、神经系统运作的精确而实用的知识。我的读者中很少有人具备这种有意识的知识，但大多数人却说得相当好。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some of the "particles" the theoretical physicists have discovered--and/or created--in their gigantic particle accelerators have unbelievably short life-spans in our terms, vanishing, it seems, almost before they're born. I like to think of such research from the particle's point of view, though, a consideration I haven't seen mentioned in the few scientific journals I read. Keep in mind that according to the Seth material the merest particle is basically conscious in its own way. Mesons are classes of particles produced from the collisions of protons. Did a meson, for example, choose to participate in an atom-smashing experiment in order to merely peek in on our gross physical reality for much less than the billionth of a second it exists with that identity, before it decays into electrons and photons? From its viewpoint, our reality might be as incomprehensible to it as its reality is to us--yet the two inevitably go together.
quotes.translation: 理论物理学家在巨型粒子加速器中发现——和/或创造——的一些“粒子”，以我们的时间尺度来看，其寿命短得令人难以置信，似乎几乎在诞生之前就消失了。不过，我喜欢从粒子的角度来思考这类研究，这是我在阅读的少数科学期刊中未曾见过的考量。请记住，根据赛斯资料，最微小的粒子在其自身方式上基本上是有意识的。介子是由质子碰撞产生的一类粒子。例如，一个介子是否选择参与原子撞击实验，仅仅是为了在我们粗陋的物质实相中瞥一眼，在其衰变成电子和光子之前，以那个身份存在不到十亿分之一秒？从它的视角来看，我们的实相对它来说可能像它的实相对我们一样难以理解——然而这两者却不可避免地共存。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 1. In Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality, I wrote in Note 7 for Session 681 that atoms are "processes" rather than things. The classical conception of the typical atom as being composed of a neat nucleus of indivisible protons and neutrons circled by electrons is largely passé, although for convenience's sake we may still describe the atom that way. (In those terms, the one exception is the hydrogen atom, which evidently consists of but one proton and one electron cloud, or "smear.") For the simple purposes of this note, then, I'm leaving out considerations involving quantum mechanics, which concept repudiates the idea of "particles" to begin with. (And surely that notion involves more than a little of the psychic, or "irrational." What a heretical thought from the scientific viewpoint!) But each atom of whatever element is an amazingly complicated, finely balanced assemblage of forces and particles woven together in exquisite detail--one of the more basic examples of the unending and stupendous creativity, order, and design of nature, or consciousness, or All That Is.

quotes.text: The physical brain as you know it is but the camouflage mechanism belonging to the physically oriented self. In some cases, severe damage is done to the brain, and yet rationality continues. In these cases the mind behind the brain operates as always. When the individual is convinced that his activities are completely dependent upon the physical brain, then he is incapacitated almost completely by any injury to it. The brain itself contains knowledge that comes through the inner senses, and this knowledge would simply not be available to it, if it were dependent upon physical data completely.
quotes.translation: 你所知的物质大脑，只是属于物质导向自我的伪装机制。在某些情况下，大脑受到严重损伤，但理性却依然持续。在这些情况下，大脑背后的心智一如既往地运作。当个体确信其活动完全依赖于物质大脑时，那么任何对大脑的损伤都会使他几乎完全丧失能力。大脑本身包含着通过内在感官获得的知识，如果它完全依赖于物质数据，这些知识根本不可能为它所拥有。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Your cellular structure is innately able to follow such sequences. Believing such clues to be meaningless, the conscious mind does not perceive them, or calls them coincidences. Such clues in your intimate daily life, however, looked at in a different way, can tell you much about the potentials of the species, and give you glimpses of other systems of reality in which human consciousness can respond. I am here using an incident from the experience of Ruburt and Joseph, but the reader can make his or her own correlations, and discover like events from which the same conclusions can be drawn.
quotes.translation: 你的细胞结构天生就能遵循这样的序列。由于相信这些线索毫无意义，有意识的心智便无法感知它们，或称之为巧合。然而，在你亲密的日常生活中，以不同的方式看待这些线索，可以告诉你很多关于这个物种的潜能，并让你瞥见人类意识能够回应的其他实相系统。我在此引用鲁柏和约瑟经历中的一件事，但读者可以自行关联，并发现能得出同样结论的类似事件。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 693
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: You may take a break. If you do not mind, after the break I will continue for a short while. I am trying very hard, Joseph, because dissociation will answer more than one of your particular problems, and practical ones at that. Associations which you may consider impractical are often very practical. I always feel when I speak along these lines as if I must say over and over that I do not intend any shortening of your working hours, only that you have more energy than you realize and that associations, as certainly you must see with your landlord, are often practical. Take your break.
quotes.translation: 你们可以休息一下。如果你不介意，休息后我会再继续一小会儿。约瑟，我非常努力，因为解离不仅能解决你几个特定问题，而且还能解决实际的问题。你可能认为不切实际的联想，往往非常实用。当我沿着这些思路说话时，我总觉得必须一遍又一遍地强调，我无意缩短你的工作时间，只是你拥有的能量比你意识到的要多，而且联想——就像你肯定必须与你的房东看到的那样——往往是实用的。休息吧。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Children often scare themselves. A variety of reasons exist for such behavior. People often choose to watch horror films for the same reason. Usually the body and mind are bored, and actually seek out dramatic stress. Under usual conditions the body is restored -- flushed out, so to speak -- through the release of hormones that have been withheld, often through repressive habits.
quotes.translation: 孩子们常常吓唬自己。这种行为存在多种原因。人们选择观看恐怖片也是出于同样的原因。通常，身体和心智感到无聊，实际上是在寻求戏剧性的压力。在正常情况下，身体会通过释放那些因压抑习惯而被抑制的荷尔蒙来恢复——可以说是被冲洗干净。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This data must be interpreted in such a way that the conscious mind then can give a prediction, say, but also in such a way that its reality exits simultaneously with consciousness, but never so replacing consciousness that the ego grows alarmed. This is often done by the use of symbols which are not immediately apparent to consciousness, and thus are allowed through. Then it is too late for the ego to close its gates against them. But the symbols chosen must be of a nature so that they will become meaningful to consciousness, to as large an extent as possible after emergence.
quotes.translation: 这些资料必须以某种方式被诠释，使得意识心能据此做出预测，同时又要让这些资料的真实性与意识并存，但绝不能取代意识，以免引发自我警觉。这通常通过使用意识无法立即察觉的象征符号来实现，从而让它们得以通过。等到自我想要关闭心门时，已经为时已晚。但所选用的象征符号必须具有这样的特性：在浮现之后，能尽可能地对意识产生意义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: With all due respect, your friend [the psychologist] is, with the best of intentions, barking up the wrong psychological tree. He is very enthusiastic about his value tests, and his enthusiasm is what is important. The nature of the subjective mind, however, will never open itself to such tests, which represent, more than anything else, a kind of mechanical psychology, as if you could break down human values to a kind of logical alphabet of psychic atoms and molecules. A good try (with humor), but representative of psychology's best attempt to make sense of a poor hypothesis.
quotes.translation: 恕我直言，你那位朋友（心理学家）虽然用心良苦，却找错了心理学的方向。他对自己的价值观测试充满热忱，而这份热忱才是关键所在。然而，主观心灵的本质永远不会向这类测试敞开大门——这些测试本质上代表了一种机械式的心理学，仿佛你能将人类价值观分解成某种心灵原子与分子的逻辑字母表。这是个不错的尝试（带着幽默），但也不过是心理学试图为一项糟糕假设赋予意义的最佳努力罢了。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Man's thoughts no longer seemed to have any effect upon nature because in his mind he saw himself apart from it. In an ambiguous fashion, while concentrating upon nature's exterior aspects in a very conscious manner, he still ended up denying the conscious powers of his own mind. He became blind to the connection between his thoughts and his physical environment and experience.
quotes.translation: 人类的思想似乎不再对自然产生任何影响，因为在他的心智中，他视自己与自然分离。他以一种模棱两可的方式，在极其有意识地专注于自然外在表象的同时，最终却否定了自己心智的意识力量。他对自己的思想与物理环境及经验之间的联系变得视而不见。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The body is, therefore, quite well equipped to deal with its physical stance in the physical world, and its defense systems are unerring in that respect. Your conscious mind, however, directs your temporal perception and interprets that perception, organizing it into mental patterns. The body, again, must depend upon those interpretations. The biological basis of all life is a loving, divine and cooperative one, and presupposes a safe physical stance from which any member of any species feels actively free to seek out its needs and to communicate with others of its kind.
quotes.translation: 因此，身体完全有能力应对其在物质世界中的物理处境，其防御系统在这方面是准确无误的。然而，你的意识心却主导着你对时间性感知的诠释，并将这种感知组织成心智模式。身体又必须依赖这些诠释。所有生命的生物学基础都是充满慈爱、神圣且合作的，它预设了一个安全的物理立足点，任何物种的成员都能从中积极自由地寻求自身需求，并与同类沟通。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

9:44 P.M. MONDAY

quotes.text: What they do is take quick glimpses of your plane--and hold in mind that the saucer or cigar shape seen on your planet is a bastard form having little relation to the structure as it is at home base. At a later date I may go into the inhabitants of that plane more thoroughly, but as it is I am not acquainted with them very much myself. There are so many things that you do not understand that I hope to explain to you. There are other things that you do not understand that I cannot explain to you, simply because they would be too alien now for your regular mode of thought.
quotes.translation: 他们所做的只是快速瞥一眼你们的层面——请记住，在你们星球上看到的飞碟或雪茄形状，是一种畸变形态，与它们在基地的结构几乎毫无关联。日后我或许会更深入地探讨那个层面的居民，但目前我自己对他们也了解不多。你们有太多不理解的事情，我希望向你们解释。还有一些你们不理解的事情，我无法向你们解释，仅仅是因为它们对你们当前的常规思维模式来说太过陌生了。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: (From the 22nd session for February 4, 1964:) I have never trusted the written word half as much as I trust the spoken word, and on your plane it is difficult to trust either. But I do not feel that I could be myself as easily by means of automatic writing, for example. I do not mind speaking through Ruburt's mouth -- somehow the sound of the words is rather pleasant. But seeing myself transformed more or less into plain black and white words on a sheet of paper seems dull and uninteresting. And I have always enjoyed conversation, which is the liveliest of all arts....
quotes.translation: （摘自1964年2月4日第22节课：）我对书面文字的信任从来不及对口头语言的一半，而在你们的层面上，两者都难以信任。但举例来说，我并不觉得通过自动书写就能轻松地做我自己。我不介意通过鲁柏的口来说话——不知怎的，话语的声音相当悦耳。但看到自己或多或少被转化成纸上平淡的黑白文字，就显得乏味无趣了。而我向来喜爱交谈，这是所有艺术中最生动活泼的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (However, from Session 14 [for January 8, 1964], there's other material that can be given here, as well as some that makes an interesting note at the end of this appendix.4 First, from my own note at 11:05: "Jane said that Seth was quite pleased with the new voice, and that she now knows what he's thinking sometimes, even though he doesn't relay it to or through her as part of a message."
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Two main possibilities come to mind. One, a continued lack of acceptance of the inner self could lead to worldwide catastrophe, as the ego runs wild. Two, the inner self, restrained and denied for too long a period, could explosively overwhelm the ego so that existence in the physical field was made most difficult, since the ego is equipped to handle such manipulation. ego domination must indeed be halted, but it must be replaced with a balance between the outer and inner selves. Other matters briefly mentioned this evening will be more thoroughly discussed then also.
quotes.translation: 脑海中浮现出两种主要可能性。其一，持续拒绝接纳内在自体可能导致全球性灾难，如同自我失控般疯狂。其二，长期受压抑与否认的内在自体可能爆发性地压倒自我，使得物质世界的存在变得极为困难——因为自我本应处理这类操控。自我支配确实必须被遏止，但必须以内外自体的平衡来取代。今晚简要提及的其他事项，届时也将更深入探讨。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It makes no difference basically, underlined, whether or not the sense data is real in your terms or not. If it is recorded and registered by the physical organism it becomes a part of, and an equal part of, memory. In the case of the odor smelled in a dream, the mind experiences, and then the experience is interpreted precisely as if the senses had been used to perceive it. It is then incorporated with other experience by the physical organism.
quotes.translation: 无论感官数据在你们看来是否真实，本质上毫无差别（强调）。只要被肉体组织记录并登记，它就成为记忆的一部分，且是同等重要的一部分。以梦中闻到的气味为例：心灵经历体验后，会如同感官真实感知般精确诠释该体验，随后由肉体组织将其与其他经验整合。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: (11:09.) Then, according to your beliefs, you deal with the physical dilemma as it is presented in those terms. You will react individually with your own purposes in mind. Your own unique and highly private beliefs help bring about the overall emotional condition. The pool of emotional energy into which your emotions flow is still composed of unalike charges, but generally speaking, the individual contribution of all those participating will fall into a coherent pattern that gives impetus and direction to the storm, providing the charge and the power behind it.
quotes.translation: （11:09）接着，根据你们的信念，你们会以那些术语所呈现的方式处理物质困境。你们将带着各自的目的做出个人反应。你们独特且高度私密的信念，共同促成整体情绪状态。情绪能量池中流动的仍是不同电荷，但总体而言，所有参与者的个人贡献会形成连贯模式，为风暴提供动力与方向，成为其背后的电荷与能量。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The mind can interpret the experiences that the legs and the feet have, however, and by imaginatively using that sensual data can perceive the ant's reality to some extent. Now when the mind races and runs, it sometimes has great difficulty interpreting its activities to the brain, which is usually concerned with other realities only to the extent that they impinge upon it.
quotes.translation: 然而，心灵能够诠释双腿与双脚的体验，通过想象运用这些感官数据，能在某种程度上感知蚂蚁的现实。当心灵疾驰奔涌时，它有时极难向大脑解释自身活动——大脑通常只关注那些冲击其现实的其他实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In its own way, a neighborhood fire serves the same purpose, among others, and so does a local or regional disaster. The nature of your conscious mind demands change and dramatic meaning, a sense of power, and aspirations against which to judge individual direction. A "perfect" society, idealistically speaking, would provide these qualities by encouraging each individual to use his potentials to the fullest, to revel in his challenges, and to be led on by his great natural excitement as he tries to extend powers of creative potency in his own unique way.
quotes.translation: 邻里火灾以自身方式达成相同目的，地方或区域灾难亦然。你们意识心的本质需要变化与戏剧性意义、力量感，以及用以衡量个人方向的抱负。理想而言，"完美"社会应通过鼓励每个人充分发挥潜能、沉醉于挑战、在独特方式中拓展创造力时被天然激情引领，来提供这些特质。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is not to say that you cannot understand the nature of the universe to some extent, but the answers lie in the natures of your own minds, in the processes of individual creativity, in studies that ask questions like: "Where did this thought come from? Where does it go? What effect does it have upon myself or others? How do I know how to dream, when I have never been taught to do so? How do I speak without understanding the mechanisms? Why do I feel that I have an eternal reality, when it is obvious that I was physically born and will physically die?"
quotes.translation: 这并非说你们无法在某种程度上理解宇宙本质，答案在于你们自身心灵的本性、个体创造力的过程，以及提出这类问题的研究："这个念头从何而来？去向何处？对自身或他人有何影响？我从未被教导如何做梦，为何知道如何做梦？在不理解机制的情况下如何说话？既然肉身出生且终将死亡，为何感觉拥有永恒实相？"
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Then you move out into a much larger and different kind of room, and here the furniture may be arranged in any fashion--arranged and rearranged to your hearts content, and you may form different combinations from it and use it for different purposes. So Peg is rearranging the furniture of her mind; and as you might visit a new residence and move some of your belongings into it before you have officially made it your own, so she has for some time been examining her new environment, and been in tie process of transferring her self to the new location.
quotes.translation: 接着你进入一个更大且截然不同的房间，这里的家具可以任意摆放——随心所欲地重新布置，你可以从中组合出不同的样式，并用于不同的目的。所以佩格正在重新布置她心灵的家具；就像你参观新居时，在正式将其据为己有之前，会先把一些物品搬进去一样，她这段时间也一直在审视自己的新环境，并逐步将自我转移到这个新位置。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I was about your establishment earlier, and Ruburt was right. There was a purpose in his misreading. Actually, subconsciously he picked up my thoughts on this occasion. He picked them up a few minutes earlier but his mind waited for a suitable occasion to transpose the word spacious in connection with the present. The spacious present is an excellent term. In actuality there is only a spacious present, so spacious that it cannot be explored all at once in your terms, hence your arbitrary division of it into larger rooms of past, present and future.
quotes.translation: 我早些时候曾到过你们的住处，鲁柏是对的。他误读是有目的的。实际上，他在潜意识层面捕捉到了我此刻的想法。他几分钟前就接收到了这些信息，但他的心智在等待合适的时机，将"广阔"这个词与"当下"联系起来。"广阔的当下"是个绝妙的术语。事实上，只存在一个广阔的当下，它如此浩瀚以至于无法用你们的时间概念一次性探索完毕，因此你们武断地将其分割成过去、现在和未来这些更大的房间。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: That is, dreams are the best preventative medicine. Some psychological difficulties need clear conscious light and understanding. Others, however, operate even without conscious participation, and those are often solved, or remedied, at the same level without interfering with the conscious mind. As the body handles many physical manipulations without your own conscious knowledge of what is being done, or how, so the workings of your own psychological systems often automatically solve "their own problems" through dreams of which you are not aware.
quotes.translation: 也就是说，梦境是最佳的预防药物。某些心理困扰需要清晰的意识之光与理解。但另一些困扰即便没有意识参与也会运作，而这些困扰往往能在不干扰意识心智的同一层面得到解决或疗愈。正如身体在你不自觉的情况下处理许多生理运作，你的心理系统也常通过你未曾察觉的梦境，自动解决"它们自身的问题"。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: （The following material is included here became it is dealt with in the next session, the 14th. I wrote these notes on the morning of January 8; they came to mind while I was working on an ink drawing of a complicated tree bearing within it two birds nests. I began the drawing in reference to an image Seth had conjured up in an earlier session on the fifth dimension. I believe these thoughts came to me so easily because I had been making some conscious efforts to think in new ways, and they were triggered by my working on this particular drawing.
quotes.translation: （以下资料收录于此，因将在下次第14节课程中讨论。我在1月8日早晨写下这些笔记；当时我正在创作一幅复杂的墨水画，画中一棵大树内藏着两个鸟巢。这幅画的灵感源于赛斯在早前关于第五维度的课程中勾勒的意象。我相信这些想法之所以如此轻易浮现，是因为我一直在有意识地尝试以新方式思考，而创作这幅特定画作恰好触发了它们。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Left alone, some of these people would die with a feeling of satisfaction. Kept alive through medical techniques, the physical mechanism continues its struggles to revitalize the body and bring about this second puberty -- that naturally would only come about under different conditions, with the mind far more alert and the will unimpaired. Now, to some extent (underlined) there is a connection between this innate, rarely observed second puberty and the development of cancer, in which growth is specifically apparent in an exaggerated manner.
quotes.translation: 若任其自然，这些人中有些会带着满足感离世。但通过医疗技术维持生命后，生理机制继续挣扎着恢复身体活力，试图引发这种第二次青春期——这本该在截然不同的条件下自然发生，那时心智将更为警觉，意志也完好无损。现在，在某种程度上（划线强调），这种与生俱来却鲜少显现的第二次青春期，与癌症的发展存在关联——后者以夸张的方式呈现出明显的生长迹象。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Previous limiting ideas, accepted, figuratively form a restraining bed, gathering other such material so that your mind becomes filled with debris. When you are spontaneous, you accept the free nature of your mind and it spontaneously makes decisions as to the validity or non-validity of data it receives. When you refuse to allow it this function it becomes cluttered.
quotes.translation: 被接受的过往限制性观念，形象地说会形成束缚之床，聚集其他类似材料，使你的心智充满碎片。当你处于自发状态时，你接纳心智的自由本性，它便会自发判断所接收信息的真伪。若你拒绝赋予它这项功能，心智便会变得杂乱无章。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
