quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: These multiple existences, however, are simultaneous and open-ended. In your terms the conscious mind is growing toward a realization of the part it has to play in such multidimensional reality. It is enough that you understand your part in this existence. When you fully comprehend that you form what you think of as your current reality, all else will fall into place.
quotes.translation: 这些多重存在同时发生且开放无终。用你们的话来说，意识心智正逐渐认识到它在多维实相中必须扮演的角色。只要你们理解自己在此次存在中的角色就已足够。当你们完全领悟到自己正在形成所谓的当前实相时，其余一切自会各归其位。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The mind is a system of checks and balances even as the body, and so often a set of beliefs that can be seen as highly negative will often serve beneficial ends in countering other beliefs. For some time Western civilization stressed a distorted version of intellectual reasoning, for example, and so the current stress about other portions of the self serves a purpose.
quotes.translation: 心智如同身体一样是一套制衡系统，因此那些看似高度负面的信念群，往往能通过制衡其他信念而达成有益目的。例如西方文明曾长期强调扭曲的理性思辨，因此当前对自我其他部分的关注自有其意义。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Also, in a manner of speaking, you are yourselves the ancient dreamers who dreamed your world into being. You must understand that I am not saying that you are passive, fleeting dreamers, lost in some divine mind, but that you are the unique creative manifestations of a divine intelligence whose creativity is responsible for all realities, which are themselves endowed with creative abilities of their own, with the potential and desire for fulfillment--inheritors indeed of the divine processes themselves. Spontaneity knows its own order.
quotes.translation: 此外，从某种意义上说，你们自身就是那些将世界梦入存在的古老做梦者。必须明白我并非说你们是被动无常的做梦者，迷失于某种神圣心智中，而是说你们是神圣智慧的独特创造性显化——这种智慧创造了一切实相，而这些实相本身又被赋予了各自的创造能力、实现潜能的渴望与动力——你们确实是神圣进程本身的继承者。自发性知晓自身的秩序。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They believe that diseases are the result of exterior conditions. Quite simply, their policy can be read: "You are what you eat." Some in this group also subscribe to philosophical ideas that somewhat moderate those concepts, recognizing the importance of the mind. Often though, some strong suggestions of a very negative character are given, so that all foods except certain accepted ones are seen as bad for the body, and the cause of diseases. People become afraid of the food they eat, and the field of eating then becomes the arena.
quotes.translation: 他们相信疾病源于外在条件。简而言之，其信条可解读为："你即你所食。"该群体中部分人也接受某些缓和这些概念的哲学思想，承认心智的重要性。但通常仍会给出极具负面暗示的强烈建议，以致除特定认可食物外，所有食物都被视为对身体有害并引发疾病。人们开始恐惧所食之物，饮食领域就此成为战场。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: They were particularly concerned in the beginning with developing a human being who would have built-in safeguards against violence. With them, the desire for peace was almost what you would call an instinct. There were changes in the physical mechanism. When the mind signaled strong aggression, the body would not react. Now psychologically you can see vestiges of this in certain individuals, who will faint, or even attack their own physical system, before allowing themselves to do what they think of as violence to another.
quotes.translation: 他们最初特别关注于发展一种具有内置暴力防护机制的人类。对他们而言，对和平的渴望近乎你们所谓的本能。生理机制发生了改变：当心智发出强烈攻击信号时，身体不会产生反应。如今你们在心理层面仍可见其痕迹——某些个体在允许自己对他人施加所谓暴力之前，会先晕厥甚至攻击自身生理系统。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (11:13.) There is more, but it will have to wait simply because it is not presently translatable. According to the impact of this session, your own comprehensions and perceptions will bring other clues, either in the waking or the dream state. Keep your minds open for them, but without any preconceived ideas of how they might appear. Ruburt's own development triggers certain psychic activity that then triggers further growth. He has been participating in his library, for example, whether or not he is always aware of it.
quotes.translation: （11点13分）还有更多内容，但必须暂缓，只因目前尚无法转译。根据此节课的冲击力，你们自身的理解与感知将在清醒或梦境状态带来其他线索。请保持心智开放迎接它们，但不要预设它们可能以何种形式显现。鲁柏自身的发展会触发特定心灵活动，继而引发进一步成长。例如他一直在图书馆中参与运作，无论他是否始终意识到这一点。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 818, FEBRUARY 6, 1978
10:19 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: We come here also to one of the other causes of the dreaming state, beside those of which we have spoken in the past. The mind, of itself and separated from the ego, must still be action, and therefore never still. Since action of any kind, being composed of inner vitality, must seek materialization, the dreams become the constructions of that dream universe of which, again, we have spoken. But action can never complete itself. The dream once begun continues, and the dream universe itself forms anew other constructions.
quotes.translation: 我们在此也触及了梦境状态的另一个成因，除了我们过去所讨论的那些之外。心智本身，当它与自我分离时，仍然必须是行动，因此永不停歇。由于任何形式的行动，由内在活力所组成，必然寻求物质化，梦境便成为那个梦宇宙的建构——我们同样曾提及这个宇宙。但行动永远无法完成自身。梦境一旦开始便持续下去，而梦宇宙本身又形成新的建构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: I will have more to say, per usual. I suggest your break, and keep in mind that I will speak more on the construction of the entity, the tree analogy being a good one, because all of our imaginary seeds do not develop into trees. This does not necessarily mean, either, that there is a deficiency; merely that the consciousness involved does not choose for one reason or another to materialize fully in a particular form, or to develop any given abilities along certain lines. This may also represent a needed resting point.
quotes.translation: 我照例还有更多要说的。我建议你们休息一下，并记住我将进一步谈论存有的建构，树木的比喻很恰当，因为我们所有想象中的种子并非都能长成树木。这也不一定意味着存在缺陷；只是所涉及的意识出于某种原因，选择不完全以特定形式物质化，或沿着某些路线发展任何特定能力。这也可能代表一个必要的休憩点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Often, of course, those who try the hardest to be "good" do so because they fear for their basic worth, and those who speak of having youthful minds and bodies do so because they are so terrified of age. In the same way, many who shout about independence are afraid that they are basically helpless. In most instances these opposing beliefs are held quite consciously, but kept apart from each other. Therefore they are not reconciled.
quotes.translation: 当然，那些最努力试图成为"好人"的人，往往是因为他们害怕自己基本的价值；而那些谈论拥有年轻心智和身体的人，则是因为他们对年龄如此恐惧。同样地，许多高喊独立的人，其实害怕自己根本是无助的。在大多数情况下，这些对立的信念是被相当有意识地持有，但却彼此分离。因此它们并未得到调和。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: He does all the things and says all the things that Augustus One would dearly love to do and say, with only certain safeguards. Augustus One, however, is not literally unconscious during this time, but quite aware of the "vicarious" activities and fulfillments. Again, it is a game of hide-and-seek, in which the so-called unconscious mind is relatively innocent.
quotes.translation: 他做了所有奥古斯都一号极其渴望做和说的事情与话语，仅带有某些保障。然而，奥古斯都一号在这段时间并非字面意义上的无意识，而是相当清楚这些"替代性"的活动与满足。再次强调，这是一场捉迷藏的游戏，其中所谓的无意识心智相对来说是清白的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You must be aware of the contents of your own reasoning mind. Find the ambiguities. Regardless of the nature of your beliefs they are indeed made flesh and material. The miracle of your being cannot escape itself. Your thoughts blossom into events. If you think the world is evil, you will meet with events that seem evil. There are no accidents in cosmic terms, or in terms of the world as you know it. Your beliefs grow as surely in time and space as flowers do. When you realize this you can even feel their growing.
quotes.translation: 你必须觉察自己理性心智的内容。找出其中的模糊之处。无论你信念的本质为何，它们确实化为了血肉与物质。你存在的奇迹无法逃避自身。你的思想绽放为事件。如果你认为世界是邪恶的，你就会遇到看似邪恶的事件。在宇宙的层面上，或在你所认知的世界层面上，没有意外。你的信念在时空中的生长，正如花朵般确凿。当你意识到这一点时，你甚至能感受到它们的生长。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: （When I was a few car lengths from the light, the dream suddenly popped into my mind. Just as in the dream, I found myself stopping for a red light. The effect was so startling that I at once began to wonder whether it was possible to act out dream suggestions in waking life. My self questioning was particularly intriguing because I could have chosen from a half-dozen routes to work; yet I had picked a course that enabled me to act out the dream of the night before, while not being consciously aware of the dream.）
quotes.translation: （当我距离红绿灯还有几辆车远时，那个梦突然浮现在我的脑海中。正如在梦中一样，我发现自己正为红灯停下。这种效果如此惊人，以至于我立刻开始怀疑，是否有可能在清醒生活中将梦中的暗示付诸行动。我的自我质疑尤其引人入胜，因为我本可以从六条上班路线中选择；然而我却选择了一条让我能够演绎前一晚梦境的路线，同时并未有意识地意识到那个梦。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: You with your conscious mind are to discriminate among those thoughts as to which ones you want to form into your system of beliefs (intently), but in so doing you are not to pretend blindness. You may at times wish that a rainy day were a sunny one, but you do not stand at the window and deny that the rain is falling, or that the air is cold and the sky dark.
quotes.translation: 你须以有意识心智分辨那些念头，决定哪些要纳入你的信念体系（专注地），但这样做时不可假装视而不见。你有时或许希望雨天放晴，但不会站在窗前否认雨在下、空气寒冷、天色阴沉。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Obviously, the mind can use its reasoning abilities, for example, to come to the conclusion that there is a single god behind the functioning of the world, that there are many gods, that divinity is a fantasy, and that the world itself springs from no reasonable source. New sentence: Like statistics, the reasoning abilities can be used to come to almost any conclusion. This is done, again, by taking into consideration within any given system of reasoning only the evidence that agrees with the system's premises.
quotes.translation: 显然，心智可运用其推理能力，例如得出结论：世界运作背后有一位神、有许多神、神性是幻想、世界本身源于无理性之源。新句：如同统计学，推理能力可被用来得出几乎任何结论。这同样是通过在特定推理体系内只采纳符合其前提的证据来实现的。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: It is true that the conscious mind must be relaxed to some extent, and that a state of apparent dissociation is necessary. Now, believe it or not, this will not always necessarily be the case. You can have two doors open at once. You can listen to two channels at once. But until you learn to focus in two directions, and this is simplified to a pathetic degree, in the meantime you simply turn down the volume of the first channel, while you attract your attention to the second. This process you call dissociation.
quotes.translation: 确实，有意识心智必须在一定程度上放松，且需要一种明显的解离状态。信不信由你，这并非总是必然。你可以同时打开两扇门，同时收听两个频道。但直到你学会同时聚焦两个方向——这已被简化到可悲的程度——在此期间你只需调低第一个频道的音量，同时将注意力转向第二个频道。这个过程你称之为解离。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: "It should remind you of the true effortlessness that is in a fashion responsible for your very existence. When you become overly concerned or worried in any area, remember that you are thinking those thoughts while the process of thinking is utterly effortless. That realization alone can further remind you that the conscious mind does not have to have all (underlined) the information required. It only needs to have the faith that means are available--even if those means are beyond its own scope of activity."
quotes.translation: "这应提醒你那种真正的毫不费力，它在某种程度上造就了你的存在本身。当你在任何领域过度担忧或焦虑时，记住你正在思考这些念头，而思考过程本身毫不费力。单凭这一认知就能进一步提醒你：有意识心智不必拥有所有（划线强调）所需信息。它只需相信方法存在——即便那些方法超出其自身活动范围。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now it is here that the seeming division in the self occurs, for in physical life the conscious mind must be connected with the brain, and in terms of time that organ itself must grow and develop. So all of your consciousness cannot be physically aware. The portion that must "wait for" the brain's development is the part you call in life "the conscious mind."
quotes.translation: 正是在这里，自我中看似分裂的部分出现了，因为在物质生命中，有意识心智必须与大脑相连，而就时间而言，这个器官本身必须成长发展。所以你的全部意识无法在物质层面被感知。必须"等待"大脑发展的那部分，就是你此生称为"有意识心智"的部分。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In the natural body-mind relationship the sleep state operates as a great connector, an interpreter, allowing the free flow of conscious and unconscious material. In the kind of sleep patterns suggested, optimum conditions are set up. Neurosis and psychosis simply would not occur under such conditions. And in the natural back-and-forth leeway of the system, exterior dilemmas or problems are worked out in the dream situation, and interior difficulty may also be solved symbolically through physical experience.
quotes.translation: 在自然的身心关系中，睡眠状态充当着伟大的连接者与诠释者，允许意识与无意识材料自由流动。在建议的睡眠模式中，最佳条件得以建立。神经症与精神病在此条件下根本不会发生。而在系统自然的往返余地中，外在困境或问题在梦境情境中得到解决，内在困难也可能通过物质经验以象征方式化解。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ruburt's state of mind was in correspondence with your own state of mind, even as you are in some kind of correspondence with your old environment, so in these cases you have a free flow of information at other levels.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏的心境与你的心境相互呼应，正如你与旧环境存在某种呼应一般，因此在这些情况下，你们在其他层面拥有自由的信息流动。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: The early acquiescence to beliefs has a biological importance, therefore, but as the conscious mind attains its maturity it is also natural for it to question those beliefs, and to assess them in relation to its own environment. Many of my readers may have certain ideas about good and evil that are very hampering. These may be old beliefs in new clothing. You may think that you are quite free, only to discover that you hold old ideas but have simply put new terms to them, or concentrated upon other aspects.
quotes.translation: 早期对信念的顺从具有生物学上的重要性，但当意识心智达到成熟时，质疑这些信念并根据自身环境评估它们也是自然的。我的许多读者可能对善恶持有某些非常束缚的观念。这些可能是披着新外衣的旧信念。你可能以为自己相当自由，结果却发现你只是给旧观念换了新说法，或者专注于其他方面。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There are gradations and fluctuations within its activity. It is far more flexible than you give it credit for. (Pause.) The ego can use the conscious mind almost entirely as a way of perceiving external or internal realities that coincide with its own beliefs. It is not that certain answers do not lie openly accessible, therefore, but that often you have set yourself on a course of action in which you believe, and you do not want to open yourself to any material that may contradict your current beliefs.
quotes.translation: 在其活动中有等级和波动。它比你认为的要灵活得多。（停顿）自我几乎可以完全将意识心智用作感知与其自身信念一致的内外现实的方式。因此，并非某些答案无法公开获取，而是你常常已经设定了一条你深信不疑的行动路线，不愿向任何可能与你当前信念相矛盾的材料敞开自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Experience is the product of the mind, the spirit, conscious thoughts and feelings, and unconscious thoughts and feelings. These together form the reality that you know. You are hardly at the mercy of a reality, therefore, that exists apart from yourself, or is thrust upon you. You are so intimately connected with the physical events composing your life experience that often you cannot distinguish between the seemingly material occurrences and the thoughts, expectations and desires that gave them birth.
quotes.translation: 经验是心智、灵性、有意识的思想和感受，以及无意识的思想和感受的产物。这些共同构成了你所知的现实。因此，你几乎不会受制于一个独立于你之外或强加于你的现实。你与构成你生活经历的物理事件如此紧密相连，以至于你常常无法区分看似物质的事件与赋予它们诞生的思想、期望和欲望。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: They represent, however, deep unities that you do not understand. Your conception of good and evil results in large part from the kind of consciousness you have presently adopted. You do not perceive wholes, but portions. The conscious mind focuses with a quick, limited, but intense light, perceiving from a given field of reality only certain "stimuli." It then puts these stimuli together, forming the liaison of similarity. Anything that it does not accept as a portion of reality, it does not perceive.
quotes.translation: 然而，它们代表着你所不理解的内在统一。你对善恶的观念很大程度上源于你目前所采用的意识类型。你感知不到整体，只能感知部分。意识心智以快速、有限但强烈的光线聚焦，从给定的现实领域中只感知某些“刺激”。然后它将这些刺激组合起来，形成相似性的联系。任何它不认为是现实一部分的东西，它都不会感知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (All more intently:) I hope that this book to some extent or another puts each of you in touch with your own inner psychological motion, your creative breath, so that you are invigorated and sense within your own minds and spirit a new promise, a new intent, and the exhilaration of earthly and spiritual strength. You dwell in a state of natural grace that is quite alive and vital whether or not science decrees that consciousness possesses its own intent. Nature is supernatural all the while, of course.
quotes.translation: （更加专注地：）我希望这本书在某种程度上能让你们每个人与自己的内在心理运动、创造性的呼吸相连，从而使你们充满活力，并在自己的心智和灵性中感受到新的承诺、新的意图，以及尘世与灵性力量带来的振奋。你们处于一种自然恩宠的状态，无论科学是否认定意识拥有自己的意图，这种状态都充满生机与活力。当然，自然始终是超自然的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Material like this is sifted through many layers of subconscious conceptions, and is subsequently colored. People strongly believing in your organized religions color the material in a manner that is highly disadvantageous, and that unfortunately often adds to existing superstitions. Ruburt's mind, believe it or not, is much like my own; though, if you'll forgive me, in a very limited fashion, therefore the distortions are much less distortive, much less harmful, and more easily discovered and cleared.
quotes.translation: 这类资料会经过潜意识概念的层层筛选，随后被着色。强烈信奉你们组织化宗教的人会以极为不利的方式着色这些资料，不幸的是这往往加剧了既有的迷信。信不信由你，鲁柏的心智与我的非常相似；不过，请恕我直言，是以极为有限的方式，因此扭曲的程度小得多，危害也小得多，更容易被发现和清除。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It is unfortunate that I must use terms of time to explain all of this to you, but as I told you many sessions ago, my time is not your time (humorously) ... Because you ask specific questions at a specific session, it does not necessarily mean that the program has not been prepared, in your terms, earlier. For on many occasions I will see the questions within your mind, or the minds of your witnesses, and [again in your terms] will therefore answer them ahead of time.
quotes.translation: 不幸的是，我必须使用时间术语向你们解释这一切，但正如我许多节课前告诉你们的，我的时间不是你们的时间（幽默地）……因为你们在特定的一节课上提出特定问题，这并不一定意味着计划没有在你们的时间概念里更早准备。因为在许多场合，我会看到你们心智中的问题，或你们见证人心智中的问题，并（再次用你们的时间概念）提前回答它们。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (However, from Session 14 [for January 8, 1964], there's other material that can be given here, as well as some that makes an interesting note at the end of this appendix.4 First, from my own note at 11:05: "Jane said that Seth was quite pleased with the new voice, and that she now knows what he's thinking sometimes, even though he doesn't relay it to or through her as part of a message."
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: (Slowly:) You have not learned to use your consciousness properly or fully, so that it seems that imagination, emotions and reasoning are separate faculties, or sometimes set against each other. The mature conscious mind, once more, accepts data from the exterior world and from the interior one. It is only when you believe that consciousness must be attuned only to exterior conditions that you force it to cut itself off from inner knowledge, intuitional "voices," and the depths from which it springs.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）你们尚未学会正确或充分地运用意识，以至于想象力、情感和理性看似是分离的能力，有时甚至相互对立。成熟的意识心智再次接纳来自外在世界和内在世界的资料。只有当你们相信意识必须只与外在条件调准时，你们才迫使它切断与内在知识、直觉“声音”及其所源起的深层的联系。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ideas form reality, so the body is used to reacting to some "imaginary" situations in which, for example, the mind conjures up dire circumstances which do not physically exist; but these still force the organism into an over-activation, setting up a state of stress. In massive drug therapy the body feels in greatest threat, for it is forced to use all of its resources while its own signals tell it that the messages it is getting do not have a correlation -- and yet they are of the most urgent nature.
quotes.translation: 观念形成实相，因此身体被用来对某些“想象”情境做出反应，例如心智构想出并不实际存在的可怕境况；但这些仍迫使有机体过度激活，建立起压力状态。在大规模药物治疗中，身体感受到最大的威胁，因为它被迫动用所有资源，而它自身的信号告诉它，它所接收的信息没有关联性——然而这些信息却具有最紧迫的性质。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Being your own natural and magical self when you dream, you utilize information that is outside of the time context experienced by the so-called rational mind. The creative abilities operate in the same fashion, appearing within consecutive time, but with the main work done outside of it entirely. When you finished your project,4 you had several days of feeling miserable, but you caught yourself and turned yourself around beautifully, and you have every right to congratulate yourself in that regard.
quotes.translation: 当你们做梦时，作为你们自然且神奇的自性，你们运用了所谓理性心智所经历的时间脉络之外的信息。创造能力以同样的方式运作，出现在连续时间内，但主要工作完全在时间之外完成。当你们完成你们的项目后，你们有几天感到痛苦，但你们抓住了自己，漂亮地扭转了局面，在这方面你们完全有权利称赞自己。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You consciously give the signals for reaction. It is not the other way around. Past events do not intrude in this manner unless they are beckoned by the conscious expectations and thoughts that exist within your mind. (Pause.) Those unconscious memories will be activated according to your current beliefs. You will be replenished and renewed as your thoughts motivate joyful body sensations and physical events, or you will be depressed as you bring into your awareness unpleasant past body happenings.
quotes.translation: 你们有意识地发出反应的信号。情况并非相反。过去的事件不会以这种方式侵入，除非它们被你们心智中存在的有意识的期望和思想所召唤。（停顿。）那些无意识记忆将根据你们当前的信念被激活。当你们的思想激发愉悦的身体感觉和物理事件时，你们将得到补充和更新；或者当你们将不愉快的过去身体事件带入觉知时，你们将感到沮丧。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When you enter a different dimension the abilities of the body form change, and for all intents and purposes it is a different body form, which we will call a mind form. It still seems physical in shape, but you can walk through physical matter with it, and you can truly levitate with it within your solar system, but you cannot go beyond in this mind form.
quotes.translation: 当你进入不同维度时，身体形态的能力会改变，实际上那是一种不同的身体形态，我们称之为心智形态。它在外形上仍看似物质性的，但你可以用它穿透物质实体，也能在太阳系内真正悬浮，但无法以这种心智形态超越太阳系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: I repeat: Your ideas and beliefs form the structure of your experience. Your beliefs and the reasons for them can be found in your conscious mind. If you accept the idea that the reasons for your behavior are forever buried in the past of this life, or any other, then you will not be able to alter your experience until you change that belief. I am speaking now of more or less normal experience. Later we will discuss more particular areas, such as circumstances in which illnesses date from birth.
quotes.translation: 我重申：你的观念与信念构成了你经验的架构。你的信念及其成因都能在意识心找到。若你接受"行为动机永远埋藏在此生或任何前世的过往"这种观念，那么除非改变这个信念，否则你无法改变经验。我现在谈论的是大致正常的经验范畴，日后我们会探讨更特定的领域，例如自出生即存在的疾病状况。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (Questions, of course, instantly come to mind. Who was in the classroom, Seth or me? One possibility here is that I do the projecting while Seth controls the physical body and uses the vocal cords to speak and to give verbal expression. I would be the perceptive mechanism in the other location while he would translate the perceptions and communicate them in physical terms. [In a usual out-of-body state it would be highly difficult for me, alone, to communicate my perceptions until I returned. ]
quotes.translation: （问题自然立刻浮现心头：教室里的是赛斯还是我？一种可能是：我进行投射时，赛斯控制着肉体并运用声带说话表达。我在另一处充当感知机制，而他则翻译这些感知并以物质语言传达。[在通常的离体状态下，若仅凭我独自一人，必须等到返回才能传达感知，这极为困难。]）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: May 23rd will represent another, and perhaps the last crisis as far as Miss Callahan is concerned. I would advise Mark to go ahead with his plans to find an apartment, but to look over all aspects of any particular apartment that he has in mind, foreseeing difficulties of a temperamental rather than practical nature with the landlord. This would have nothing to do with practical arrangements, but would rather be a more or less mutual antagonism that would rise up between them in a little time.
quotes.translation: 就卡拉汉小姐而言，5月23日将代表另一次危机，或许也是最后一次。我建议马克继续执行寻找公寓的计划，但需仔细审视他心中特定公寓的所有面向，预见与房东之间属于性情冲突而非实际层面的困难。这与实际安排无关，而是指他们之间不久后会产生的某种相互对立。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: When Ruburt replaces the bed sheets, I suggest colored ones. As he goes to sleep this evening, have him try imagining a scene in springtime, with him walking briskly or running. When he walks now on the streets, he should keep the mental image just given in his mind, picturing the whole flexible and fleet image, and the inner self will take steps to see that the suitable adjustments are made. He should remember to give frequent suggestions pertaining to relaxation. This is extremely important.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏更换床单时，我建议选用彩色床单。今晚入睡时，让他尝试想象春日场景，自己正轻快行走或奔跑。现在他走在街上时，应保持刚才给予的心像，想象那灵活敏捷的完整画面，内在自我自会采取步骤促成适当调整。他需记得频繁给予放松相关的暗示，这点极为重要。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Your enthusiasm is excellent. However, your enthusiasm can cause waves that can go up and down. You want a quiet surface. It would help you if before bed you imagine your mind like an ocean upon which you could walk. All feelings are on the waters, you are above. Then imagine yourself walking on the water, listening with your inner ear and using inner eyes. You should hear voices and see a figure on the horizon... let the figure come closer... Do not run. Relax and let the figure come to you.
quotes.translation: 你的热忱极佳，但这份热忱可能引发起伏不定的波浪。你需要平静的水面。若能在睡前想象你的心智如同可行走其上的海洋，所有感受都浮于水面而你在其上，这将有所助益。接着想象自己行走于水面，用内在耳朵倾听、内在眼睛观看。你应听见声音，看见地平线上的人影……让人影靠近……不要奔跑。放松，让人影自行走向你。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The reasoning mind is highly necessary, effective, and suitable for physical existence, and for the utilization of free will, which is very dependent upon perception of clearly distinguishable actions. In the larger framework of existence, however, it is simply one of innumerable methods of organizing data. A psychological filing system, if you prefer.
quotes.translation: 推理心智对于物质存在、以及对于自由意志的运用而言，是高度必要、有效且合适的——自由意志非常依赖于对清晰可辨行动的感知。然而在更广大的存在架构中，它仅是组织数据的无数方法之一。若你愿意，可称之为一种心理归档系统。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This is what the ego does when it reacts too violently to purely physical data on your plane. As a result it stiffens and you have, my well-meaning friend, the cold detachment with which you have faced the world. I do not want to digress here. I have certain points in mind for this evening. Nevertheless lest Ruburt thinks he is getting off scot-free, let me remind him that the tree's bark is quite necessary, cannot be dispensed with--but I will get into that and into Ruburt at a later time.
quotes.translation: 当自我对你层面上的纯粹物质数据反应过于激烈时，就会发生这种情况。结果它变得僵化，于是你——我善意的朋友——便以那种冷漠疏离的态度面对世界。我不想在此离题。今晚我有几个要点要谈。不过，免得鲁柏以为他能轻易脱身，让我提醒他：树皮是相当必要的，不可或缺——但我稍后会深入探讨这一点，也会谈到鲁柏。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: It was at this point that the nature of the conscious mind itself became so misunderstood, and those unrecognized or denied powers were assigned to unconscious portions of the self by ensuing schools of psychology. (With emphasis:) Very natural functions of the conscious mind, therefore, were assigned to the "underground" and cut off from normal use.
quotes.translation: 正是在这一点上，意识心智的本质遭到了严重误解，而后续的心理学派将那些未被认识或否认的能力，归因于自我的无意识部分。（强调地：）因此，意识心智非常自然的功能被划归到"地下"，与正常使用隔绝。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This could be studied to some degree if proper suggestions were given to the individual that he would awaken at the exact point when a dream ends. The dream state and dream conditions could also be studied quite legitimately, and to more purpose, using hypnosis. Here you are working with the mind itself as your material, and merely suggesting that it operate in a certain fashion. You are not tampering with the mechanics of its operation, and therefore automatically altering the conditions.
quotes.translation: 若能给予个体恰当暗示，使其在梦境恰好结束时醒来，便可对此进行一定程度的研究。梦境状态与梦境条件也可通过催眠进行相当合理且更有成效的研究。此时你以心智本身为素材，仅暗示它以特定方式运作，而非篡改其运作机制，从而自动改变条件。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: There are verbal difficulties having to do with the definition of life. Because of the psychological strength of preconceived notions, I have to work around many of your concepts. Your own kind of conscious mind is splendid and unique. It causes you, however, to interpret all other kinds of life according to your own specifications and experiences.
quotes.translation: 关于生命的定义存在语言上的困难。由于先入为主观念的心理力量，我不得不绕开你们的许多概念。你们这类意识心智确实辉煌而独特。然而，它却导致你们按照自身的规格与经验，去诠释所有其他形式的生命。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 705)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The inner state of each individual mind is projected outward. The inner state causes chemical changes in the physical body. Chemicals are thrown outward into the atmosphere. Hormones are released of particular varieties. Very definite and unique electrical changes occur in the skin, which change on a mass level the atmosphere at any given unit.
quotes.translation: 每个个体心智的内在状态向外投射。内在状态引起肉体的化学变化。化学物质被释放到大气中。特定种类的激素被分泌出来。皮肤上发生非常明确且独特的电性变化，这些变化在集体层面上改变着任何给定单位的大气。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: I also want to stress here that all aspects of life experience not only sensations but emotional feelings. Therefore, there is a kind of innate gallantry that operates among all segments of life -- a gallantry that deserves your respect and consideration. You should have respect, then, for the cells of your body, the thoughts of your mind (pause), and try to understand that even the smallest of creatures shares with you the emotional experience of life's triumphs and vulnerabilities.
quotes.translation: 我也要在此强调，生命经验的各个面向不仅包括感官感受，更包含情感体验。因此，生命各层次间存在着一种与生俱来的勇毅——这种勇毅值得你尊重与考量。你应当尊重自己身体的细胞、心智的念头（停顿），并试着理解：即使最微小的生物，也与你共享着生命胜利与脆弱的情绪体验。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (I talked about the first session for Jane's symptoms -- the private portion of Session 208, for November 15, 1965 -- yet she said that for her the whole thing really started the day before we went to a party in June 1966 regarding How to Develop Your ESP Power. Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: The thinking mind to a large degree directs the activity of great spontaneous forces, [with] energy-cellular organization being, say, the captain (pause) of the body's great energy sources. The reasoning mind defines, makes judgments, deals with the physical objects of the world, and also with the cultural interpretations current in its time.
quotes.translation: 思维心智在很大程度上引导着巨大自发力量的活动，而能量-细胞组织好比（停顿）身体庞大能量源的船长。理性心智进行界定、判断，处理世界上的物质客体，也处理其时代流传的文化诠释。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: The inner senses, though I have in the past described them by separating their functions and characteristics, basically operate together in such a way that in your terms it would be highly difficult to separate one from the others. They function with a perfect spontaneous order, aware of all synchronicities. In that psychological universe, then, it is possible for entities "to be everywhere at once," aware of everything at once. Your world is composed of such "entities"--the units of consciousness that form your body. The kinds of conscious minds that you have cannot hold that kind of information.
quotes.translation: 内我感官——虽然我过去曾通过区分其功能与特性来描述它们——本质上是以整体运作的方式存在，以致在你看来极难将其中任一感官与其他感官分离。它们以完美的自发秩序运作，觉察所有共时性。在那个心理宇宙中，实体得以"同时存在于各处"，同时知晓一切。你的世界正是由这些"实体"——构成你身体的意识单位——所组成。你们所拥有的那种意识心智无法承载这类信息。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They also underestimate dream events, for many associations in your present are the result of events which have occurred in the dream state. The mind is hardly shut off when you dream. It continues to use its associative processes. Therefore any given personal association may originate from a dream event as well as from a past waking event.
quotes.translation: 他们也低估了梦境事件，因为你当下的许多联想都源于梦境状态中发生的事件。当你做梦时，心智并未关闭。它持续运用其联想过程。因此，任何特定的个人联想可能源自梦境事件，也可能源自过去的清醒事件。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: I do not bring about the trance state in the manner of which you are speaking. Ruburt switches another channel on, through which my essence can enter more readily. There is the problem here of my entry also into your conscious minds. This does certainly involve a looking inward on Ruburt's part, but it is not self-hypnosis in the terms usually spoken of, merely a focusing upon an objective inner stimulus in much the same manner that you focus upon an outside camouflage stimulus.
quotes.translation: 我并非以你所说的那种方式引发出神状态。鲁柏切换了另一个频道，使我的本质能更顺畅地进入。这里存在我进入你们意识心智的问题。这确实涉及鲁柏的内在观照，但并非通常所说的自我催眠，而只是聚焦于一个客观的内在刺激，其方式与你聚焦于外在伪装刺激极为相似。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: As you read this book, the words are meant to release your own intuitional abilities. When you are reading it, your own dreams will give you added information and will be in your mind upon awakening if you are alert for them. There is no such simple end to the life that you know, [such] as the story of heaven. There is the freedom to understand your own reality, to develop your abilities further, and to feel more deeply the nature of your own existence as a part of All That Is.
quotes.translation: 当你阅读本书时，这些文字旨在释放你自身的直觉能力。当你阅读时，你自己的梦境会提供额外信息，若你保持警觉，醒来时这些信息便会浮现于你心中。你所知的生命并非如天堂故事那般有简单的终点。你拥有自由去理解自己的实相，进一步发展自身能力，并更深刻地感受自己作为一切万有一部分的存在本质。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You may perceive the thought patterns as quickly flashing sentences or words that are usually seen within your mind or within the other mind, or as black letters that form words. Or you may hear the words and thoughts being expressed, or you may see the earlier mentioned "landscape" in which the thoughts symbolically form into a picture.
quotes.translation: 你可能将思维模式感知为快速闪现的句子或词语，这些通常在你或他人的心智中被看见，或表现为形成词语的黑色字母。或者你可能听见这些词语和思维被表达出来，也可能看见先前提到的"景观"，其中思维象征性地形成一幅图画。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are specific functions brought into operation quite naturally that are scarcely perceived by your scientists, much less understood. As the mind within the body clearly sees its earthly time coming to an end, mental and psychic accelerations take place. These are in many ways like adolescent experiences in their great bursts of creative activity, with the resulting formation of questions, and the preparation for a completely new kind of personality growth and fulfillment.
quotes.translation: 有些特定功能自然而然地运作起来，却几乎不被你们的科学家察觉，更不用说理解了。当身体内的心智清晰看到其尘世时光即将结束时，心理和灵性的加速便发生了。这些在许多方面类似于青春期的经历，伴随着创造活动的巨大爆发，从而形成问题，并为一种全新的人格成长和实现做准备。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: As you mature and gain in knowledge, you do not obviously grow fatter; in other words, these qualities take up no space. They are not even visible in your space. Physical growth exists in terms of your sense of continuity, and therefore is projected into space and time. The evolving mind takes up no space. The personality takes up no space. You cannot look at it, or feel it. You can merely see its results.
quotes.translation: 随着你成熟并增长知识，你显然不会变得更胖；换句话说，这些品质不占据任何空间。它们甚至在你的空间中不可见。物理成长存在于你连续性的感知中，因此被投射到空间和时间里。进化中的心智不占据空间。人格不占据空间。你无法看见它，也无法触摸它。你只能看到它的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Now I found intermission highly amusing. The conditions of which I have been speaking are one thing. As far as the effect of weather upon the Moods of individuals, we do have something else. For the weather is created by you, on a subconscious level. The weather, at any given time, is a direct physical interpretation of the inner mass mind. You do indeed react, but you have already created the conditions, you see, and you then react to these in both psychic and physical ways.
quotes.translation: 现在我觉得间歇非常有趣。我一直在谈论的情况是一回事。至于天气对个人情绪的影响，我们确实有另一回事。因为天气是由你在潜意识层面创造的。在任何特定时间，天气都是内在集体心智的直接物理诠释。你确实会做出反应，但你已经创造了这些条件，你明白吗，然后你以心理和物理两种方式对这些条件做出反应。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Instinctively you leave your body for varying amounts of time each night while you sleep, but those journeys are not "programmed." You plan your own tours, in other words. As many people with the same interests may decide to visit the same country together, on tour, so in the out-of-body condition you may travel alone or with companions. If you are alert you may even take snapshots -- only as far as inner tours are concerned, the snapshots consist of clear pictures of the environment taken at the time, developed in the unconscious, and then presented to the waking mind.
quotes.translation: 你本能地每晚在睡眠中离开身体一段时间，但这些旅程并非"预设"。换句话说，你计划自己的旅行。就像许多有相同兴趣的人可能决定一起前往同一个国家旅行一样，在出体状态下，你可以独自旅行或与同伴同行。如果你保持警觉，你甚至可以拍下快照——只是就内在旅行而言，这些快照由当时环境的清晰画面组成，在潜意识中显影，然后呈现给清醒的心智。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 710
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Deliberately he decides to create a sculpture, and automatically focuses his energies in that direction. When you form the living sculpture of your body, which is far more important to you than any work of art, you should certainly follow the same course. In other words, direct your energies toward the creation of a healthy functioning body. You form your image constantly; as many of the artistic processes are hidden, so the inner mechanisms by which you create your material self lie beneath the surface of your conscious mind. They are highly effective, nevertheless.
quotes.translation: 他刻意决定创作一件雕塑，并自动将能量聚焦于那个方向。当你塑造你身体这座活的雕塑时——这对你来说远比任何艺术品重要——你当然应该遵循同样的过程。换句话说，将你的能量导向创造一个健康运作的身体。你不断地形成你的形象；正如许多艺术过程是隐藏的，你创造物质自我的内在机制也位于你意识心智的表面之下。然而，它们非常有效。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: As breathing is carried on in a manner that seems automatic to the conscious mind, so this important function of transforming the vitality of the universe into pattern units also seems to be carried on automatically. But this transformation is not as apparent to the one part of yourself that you are pleased to recognize, and therefore it seems as if this transformation is carried on by someone even more distant and alien than the unrecognized part of yourself that breathes.
quotes.translation: 正如呼吸以看似自动的方式进行于意识心智，这种将宇宙活力转化为模式单元的重要功能似乎也自动进行着。但这种转化对你乐于承认的那部分自己而言并不明显，因此它仿佛是由比那个未被承认的呼吸部分更遥远陌生的存在所执行。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Ideas represent this sort of construction, and can actually be handed down generation through generation, given a strong enough sendoff by any given individual. I mentioned to you that there is a plane given to the dream world, and this plane also represents actual constructions with which your conscious mind is not usually familiar.
quotes.translation: 观念代表着这类建构，只要任何个体给予足够强烈的推动力，它们实际上可以代代相传。我曾向你提及梦境世界拥有一个层面，这个层面也代表着你的意识心智通常不熟悉的实际建构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: In such a manner a simplicity is given which is helpful in explanations, but it must be kept in mind that all these explanations are extremely simplified. Dreams have a definite effect within the physical system. They are in part sparked with the aid of the physical system, but they do not happen within the physical system. They are not directly experienced by the physical system, but only translations of the original dream experience is felt by the actual physical system.
quotes.translation: 如此呈现的简化有助于解释，但必须谨记所有这些解释都极度简化。梦境在物质系统内具有明确影响。它们部分借助物质系统的协助被激发，但并非发生在物质系统之内。物质系统无法直接体验梦境，实际物质系统感受到的只是原始梦境经验的转译。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: He believed in Moses more than he did Allah, and I did not know until the last moment which form I was to assume. He was a very likable chap, and under the circumstances I did not mind when he seemed to expect a battle for his soul. Moses and Allah were to fight for him. He could not rid himself of the idea of force, though he had died by force, and nothing could persuade him to accept any kind of peace or contentment, or any rest, until some kind of battle was wrought.
quotes.translation: 他信仰摩西胜过安拉，直到最后一刻我才知道该以何种形象显现。他是个非常讨喜的家伙，在这种情况下，当他似乎期待一场灵魂争夺战时我并不介意。摩西与安拉要为他而战。他无法摆脱暴力的观念，尽管他死于暴力，除非经历某种战斗，否则任何事物都无法说服他接受任何形式的和平、满足或安息。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This happens fairly often. It does not change the fact that it is the last reincarnation cycle for parents for example, although it may be lonely for the child in such an instance. I have not gone into these questions; however, to some degree in the subconscious mind you have what could be called a ghost image of your ancestors, and when your parents are in their last reincarnation cycle they leave your plane and it is more difficult for the ghost image to be imprinted.
quotes.translation: 这种情况相当常见。例如这并不会改变父母处于最后一次转世周期的事实，尽管在此情况下孩子可能会感到孤独。我尚未深入探讨这些问题；然而在潜意识层面，你拥有可称为祖先鬼影的东西，当你的父母处于最后一次转世周期时，他们会离开你的层面，使得鬼影更难被铭印。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The conscious mind is a maker of distinctions. It brings to the surface of awareness whole gestalts of previously unconscious material, then assembles and organizes it in ever-changing form. Through purposeful focus, a literally infinite amount of such data can be unconsciously sorted; then only the desired elements will emerge.
quotes.translation: 意识心智是区分者。它将先前无意识材料的整体完形带到意识表层，然后以不断变化的形式进行组装与组织。通过有目的的聚焦，无限量的此类数据可以被无意识分类；随后只有所需的元素会浮现出来。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: When you receive a bill do not automatically react as you have, instead as if you were contemplating a new painting. Form in your mind the image of a check to that amount. See yourself celebrating as you pay the final amount on your car. See in your mind's eye a surplus of money in your folder when the months expenses are paid.
quotes.translation: 当你收到账单时，不要像往常那样自动反应，而要像在欣赏一幅新画作那样。在心中形成一张等额支票的影像。想象自己庆祝付清汽车最后一笔款项的情景。在心中之眼看到当月的开销付清后，文件夹里还有盈余的钱。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Again, the nature of space and time is glimpsed more clearly as it appears to the sleeping self, for in the dream state reality is to a large measure uncamouflaged, and the personality appears in a freer state. It should also be borne in mind that all aspects of the personality are part of the whole self. As such there is an overall communication between the various aspects of the self, although the separate aspects of the self may not be aware of the communications.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，当空间与时间的本质向沉睡的自我显现时，人们能更清晰地瞥见其真貌，因为在梦境状态中，现实在很大程度上是不加伪装的，人格也以更自由的状态呈现。同时必须谨记，人格的所有面向都是整体自性的一部分。正因如此，自我的各个面向之间存在着全面的沟通，尽管这些分离的面向可能并未意识到这些交流。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Therefore the inner self forms, first, the "invisible" body structure which will "later" emerge in flesh. At the event of this mental seeding, the conscious mind, in your terms, is obviously not connected with the brain, which has not yet been formed in flesh. The idea of the body is held and made physical by a conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 因此，内在自性首先形成"隐形"的身体结构，这个结构"后来"才会以血肉之躯显现。在这次心灵播种的事件中，用你们的话来说，意识心显然与尚未在肉身中成形的大脑没有关联。身体的概念是由意识心持有并使之物质化的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Sensual data is not basically--underlined--dependent upon the physical body. The individual can receive sensual data in other ways. The senses are indeed often used by the inner self as a constant method of acquainting it with circumstances pertinent to the survival and interests of the physical body, but the inner self is not entirely dependent on them for its entire data by any means. The mind can bypass the senses, and receive its data in a more direct manner, translating what it perceives as automatically as it translates sensual data.
quotes.translation: 感官数据基本上——强调——并不依赖于肉体。个体可以通过其他方式接收感官数据。内在自性确实经常将感官用作持续了解与肉体生存及利益相关情况的工具，但内在自性绝不完全依赖感官来获取全部数据。心灵可以绕过感官，以更直接的方式接收数据，将其感知的内容自动翻译，就像它翻译感官数据一样。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: I have used the phrase "moment of reflection" several times because it is another attribute peculiar to the conscious mind and, again in your terms, is largely denied to the rest of creaturehood. Without that pause -- in which man can remember past in the present, and envisage a future -- natural guilt would have no meaning. Man would not be able to recall past acts, judge them against the present situation, or imagine the future sense of guilt that might result.
quotes.translation: 我多次使用"反思时刻"这个说法，因为这是意识心特有的另一项属性，用你们的话说，这在很大程度上是其他生物所不具备的。没有这个停顿——人类可以在当下回忆过去，并展望未来——自然罪恶感就毫无意义。人类将无法回忆过去的行为，无法根据当下情境进行判断，也无法想象未来可能产生的罪恶感。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The new balance signals the organism that an inner problem has been resolved. The body, mind and psyche are then more or less operating together. When new psychic challenges arise, another round of natural therapy begins in rhythmic pattern. When imbalances of a physical nature are removed by the introduction of drugs, however, the body signals say that the inner dilemma must have been taken care of also -- while this may not be the case at all (very positively).
quotes.translation: 新的平衡向有机体发出信号，表明内在问题已经解决。身体、心灵和心智随后或多或少地协同运作。当新的心灵挑战出现时，另一轮自然疗法会以节律模式开始。然而，当通过引入药物消除身体性质的不平衡时，身体信号表明内在困境也必然得到了处理——而实际情况可能完全并非如此（非常肯定地）。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ruburt instinctively likes your tree painting. It represents a certain state of consciousness -- an in-between threshold dimension of awareness, in which the imagination and the senses are almost caught in the act of putting an object together, or of bringing the world into a sensed reality, brand-new, from the realm of the inner mind: a very evocative state of consciousness, and one that as I believe Ruburt mentioned, you could also use in connection with faces.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏本能地喜欢你的树画。它代表了某种意识状态——一种介于阈限之间的觉知维度，在此维度中，想象力与感官几乎在将物体组合成形、或从内在心灵领域将世界带入全新感官实相的过程中被捕捉：一种极具唤起力的意识状态，正如鲁柏曾提及的，你也可以将其运用于面容的描绘。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Now nevertheless the dream world, the mind and the reality universe do exist. They exist in a climate that we will call the value climate of psychological reality. This is the medium. This takes the place of what you call space. This value climate of psychological reality is a quality which makes all existences and consciousness possible. It is one of the most powerful principles behind or within the vitality that itself composes from itself all other phenomena.
quotes.translation: 然而，梦境世界、心灵与实相宇宙确实存在。它们存在于我们称之为"心理实相的价值气候"中。这是媒介，取代了你所谓的空间。这种心理实相的价值气候是一种品质，使所有存在与意识成为可能。它是生命力背后或内部最强大的原则之一，而生命力本身从自身构成了所有其他现象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: So what we want, obviously, is to ensure that the conscious mind, with its reasoning processes, can make proper adjustments about the nature of the world and the individual citizens within it. I will return later to the purposes of man's conscious mind in nature, and part of that discussion will fall in our book (Dreams).
quotes.translation: 因此，我们显然要确保拥有理性思维过程的意识心，能够对世界本质及其中的个体公民做出适当调整。我稍后将回归人类意识心在自然中的目的，这部分讨论将纳入我们的书（《梦》）。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: Your scientists can count their elements. While they are on the wrong track they will discover more and more elements. That is, they will create more and discover more until they are ready to go out of their minds. Because what will happen is, they will always create camouflages of the real thing. And while they create instruments to deal with smaller and smaller particular particles, they will actually see smaller and smaller particles, seemingly without end.
quotes.translation: 你们的科学家可以计算元素。当他们走在错误轨道上时，会发现越来越多的元素。也就是说，他们会创造更多、发现更多，直到他们准备发疯为止。因为会发生的是，他们总会创造出真实事物的伪装。当他们制造仪器来处理越来越小的特定粒子时，实际上会看到越来越小的粒子，似乎永无止境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The psychological-time experiments should also be suspended. When the conscious mind is so diverted the intuitions can do their work. You did well Joseph, teaching him discipline, but as he never goes halfway, so sometimes he learns his lessons too well. One point here also: you pace yourselves differently in your work.
quotes.translation: 心理时间实验也应暂停。当意识心被如此转移时，直觉才能发挥作用。约瑟，你做得很好，教导他自律，但正因为他从不半途而废，有时反而把功课学得太好了。这里还有一点：你们在工作中以不同的节奏前进。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: This obviously does not mean that the time of the body's death would not come. It does mean that the seasons of the body would be understood as following those of the mind, ever-changing and flowing, with conditions coming and going but always maintaining the splendid unity within the body's form. You would not have chronic illnesses. Generally speaking, and ideally, the body would wear out gradually while still showing far greater endurance than it does now.
quotes.translation: 这显然不意味着身体死亡的时间不会到来。它意味着身体的季节将被理解为跟随心灵的季节，不断变化流动，状况来来去去，却始终在身体形态内维持着辉煌的统一。你不会患上慢性疾病。一般而言，理想情况下，身体会逐渐磨损，同时仍展现出比现在远为强大的耐力。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: During Western years of adulthood, consciousness is focused most intently in one specific area of activity and physical manipulation. From childhood, the mind is trained to use its argumentative, separating qualities above all others. Creativity is allowed to flow only through certain highly limited, accepted channels.
quotes.translation: 在西方成年岁月中，意识最专注地集中于特定活动领域与物质操控。自童年起，心智便被训练优先运用其争辩性与分离性的特质。创造力仅被允许通过高度受限的特定渠道流动。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now, I would suggest that two main categories in particular be given special notice. These as you know will have to do with primary and secondary reality. And each dream should be scrutinized first of all with these main points in mind. You must look out for pitfalls, and use your critical faculties. Ruburt uses his critical faculties supremely well in scrutinizing my activities and nature, so I have no doubt that he can also apply them to the job at hand.
quotes.translation: 现在，我建议特别关注两个主要类别。如你所知，这涉及首要实相与次要实相。每个梦境都应首先带着这些要点进行审视。你必须警惕陷阱，运用批判能力。鲁柏在审视我的活动与本质时，将批判能力运用得极为出色，因此我毫不怀疑他也能将其应用于手头的工作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: In any case, I feel that the entire production, Seth's dictated work and my running commentaries and references, adds up to extra dimensions of creativity that can be sensed, if not described. When I get that feeling of psychological multiplicity, I realize that the goal I had in mind was at least somewhat realized.
quotes.translation: 无论如何，我认为整个创作——赛斯的口述内容与我的实时评论及参考——构成了可感知（即便无法描述）的额外创造力维度。当我感受到那种心理多重性时，便意识到心中目标至少已部分实现。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 7. In Session 692 for Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality see the material on Sue's double dreams in the opening notes and in Note 2. Personally, at least, I see strong connections between the idea of double dreams and the kind of conscious reincarnational memory -- or knowledge -- detailed by Sue in this appendix.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The body will seek its release, and so will the mind. Dreams, or even daydreams of a frightening nature, can fulfill that purpose. The mind's creative play often serves up symbolic events that result in therapeutic physical reactions, and also function as postdream suggestions that offer hints as to remedial action.
quotes.translation: 身体会寻求释放，心智亦然。具有惊悚性质的梦境甚至白日梦都能达成此目的。心智的创造性游戏常呈现象征性事件，引发治疗性生理反应，并作为梦后暗示提供补救行动的线索。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There are two ways to get at your own conscious beliefs. The most direct is to have a series of talks with yourself. Write down your beliefs in a variety of areas, and you will find that you believe different things at different times. Often there will be contradictions readily apparent. These represent opposing beliefs that regulate your emotions, your bodily condition and your physical experience. Examine the conflicts. Invisible beliefs will appear that unite those seemingly diverse attitudes. Invisible beliefs are simply those of which you are fully aware but prefer to ignore, because they represent areas of strife which you have not been willing to handle thus far. They are quite available once you are determined to examine the complete contents of your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 有两种方式可触及你自身的意识信念。最直接的是与自己进行系列对话。写下你在各领域的信念，会发现不同时期相信不同事物。矛盾往往显而易见，这些代表调节你情绪、身体状况与物质经验的对立信念。审视这些冲突，隐而不显的信念将浮现，统一那些看似分歧的态度。隐而不显的信念不过是你完全知晓却宁愿忽视的部分，因为它们代表你至今不愿处理的冲突领域。一旦你决心检视意识心智的全部内容，它们便唾手可得。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Exterior conditions can always be changed if you understand the principles of which I am speaking. Diseases can be eliminated, even those that seem fatal -- but only if the beliefs behind them are erased or altered enough so that their specific focusing effect upon the body is sufficiently released. The present as you think of it, and in practical working terms, is that point at which you select your physical experience from all those events that could be materialized. Your physical circumstances change automatically as your beliefs do. As your knowledge grows, so your experience becomes more fulfilling. This does not necessarily mean that it evens out in any way, or that there are not peaks and valleys. Each aspiration presupposes the admission of a lack, each challenge presupposes a barrier to be overcome. The more adventurous will often choose greater challenges, and so in their minds the contrasts between what they want to achieve and their present status can seem to be impossible.
quotes.translation: 若理解我所阐述的原理，外在状况总能改变。疾病可以消除，甚至那些看似致命的——但前提是必须抹除或充分改变其背后的信念，使它们对身体的具体聚焦效应得到充分释放。你所认知的当下，在实际运作层面，正是你从所有可能物质化的事件中选择物质经验的节点。你的物质环境会随信念改变而自动变化。随着知识增长，你的经验会愈发充实。这未必意味着经验会以任何方式趋于平缓，或不再有高峰低谷。每个渴望都预设了对匮乏的承认，每个挑战都预设了需跨越的障碍。更具冒险精神者常选择更大挑战，因此在他们的心智中，所欲达成的目标与当前状态之间的反差可能看似不可逾越。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Because of the ego's particular line of development, you have experimented with artificial drugs and chemicals, both in foods and for medicinal purposes, as well as for "religious" enlightenment. Some of the effects of LSD7 and other artificial psychedelic drugs give you a hint of other probable directions your consciousness might have followed, or might still follow. As the experiments are conducted, however, and in the ignorance of the framework, the conscious mind takes a subordinate position. Instead, using methods other than drugs, it could be taught to expand its knowledge far more safely, to organize it in ways that could be most advantageous. Still, some of the experiments do give hints of certain aspects of one of the species' probable developments.
quotes.translation: 由于自我的特定发展路线，你们在食物和药物中实验了人工合成的化学物质，也用于"宗教"启蒙。LSD7及其他人工迷幻药的部分效果，暗示了你们的意识可能曾遵循或仍可能遵循的其他可能方向。然而，在这些实验进行时，由于对框架的无知，意识心处于从属地位。其实，若能采用药物以外的方法，意识心本可被教导以更安全的方式扩展其知识，并以最有利的方式组织这些知识。不过，某些实验确实暗示了该物种可能发展的某些面向。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Think of your ideas about your own sexuality in connection with those about your being and consciousness. Regroup your ideas so that you automatically think of sexuality in relationship to your religions and sciences. You have associated the word "female" with the unconscious, while you have been working toward what you now think of as an egotistically based consciousness. There is more in what I am saying than you presently realize. You cannot, each of you, consider the real meaning of your own sexuality unless you understand your own religious history. Follow through, in your own minds, with your memories. Try to be honest with yourselves as to those early experiences in which you forced yourselves to behave differently than you were, because adults told you that you must ... You had better understand the beautiful, unique quality of your own individuality lest you project upon the other sex -- whichever sex you are -- those abilities and qualities that you are afraid are your own, or project upon them those abilities and qualities that you wish you possessed and fear you do not.
quotes.translation: 请将你们对自身性别的看法，与对存在和意识的认知联系起来思考。重组你们的观念，使你们能自动将性别与宗教和科学关联起来。你们将"女性"一词与无意识相连，而你们正朝着自以为基于自我的意识发展。我所说的远非你们目前所能理解。除非你们了解自身的宗教历史，否则无法真正理解自己性别的真实含义。请在你们自己的心智中追溯记忆。诚实地面对那些早期经历——当你们被迫表现得与本性不同时，因为成年人告诉你们必须如此……你们最好理解自身个体性那美丽而独特的特质，以免将自己恐惧拥有的能力与品质投射到异性身上，或将自己渴望拥有却恐惧缺乏的能力与品质投射给他们。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (FOR SESSION 690)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Some individuals find themselves with memories of other lives, which are other days to the soul. Such persons then become aware of a greater consciousness reaching over those gaps, and realize that earthly experience can contain [among other things] a knowledge of existence in more than one body. Inherently then consciousness, affiliated with the flesh, can indeed carry such comprehensions. The mind of man as you know it shows at least the potential ability for handling a kind of memory with which you are usually not acquainted. This means that even biologically the species is equipped to deal with different sequences of time, while still manipulating within one particular time scheme. This also implies a far greater psychological richness -- quite possible, again, within corporal reality -- in which many levels of relationships can be handled. Such inner knowledge is inherent in the cells, and in ordinary terms of evolution is quite possible as a "future" development.
quotes.translation: 有些人发现自己拥有其他生的记忆——对灵魂而言那只是其他日子。这些人于是意识到有更伟大的意识跨越这些间隙，并认识到尘世体验可以包含（除其他事物外）在多个身体中存在的知识。因此，与肉体相连的意识本质上确实能承载这样的理解。你们所知的人类心智至少展现出处理某种你们通常不熟悉的记忆的潜在能力。这意味着即使在生物学层面，该物种也具备处理不同时间序列的能力，同时仍能在特定时间架构内运作。这也暗示了更丰富的心理层次——再次强调，这在肉体实相中完全可能——其中可以处理多重关系层面。这种内在知识存在于细胞中，以寻常的演化术语而言，完全可能作为"未来"的发展。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 708
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You may, for example, have read books numbering the "inner realms," and telling you what you can expect to encounter in each. Many of these speak of lords or gods of the realm, or of demons. In a strange way these books do provide a service, for at certain levels you will find your own ideas materialized; and if you believe in demons then in those terms you will encounter them. The authors, however, suppose that the devils have a reality outside of your belief in them, and such is not the case. The demons simply represent a state of your own mind that is seemingly out there, objectified. Therefore, whatever methods the authors used to triumph over these demons is often given as proof not only of the demons' reality, but of each method's effectiveness.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，你们可能读过列举"内在领域"的书籍，告诉你们在每个领域会遇到什么。许多这类书提到领域之主、神祇或恶魔。奇怪的是，这些书确实提供了某种服务，因为在特定层次，你们会发现自己的观念被具体化；若你们相信恶魔，那么在这些层面你们就会遭遇它们。然而，作者们假设恶魔具有独立于你们信仰之外的实相，但事实并非如此。恶魔只是你们心智状态的投射，看似存在于外而具体化。因此，无论作者们用什么方法战胜这些恶魔，往往被当作证明恶魔实相及方法有效性的证据。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 710
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You are social creatures, as the animals are. Despite many of your cherished, erroneous beliefs, your nations exist as the result of cooperation, not competition, as do all social groupings. To be ostracized is no laughing matter. The comfort of social discourse represents one of the great building blocks of families and civilizations. The set criteria of reality, therefore, operate as organizing, psychic, and physical frameworks. There is still more flexibility within those frameworks, however, than is recognized. You still try to carry your own cultural versions of reality into the dream state, for example, but the natural heritage of both body and mind escapes such repression -- and despite yourselves, in your dreams you come in touch with a greater picture of reality that will not be shunted aside.
quotes.translation: 你们是群居生物，如同动物一般。尽管有许多珍视的错误信念，你们国家的存在源于合作而非竞争——所有社会群体皆然。被排斥绝非小事。社会交流的慰藉是家庭与文明的基石。因此，实相的既定标准运作着，作为组织性的、心理的和肉体的框架。然而，这些框架内仍有比人们所认知的更大的灵活性。例如，你们仍试图将自身文化的实相版本带入梦境，但身体与心智的自然传承会逃脱这种压抑——尽管你们不情愿，在梦中你们仍会触及无法被排除的更大实相图景。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Session 757 was devoted to other matters that Jane and I were interested in.)

quotes.text: Those experienced events, however, are also the result of a screening process. They attain their focus, brilliance, and physical validity because they rise into prominence on the backs of other seemingly unperceived events. In the dream state you work intimately with the "inner grammar" of events. In dreams you find the unspoken sentence and the physically unexperienced act. The skeletons of the inner workings of events are there more obvious. Actions are not yet fully fleshed out. The mechanics of your waking psychological behavior are brilliantly delineated. That state can be explored and utilized far more fully than it is, and should be. Yet there will always be a veil between the waking and sleeping consciousness, for while you are physical, the waking mind can only deal with so much information. It would simply forget what it cannot hold.
quotes.translation: 然而，那些被体验的事件也是筛选过程的结果。它们之所以获得焦点、光辉与肉体有效性，是因为它们借由其他看似未被感知的事件之背而凸显。在梦境中，你们与事件的"内在语法"密切合作。在梦中，你们找到未被说出的句子和未被肉体体验的行为。事件内在运作的骨架更为明显。行动尚未完全成形。你们清醒时心理行为的机制被清晰地描绘出来。这个状态可以被更充分地探索和利用，也应该如此。但清醒与睡眠意识之间永远存在一层帷幕，因为只要你们是肉体的，清醒心智只能处理有限的信息。它只会遗忘无法承载的内容。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the dream state, with your body more or less safe and at rest, and without the necessity for precise action, these psychological intrusions become more apparent. Many of your dreams are like the tail end of a comet: Their real life is over, and you see the flash of their disappearance as they strike your own mental atmosphere and explode in a spark of dream images. They are transformed, therefore, as they travel through your own psychological atmosphere. You could not perceive them in your own state -- nor can they maintain their native state as they plunge through the far reaches of the psyche. They fall in patterns, forming themselves naturally into the dream contents that fit the contours of your own mind. The resulting structure of the dream suits your reality and no other: As this intrusive matter falls, plummets, or shifts through the levels of your own psychological atmosphere, it is transformed by the conditions it meets.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，当你的身体大致处于安全与休息状态，且无需精确行动时，这些心理入侵会变得更加明显。你的许多梦境就像彗星的尾巴：它们的真实生命已经结束，你看到它们消失的闪光，当它们撞击你的心理大气层，并在梦意象的火花中爆炸时。因此，当它们穿越你的心理大气层时，它们被转化了。在你的状态中你无法感知它们——它们也无法在穿越心灵遥远领域时保持其原生状态。它们以模式坠落，自然形成适合你心灵轮廓的梦内容。由此产生的梦境结构只符合你的现实，而非其他：当这些入侵物质坠落、骤降或穿过你心理大气层的各个层面时，它被所遇条件转化。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those who imagine they look upon nature with the most objective of eyes are those whose subjective beliefs blind them most of all, for they cannot see through their own misinterpretations. It has been said that statistics can be made to say two things at once, both contradictory; so the facts of nature can be read in completely different fashions as they are put together with the organizational abilities of the mind operating through the brain's beliefs. The exterior core of dreams is also blemished to that degree, but the inner core of dreams provides a constant new influx of material, feedback, and insight from the psyche, so that the personality is not at the mercy of its exterior experience only -- not confined to environmental feedback only, but ever provided with fresh intuitive data and direction.
quotes.translation: 那些想象自己以最客观的眼光看待自然的人，正是那些主观信念最盲目的人，因为他们无法看穿自己的误解。据说统计数字可以同时说出两件相互矛盾的事情；同样，自然的事实也可以以完全不同的方式被解读，当它们通过大脑信念运作的心灵组织能力被组合在一起时。梦的外在核心也因此受到玷污，但梦的内在核心却不断提供来自心灵的新材料、反馈和洞见，使人格不仅受制于外在经验——不仅局限于环境反馈，而且始终获得新鲜的直觉数据和方向。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I'm well aware of current scientific theories about the supposed separate functions of the two hemispheres of the brain: The left half is said to control logical activities like writing, while the right half is responsible for the intuitive artistic abilities. Perhaps -- but after all, writing can be intuitively based, and art can be logically produced. At least the whole brain (its hemispheres are connected deep within by the corpus callosum) must contain that necessary basic creative ability that may then be apportioned out -- but only to an extent, I think -- between the hemispheres. Barring physical injury/surgery, there must be more communication between its halves (via the corpus callosum) than the brain is given credit for. There's so much we don't know yet about the brain (let alone the mind!). What about telepathy between the hemispheres? I think the divisions charted for the brain so far may be too strict, that beliefs about such separations may get in the way of our perception of the brain's beautifully whole operation.
quotes.translation: 我深知当前关于大脑两个半球所谓独立功能的科学理论：据说左半球控制逻辑活动如写作，而右半球负责直觉艺术能力。也许如此——但毕竟，写作可以基于直觉，艺术也可以逻辑地产生。至少整个大脑（其半球通过深处的胼胝体相连）必须包含那种必要的基本创造能力，然后可能被分配——但只是在一定程度上，我认为——到两个半球之间。除非有身体损伤/手术，否则其两半之间的沟通（通过胼胝体）一定比大脑所被认可的更多。我们对大脑的了解还太少（更不用说心灵了！）。半球之间的心灵感应呢？我认为迄今为止对大脑的划分可能过于严格，关于这种分离的信念可能会妨碍我们感知大脑美妙整体运作。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: NOTES: SESSION 814
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Some people are only aware of -- or largely aware of -- impulses toward anger, because they have inhibited those natural impulses toward love that would otherwise temper what seemed to be aggressive desires. When you begin trusting yourselves, you start by taking it for granted that to some extent at least you have not trusted yourself or your impulses in the past: You have thought that impulses were dangerous, disruptive, or even evil. So as you begin to learn self-trust, you acknowledge your impulses. You try them on for size. You see where they lead you by allowing them some freedom. You do not follow urges through that would hurt others physically, or that seem in direct contradiction to your present beliefs -- but you do acknowledge them. You do try to discover their source. Behind them you will almost always find an inhibited impulse -- or many of them -- that motivated you to move in some ideal direction, to seek a love or understanding so idealized in your mind that it seemed impossible to achieve. You are left with the impulse to strike out.
quotes.translation: 有些人只意识到——或主要意识到——愤怒的冲动，因为他们抑制了那些本可调和看似攻击性欲望的自然爱的冲动。当你开始信任自己时，你首先会理所当然地认为，至少在某种程度上，你过去并不信任自己或自己的冲动：你认为冲动是危险的、破坏性的，甚至邪恶的。因此，当你开始学习自我信任时，你承认你的冲动。你试穿它们的大小。你通过给予它们一些自由来观察它们将你引向何处。你不会遵循那些会伤害他人身体、或似乎与你当前信念直接矛盾的冲动——但你确实承认它们。你确实试图发现它们的来源。在它们背后，你几乎总会发现一个被抑制的冲动——或许多个——它们曾激励你朝着某个理想方向前进，去寻求一种在你心中如此理想化以至于似乎不可能实现的爱或理解。你只剩下出击的冲动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 872, AUGUST 8, 1979
9:15 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition
