quotes.text: But if the interactions between or among frameworks exist for everybody, in our terms, then as far as I'm concerned they exist for each thing as well--and I do mean the so-called "inanimate." (This isn't the place to go into it, but Seth maintains that for many reasons we arbitrarily decide what's living and nonliving.) Each reincarnational self, each counterpart self and probable self has its complement of frameworks. So does the most minute living or nonliving entity and the most gigantic. So, "probably," do most of the far-out probable realities one can imagine--for I won't go so far as to deny that some probable realities may exist without such framework structures. Strange one-dimensional "flatlands" indeed! But in each case where those framework interactions operate, they help each creation, each presence, each essence or vital principle fulfill "a larger picture that the conscious mind may not be able presently to perceive." In ways I can't even begin to describe here, all frameworks must ultimately be joined within the ineffable context of All That Is.
quotes.translation: 但如果框架之间的互动存在于每个人身上，用我们的说法，那么在我看来，它们也存在于每件事物上——我确实指的是所谓的"无生命"。（这里不是深入探讨的地方，但赛斯坚持认为，出于许多原因，我们武断地决定了什么是活的，什么是非活的。）每个转世自我、每个对应自我和可能自我都有其框架的补充。最微小的生命或非生命实体和最庞大的实体也是如此。因此，"很可能"，大多数人们能想象的遥远可能现实也是如此——因为我不至于否认某些可能现实可能在没有这种框架结构的情况下存在。奇怪的一维"平原地"确实存在！但在每种框架互动运作的情况下，它们帮助每个创造、每个存在、每个本质或生命原则实现"一个当前意识心灵可能无法感知的更大图景。"以我甚至无法在此开始描述的方式，所有框架最终必须在一切万有的不可言喻的语境中联合。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: I don't really think we can conceive of anything to be truly "alien," though, so I use the word here only to lead into the next of Seth's larger concepts that I want to mention: that of probable realities, or probabilities, as Jane and I usually say. Not only does Seth stress the constant psychic motion of reincarnational and counterpart selves upon this earth we know--but he also tells us that each of those selves can move into other or parallel realities. I quote him from Session 681 in Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 6: Genetic Heritage and Reincarnational Predilections

quotes.text: If small families kept track of their own family dreams, for example, they could discover unsuspected correlations and sense the interplay of subjective and objective drama with which they are always psychologically involved. Notice what kind of information you seek out from the newspapers, for example. Do you read the front page and ignore sports, or vice versa? Do you read the gossip column? The obituary? Do you seek out stories of lurid crime, or look for further incidents of political chicanery? The answers will show you the kind of material you look for most often. You will to some extent specialize in the same kind of information when you dream. You will organize the contents of your mind and the information available to you according to your own intents and purposes.
quotes.translation: 例如，如果小家庭记录自己的家庭梦，他们就能发现未曾料到的关联，并感受到他们始终心理参与的主观与客观戏剧的相互作用。注意你从报纸中寻找什么样的信息，例如。你读头版而忽略体育，还是反之？你读八卦专栏吗？讣告？你寻找耸人听闻的犯罪故事，还是寻找政治诡计的进一步事件？答案将显示你最常寻找的材料类型。你在做梦时也会在某种程度上专注于同类信息。你将根据你自己的意图和目的来组织你心灵的内容和你可获得的信息。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "I did briefly give him a message (on October 23, 1981): Attend to what is before you, for it is there for a reason. In each person's life, and in your own, at each and every point of your existence, the solutions to your problems, or the means of achieving those solutions, are always as apparent--or rather as present--within your days as is any given problem itself. What I mean is quite simple: The solutions already exist in your lives. You may not have put them together yet, or organized them in the necessary ways. The solutions in Ruburt's case lie in all of those areas with which you are normally concerned--the mail, the sessions, the psychic abilities. When you attend to what is there with the proper magical attitude of mind, then the altered organizations can take place.
quotes.translation: 我确实曾简短地给他传过话（1981年10月23日）：关注眼前之事，因其存在自有缘由。在每个人的生命中，在你自己的生命里，在你存在的每一个时刻，问题的解决方案或达成这些方案的方法，始终如问题本身一样显而易见——或者说一样触手可及。我的意思很简单：解决方案已经存在于你们的生活中。你们或许尚未将它们整合起来，或以必要的方式组织它们。在鲁柏的情况中，解决方案存在于你们通常关注的所有领域——邮件、课程、通灵能力。当你以恰当的神奇心态关注眼前之物时，重组便可能发生。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 7. In Chapter 2 of Dreams, in Volume 1, see Note 1 for Session 885, which we held on October 24, 1979. Through a series of misunderstandings, the people at Ankh-Hermes had published an abridged translation of Seth Speaks without having permission for the cutting from Jane and me. We still feel regret that the company had to go to all of that extra expense in order to publish a second edition of the book. I also wrote that "all concerned must wait for at least another year before a full-length version of Seth Speaks will be published in the Dutch language." Actually, almost two years passed before we received our copies of the new edition.

quotes.text: Your species shares with the other species a feeling of kinship for its kind. There is a great give-and-take of ideas. You end up, then, with a consensus, generally speaking, as to what a reasonable picture of agreed-upon reality is. Your system has frowned upon many experiences, considering them eccentric behavior in an adverse fashion, since your belief systems have so regimented behavior, and so narrowly defined sanity. (Long pause.) The intellect, I want to stress, is socially oriented. It is peculiarly suited, of course, to react to cultural information. (Pause.) It wants to see the world as it is seen by the minds of others. Through that kind of action it helps form your cultural environment, the civilization of which you are justly proud.
quotes.translation: 你们的物种与其他物种共享一种同类相惜之情。思想之间存在着大量的交流与互动。因此，总体而言，你们最终会形成一种共识，即什么是公认现实的合理图景。你们的体系对许多经验持否定态度，将它们视为反常行为，因为你们的信念体系如此严格地规范了行为，并如此狭隘地定义了理智。（长停顿。）我想强调，理智是社会导向的。它当然特别适合对文化信息做出反应。（停顿。）它希望看到世界如同他人心智所见。通过这种行动，它帮助塑造了你们的文化环境，以及你们引以为傲的文明。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: This needs brief explanation. If the three of you turn your attention to the set, the first one to consider it will form the so-called initial construction. This is for simplicity's sake. I have told you that all living--and I will use the term conditions--cooperate in the formation of your physical universe. Your construction of the set, Joseph, will involve the use of energy formed into various combinations of atoms and molecules; and these themselves give off vibrations that are received subconsciously, and also serve therefore to give indications of approximate location, bulk, and even particular material and color, to the subconscious mind of any observer, so-called; although he does not see your construction but forms his own, more or less in faithful replica, to what he has subconsciously perceived telepathically from you and any other constructor, from generalized notions of the idea behind the construction, and from vibrations and even impacts received from the atoms and molecules that compose other constructions of the so-called single object.
quotes.translation: 这需要简要说明。如果你们三人将注意力转向这个布景，第一个考虑它的人将形成所谓的初始建构。这是为了简化起见。我曾告诉过你们，所有活生生的——我将使用"条件"一词——都合作形成你们的物质宇宙。约瑟，你对布景的建构将涉及能量被形成为原子和分子的各种组合；这些原子和分子本身会发出振动，被潜意识接收，因此也为任何所谓观察者的潜意识心智提供大致位置、体积、甚至特定材料和颜色的指示；尽管他并非看到你的建构，而是形成自己的建构，或多或少忠实地复制了他从你和其他建构者那里通过心灵感应潜意识感知到的内容，从建构背后理念的普遍概念，以及从构成所谓单一物体其他建构的原子和分子所接收到的振动甚至冲击。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: (Unfortunately it was a display; at least this is our way of looking at it and certainly our way of looking at it must he tin most important one and our attitudes must guide our actions; no one else altitudes. The information in the first part was excellent, to my way of seeing it; but it was gained at the cost of this exhibition. And in order to use the ability it must be disciplined: it could hardly be allowed to run wild. It probably wouldn't in any case certainly mediums who charge for readings and hold regular ones daily, don't go through that all the time. On the other hand the strength of the ability, also determines the amount of discipline to be used, it has to. You cant clam up so tight in fear that you don't give yourself the opportunity to use or develop the ability surely but you don't use it indiscriminately either。The way it was used the other might does frighten me to some degree surely. I'd even go so far as to say that an overly disciplined attitude would lead to a fairly decent and balanced frame of mind. I permission altitude would make me more frightened. Looking back, I see that the situation actually was strictly supervised; if not I do not think I would have done anything. But the fact is: I'm not sure.
quotes.translation: （不幸的是，这是一场展示；至少这是我们的看待方式，而我们的看待方式当然必须是最重要的，我们的态度必须指导我们的行动；没有其他人的态度。第一部分的信息在我看来非常出色；但它是通过这场展示的代价获得的。而为了使用这种能力，它必须受到约束：几乎不能任其失控。无论如何，它可能不会失控——当然，那些收费进行解读并定期举行日常解读的灵媒，并不会一直经历这种情况。另一方面，能力的强度也决定了需要施加的约束程度，这是必然的。你不能因为恐惧而把自己封闭得如此严密，以至于不给自己机会去使用或发展这种能力，但你也不能不加区分地使用它。上周五晚上使用它的方式确实在某种程度上让我感到害怕。我甚至可以说，过度约束的态度会导致相当体面和平衡的心态。而放任的态度会让我更加害怕。回想起来，我看到当时的情况实际上是受到严格监督的；如果不是这样，我想我不会做任何事。但事实是：我不确定。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: And good evening to our friend. I do have some remarks for you and I hope that they will be helpful. You must remember to hold your head up. You should know better than to cower when you come here. I have no whip; and I would not use one if I had one. You are not sure enough of your own abilities or of your own worth. You cannot drive through physical life in the same way that you drive your car down the highway. You shall indeed, get traffic tickets and of a different kind, only you yourself give yourself tickets. You cannot force reality to give you what you want. You cannot manipulate events for egotistical purposes. You can manipulate events and you can manipulate them for your own egotistical purposes; but when you do so, you give yourself a traffic ticket. You must want what is best for your own development and the development of others rather than specifically determining what you think consciously is better for you and then trying to force or coerce fate to get this for you. If you want what is best for your own development and what is best for the development of others, then you shall attain it. It shall come to you effortlessly. I am leading up to certain issues here. You are not always aware of what is best for you on a conscious level. Often the person that you think you want or need is not the person you want or need on other levels. When you drive your car you often attempt to speed through reality as quickly as you can, and you are pleased with yourself as the driver of the vehicle. You like driving because you feel that it gets you where you want to go and quickly, and you do not mind breaking a few small rules of the road in the process. Now the small rules that you break, are indeed, minute ones. It is not that you break a specific rule; it is the attitude that allows you to break the rule; and this applies to other roads beside the physical highway. You want to get to your destination too quickly. The destination is within you. You do not have to go any place to get that destination; and it is only when you think that that destination lies elsewhere that you allow yourself to go astray. Your identity is within you and do not look for it in others. This is perhaps the strongest point of my message to you this evening, the one I would have you take to heart. When you realize that your own identity is within you will not spend energy seeking to find yourself in others. Others cannot give you a sense of worth; this is your own. Any lack is your own lack. I may perhaps deal with more specific issues this evening. But I will not discuss them until these points have been made.
quotes.translation: 晚上好，我们的朋友。我确实有一些话要对你说，希望它们能有所帮助。你必须记住要抬起头来。你来这里时，应该知道不该畏缩。我没有鞭子；即使有，我也不会用它。你对自己的能力或自身价值不够确信。你不能像在高速公路上开车那样穿越物质生活。你确实会收到交通罚单，而且是另一种类型的，只是这些罚单是你自己开给自己的。你不能强迫现实给你想要的东西。你不能为了自我目的而操纵事件。你可以操纵事件，也可以为了自己的自我目的而操纵它们；但当你这样做时，你就给自己开了一张交通罚单。你必须渴望对自己发展最有利的东西，以及对他人发展最有利的东西，而不是具体决定你意识上认为对自己更好的东西，然后试图强迫或胁迫命运为你实现它。如果你渴望对自己发展最有利的东西，以及对他人发展最有利的东西，那么你就会得到它。它会毫不费力地来到你身边。我在这里要引出某些问题。你并不总是在意识层面知道什么对你最有利。通常，你认为自己想要或需要的人，在其他层面上并不是你想要或需要的人。当你开车时，你常常试图尽可能快地穿越现实，你为自己作为车辆的驾驶者而感到得意。你喜欢开车，因为你感觉它能让你快速到达想去的地方，并且你不介意在这个过程中违反一些小的交通规则。现在，你违反的那些小规则，确实是很微小的。问题不在于你违反了某个具体规则；而在于允许你违反规则的态度；这适用于物质高速公路以外的其他道路。你太急于到达目的地。目的地就在你之内。你不需要去任何地方才能到达那个目的地；只有当你认为那个目的地存在于别处时，你才会让自己误入歧途。你的身份就在你之内，不要到别人身上去寻找它。这也许是我今晚对你传达的信息中最有力的一点，我希望你能铭记在心。当你意识到自己的身份就在你之内时，你就不会花费精力在别人身上寻找自己。别人不能给你价值感；这是你自己的。任何匮乏都是你自己的匮乏。今晚我或许会讨论更具体的问题。但在这些要点被阐明之前，我不会讨论它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

source_raw.title: (I thought it a good idea that this be the end of the session, however, Seth came through again at 10:30 as the three of us sat talking about various things. A humorous repartee followed, with Seth answering our questions on a variety of subjects. Two serious notes: Alice Butts, who died last week, has already visited my father. And Dr. Instream, who would say "There is no Seth, " has not much longer to live. Also -- Bill Gallagher will not see certain truths about himself etc. Alice Butts not helping Ruth Butts ' son, etc. See Session 398.)

quotes.text: Now this also overshadows your relationship with the males to whom you have come in contact. For you have been, on the one hand, terrified of them, and on the other hand wanted a normal relationship. Give me a moment here --on the one hand you desire more from a relationship with a man than you have any right to expect. No human being could ever deliver what you expect a man to deliver in a relationship. This is because you see the male in terms inspired in you when you were a child. You were terrified of the male, your father. On the other hand, you felt that he did contain wisdom, truth, almost godlike qualities. These qualities you attempt to project into the male that you meet. At the same time you are also terrified because of this background. No man can possibly be as godlike as your inner conception. Therefore, each man is bound to disappoint you. At the same time, you hope and pray subconsciously that the man will disappoint you because this male in your mind has godlike qualities that attract you; on the other, you see him as all powerful and as one who gives out punishment and one who is unreasoning and cruel because you felt that your father was cruel. You are afraid, so to speak, to come under a man's thumb for this reason, to come under his domination. For to do so is to place yourself in a humble position and a frightening position underneath the male figure. Your terror as a child gave you an inner idea of reality and family group whereby you saw yourself completely powerless and helpless under the domination of this father figure. He was the source of all and yet he could at the same time take all away. And you felt, at the same time, that he would indeed do so. Because you were a male in past lives, you resented this all the more strongly. Give us a moment.
quotes.translation: 现在，这也影响了你与所接触男性之间的关系。因为你一方面对他们感到恐惧，另一方面又想要一段正常的关系。请给我一点时间——一方面，你从与男性的关系中期望得到的，超出了你有权期望的任何东西。没有人能够满足你在关系中期望男性给予的东西。这是因为你以童年时被激发的方式看待男性。你害怕男性，你的父亲。另一方面，你觉得他确实蕴含着智慧、真理，几乎是神一般的品质。你试图将这些品质投射到你遇到的男性身上。同时，由于这个背景，你也感到恐惧。没有男人可能像你内心构想的那样神一般。因此，每个男人都注定会让你失望。同时，你在潜意识中希望并祈祷男人会让你失望，因为一方面，你心中的这个男性具有吸引你的神一般品质；另一方面，你将他视为全能者，是施加惩罚者，是不讲理且残忍的，因为你认为你的父亲是残忍的。因此，可以说，你害怕被男人掌控，害怕受他支配。因为这样做就是将自己置于男性形象之下的卑微和可怕位置。你童年时的恐惧给了你一个关于现实和家庭群体的内在观念，在这种观念中，你看到自己在这个父亲形象的支配下完全无能为力、无助。他是一切之源，同时也能夺走一切。而你觉得，他确实会这样做。因为你在前世是男性，你对此更加愤恨。请给我们一点时间。

quotes.text: I realize that when you discuss him that you say--I know he has failings. This is to assure yourself that, after all, the male is not so all powerful. But you do not see this man's good points or failings clearly. Some of the qualities that you imagine in him as virtues are not and some of the qualities that you imagine to be failings are not failings. You will never have any relationship with the Dick Reed that you have projected upon a living human being. You may have a relationship with that human being, but there is a world of difference between that human being and the imagined image of him to which you react. And it is that image that you see when you look at him and when think of him. That imagined image is real in your mind, it is reality. But you cannot project that image upon another human being and deny him his own reality. You have no chance in a thousand lives of having a relationship with the man you think of as being Dick Reed, because you cannot have a two-way relationship with an image that is one-sided and has no flesh. Now give us a moment. While we are beginning a job, we may as well do a good one.
quotes.translation: 我明白当你谈论他时，你会说——我知道他有缺点。这是为了让自己确信，男性终究并非无所不能。但你并未看清这个人的优点或缺点。你想象中属于他的某些品质并非美德，而某些你视为缺点的特质也并非缺点。你永远无法与你投射在活生生人类身上的那个迪克·里德建立任何关系。你或许能与那个真实的人建立关系，但真实的人与你所回应的那个想象形象之间存在着天壤之别。当你注视他、思念他时，你所见的正是那个想象形象。那个想象形象在你的心智中是真实的，它就是实相。但你不能将这个形象投射到另一个人身上，并否认他自身的实相。你在一千次生命中都没有机会与你所认为的迪克·里德建立关系，因为你无法与一个片面且没有血肉的形象建立双向关系。现在请给我们一点时间。既然我们开始一项工作，不妨把它做好。

quotes.text: You must mentally wipe out the negative image, for example. If you think that tomorrow Johnny F will misbehave in study hall, you should, in your mind, replace this with the image of Johnny F behaving very well. In the first place, if you imagine that a particular student will misbehave, you are automatically sending him a telepathic message to that effect. If he is highly susceptible to suggestion, he will carry out the suggestions that you have given.
quotes.translation: 举例来说，你必须在心智中抹去负面形象。如果你认为明天约翰尼·F会在自习室捣乱，你应在心中用约翰尼·F表现良好的形象取而代之。首先，如果你想象某个学生会捣乱，你便自动向他发送了相应的心灵感应讯息。若他极易受暗示影响，便会执行你所给予的暗示。

quotes.text: There is actually a quite natural and most effective manner of psychic refreshment, and most particularly when the conscious mind is somewhat diverted, as by music and so forth. The intuitions act well under such circumstances, and these intuitions are stored subconsciously, and will show themselves in your work.
quotes.translation: 实际上存在一种非常自然且极为有效的灵性休憩方式，尤其当意识心被音乐等事物稍加转移时。直觉在此类情境下运作良好，这些直觉会被潜意识储存，并将在你的工作中显现出来。

quotes.text: The proximity of so many trees also has considerable health value, and to those doing psychic or other creative work the effects are particularly conducive to a peaceful state of mind. Trees are great users and yet conservators of energy, and they automatically provide much vitality to areas in which they are plentiful. This is physically obvious in scientific terms. Besides that, however, the consciousnesses of trees are remarkably kind and enduring.5
quotes.translation: 众多树木的邻近也具有相当的健康价值，对于从事灵性或创意工作的人而言，其效果特别有助于营造平静的心境。树木是能量的巨大使用者与保存者，它们会自动为树木繁茂的区域提供大量活力。这在科学术语上于物质层面显而易见。除此之外，树木的意识极为仁慈且持久。

quotes.text: What is required of them both in their daily working lives is in many respects precisely the same. They are both excellent salesmen. They are both intimately familiar with the use of energy, in that they are able to use their own energy to affect the minds of other individuals. But their reactions are entirely different, and Philip's past life experiences prepared him for the nature of his present occupation, where he is dealing with medicinal matters.
quotes.translation: 他们在日常工作中所需具备的特质，在许多方面完全相同。两人都是出色的销售员。他们都深谙能量的运用之道，能够运用自身能量影响他人的心智。但他们的反应截然不同，而菲利普的前世经历为他当前职业的性质做好了准备——他正处理医药相关事务。

quotes.text: Before children are acquainted with conventional ideas of guilt and punishment, they realize that it is easier to bring about good events, through wishing, than it is to bring about unhappy ones. The child carries with him [or her] the impetus and supporting energy provided him at birth from Framework 2, and he knows intuitively that desires conducive to his development "happen" easier than those that are not. His natural impulses naturally lead him toward the development of his body and mind, and he is aware of a cushioning effect and support as he acts in accordance with those inner impulses. The child is innately honest. When he gets sick he intuitively knows the reason why, and he knows quite well that he brought about the illness.
quotes.translation: 在儿童熟悉传统的罪恶与惩罚观念之前，他们便明白通过许愿带来好事比带来坏事更容易。孩子携带着出生时从架构二获得的动力与支持能量，直觉知道有利于自身发展的愿望比不利的愿望"更容易实现"。其自然冲动自然引导他们发展身心，当他们依循这些内在冲动行事时，会意识到缓冲效应与支持。孩子天生诚实。生病时他们直觉知道原因，并且很清楚是自己导致了这场疾病。

quotes.text: The physical brain alone cannot pick up these connections with any great success. The mind, which is the inner counterpart of the brain, can at times perceive the far greater dimensions of any given event through a burst of sudden intuition or comprehension that cannot be adequately described on a verbal level.
quotes.translation: 单靠物质大脑无法成功捕捉这些联系。心灵作为大脑的内在对应物，有时能通过突如其来的直觉或领悟感知任何特定事件的更广阔维度，这种领悟无法用言语充分描述。

quotes.text: Your ideas of sexuality follow both your religions and your sciences, then, for you have created each. But you always know what you are doing, and there are cycles in the earth, and in your being and your soul. And so you are in the process of reuniting yourself, and discovering what the word "humanity" means. You are finding out the meaning of individuality, which is a far more important word than you realize, and when you understand that meaning, then your own individuality will express itself in its natural form. Regardless of what words are put upon your experience in terms of sexual roles, you will be a full human being. When the conscious and the unconscious minds are understood, you will have no more problems with sexuality.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们对性的观念既遵循宗教也遵循科学，因为两者都是你们创造的。但你们始终清楚自己在做什么，地球、你们的存有和灵魂都有其循环。所以你们正处在重新整合自身、发现"人性"一词含义的过程中。你们正在探索"个体性"的意义——这个词远比你们意识到的更重要，当你们理解其含义时，你们的个体性将以自然形式表达自身。无论用何种词汇描述你们在性别角色方面的经验，你们都将成为完整的人。当有意识和无意识心灵被理解时，你们将不再有性方面的问题。

quotes.text: The time that any artistic creator is involved with follows earth's own time, however. The creator's time rises out of the seasons and the tides, even though in your society you make a great effort to fit the creator's time into what I will call assembly-line time. If you are a writer or an artist, then it seems that you must produce so many paintings or books or whatever as, say, an automobile worker must process so many pieces of the overall car chassis. Particularly if you want to make a living at your art, you fall into the frame of mind in which you think that "each minute is valuable" -- but what you mean is that each minute must be a minute of production. But each moment must be valuable in itself, whatever you do with it.
quotes.translation: 然而，任何艺术创作者所涉及的时间都遵循地球自身的时间。创作者的时间源于季节与潮汐，尽管在你们的社会中，你们极力将创作者的时间纳入我所谓的流水线时间。如果你是一位作家或艺术家，似乎你必须产出一定数量的画作、书籍或其他作品，就像汽车工人必须加工一定数量的底盘部件。特别是如果你想靠艺术谋生，你会陷入"每一分钟都很宝贵"的心态——但你的意思是每分钟都必须用于生产。然而，无论你如何利用每一刻，它本身都必须是宝贵的。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You do not have to wonder at the small physical symptoms which have bothered both of you lately. Before our sessions began your reactions were, as you might recall, much more vehement. The interaction between body and mind is always present. It is only when adverse symptoms arise that you pay it any attention.
quotes.translation: 你们不必为近来困扰双方的轻微身体症状感到困惑。在我们的课程开始前，你们的反应——你们可能还记得——要激烈得多。身体与心灵的互动始终存在，只有当不良症状出现时，你们才会注意到它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The point is that past events grow. They are not finished. With that in mind, you can see that future lives are very difficult to explain from within your framework. A completed life in your terms is no more completed or done than any event. There is simply a cutoff point in your focus from your framework, but it is as artificial as, basically, perspective is applied to painting.
quotes.translation: 关键在于，过去的事件会成长。它们并未完结。记住这一点，你们就能明白，从你们的框架内解释来世非常困难。在你们看来，一个完成的生命并不比任何事件更完整或终结。从你们的框架来看，焦点只是被截断了，但这种截断本质上就像透视法应用于绘画一样人为。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (First, after holding session 805 we took a six-week break from sessions of any kind. We didn't plan to do this; it just developed, and we eventually realized that it did so through Jane's simple need for a change in routine. We had plenty of other things to do: I was still occupied daily with writing notes and appendixes for Volume 2 of "Unknown" Reality; on June 4 Jane received the page proofs for Cézanne, and began correcting them for the printer; on the 14th of the month "our" contractor began converting half of our garage into a writing room for Jane, and adding a large back porch [see the end of Note 2 for Session 801]. All of that building activity was much noisier and more disruptive than the work had been for the front porch, and forced some changes in our schedules, including more night work, as we manipulated around those distractions.

quotes.text: Give us a moment please... We find we do not believe that he is interested in the kind of relationship that you have in mind. He is somewhat sentimentally intrigued, but he is not about to change the framework of his days, and he is not resolute. He realizes that basically you expect what he cannot give. There will be sentiment. There will not be any basic long-term commitment.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……我们发现他不像你们所想的那样对建立关系感兴趣。他有些多愁善感地被吸引，但他不会改变自己的生活框架，而且他并不坚定。他意识到你们本质上期待他无法给予的东西。会有情感，但不会有任何基本的长期承诺。

quotes.text: It is as much camouflage as the glass on the table, （pointing） and its knowledge must come through the physical system. Now. The mind is uncamouflaged. It perceives the uncamouflaged reality of sense data, or its sees the energy that exists. It sees the energy independently of the physical object, you see.
quotes.translation: 它和桌上的玻璃杯一样是伪装（指向），其知识必须透过物质系统才能获得。现在。心智是不伪装的。它感知感官资料的不伪装实相，或看见存在的能量。它独立于物质客体看见能量，你明白吗。

quotes.text: States of mind are not permanent. Their reflections upon the system are not permanent, generally speaking, though they can be cumulative. It is much easier to pass through such periods if names are not given to symptoms, and many people pass through many so-called diseases without ever being aware of it.
quotes.translation: 心境并非永久不变。一般而言，它们对物质系统的反映也非永久，尽管可能累积。若不给予症状命名，就更容易度过这类时期，许多人经历许多所谓的疾病却从未察觉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Where matter would exist there, a concentration of energy forms what you could think of as a body form, body meaning mass, yet it would not be matter in your terms. Such manifestations in the electric field are much more powerful, in terms of intensity per mass unit, than anything you know. This is a very important point, which will come up with us again in the future. Now again: regardless of current scientific thought, there are at least three different kinds of electric force which your scientists have not yet discovered, and one of these has much to do with the intensity of thoughts as they are formed in the intangible mind, and translated to the physical brain and then into action, as the case may be.
quotes.translation: 在物质存在之处，能量凝聚形成你所谓的身形，身形意指质量，但并非你们认知中的物质。电场中的这类显化，就单位质量强度而言，远比你们所知的一切更强大。这是非常重要的一点，未来我们会再次提及。现在再说一次：不论当前科学观点如何，至少存在三种你们科学家尚未发现的电力，其中一种与无形心智中形成的思想强度密切相关，并视情况转化为物质大脑，进而付诸行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Pretend a particular event happened that greatly disturbed you. In your mind imagine it not simply wiped out, but replaced by another event of more beneficial nature. Now this must be done with great vividness and emotional validity, and many times. It is not a self-deception. The event that you choose will automatically be a probable event, which did in fact happen, though it is not the event you chose to perceive in your given probable past.
quotes.translation: 假设某件令你极度困扰的事件发生了。在脑海中想象它并非被简单抹除，而是被另一件更具建设性的事件取代。这必须带着极其鲜明的画面与情感真实性，反复进行多次。这不是自我欺骗。你选择的事件将自动成为确实发生的可能性事件，尽管它并非你在既定可能过去中选择感知的那件。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: These invisible structures preceded the emergence of the physical body. They also exist after the body's death. While the condition of the body is directed by the conscious mind in life, then, the idea or mental pattern for the body existed before the conscious mind's connection with the physical brain.
quotes.translation: 这些无形结构先于物质身体的出现而存在。它们也在身体死亡后继续存在。既然生前身体状态由意识心主导，那么身体的概念或心智模式在意识心与物质大脑连接之前就已存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Again, while the conscious mind is meant to direct the flow of your experience through your beliefs, and to materialize them, the actual mechanics are taken care of automatically by other portions of the self. You must indeed trust that your new beliefs will work as completely for you as your old ones.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，虽然意识心旨在通过信念引导经验流向并使其物质化，但实际运作机制由自我的其他部分自动处理。你确实必须相信，新信念会像旧信念一样完全为你运作。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You go where you want to go. Now. When your ordinary, conscious, waking mind is lulled in like your sleep state, you travel in other dimensions. You are already having experience within those other dimensions. You are preparing your own way. When you die, you go into those ways which you have prepared.
quotes.translation: 你前往你想去的地方。就在当下。当你平常、清醒的意识心智像睡眠状态般被安抚时，你便在其他维度中旅行。你早已在这些维度中体验着。你正在铺设自己的道路。当你死亡时，你便进入这些你已准备好的道路。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: As a rule you have enough difficulty dealing with the day's occurrences, much less next week's, and so in the sequence of events the reality of probable actions is usually hidden from your view. (Pause.) This more complex reality is an ever-existing property of your personal creaturehood. Beside this, in your terms you exist as a creature more than once. In each of your "reincarnational" existences you are faced with the same relationship with probabilities. In each case, also, the nature of the conscious mind sets up its own territory-of-identity (with hyphens) that it regards as its own. This provides a clear focus in which "present" action can be considered. These incarnations are all simultaneous.
quotes.translation: 通常，你处理日常事件已足够困难，更不用说下周的事了，因此在事件序列中，可能行动的现实通常隐藏在你的视野之外。（停顿）这种更复杂的现实是你个人存有中永恒存在的属性。除此之外，就你的时间而言，你作为存有不止存在一次。在你每一次"转世"存在中，你都面临与可能性相同的关联。在每种情况下，意识心智的本质也设定了它视为己有的身份领域（以连字符连接）。这提供了一个清晰的焦点，使"当下"的行动得以被考量。这些转世都是同时发生的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Usually there are a variety of physical actions, not involving killing, that would suffice. As long as you believe that violence must be met with violence you court it and its consequences. On individual terms, your own body and mind become the battleground, as does the physical body of the earth in mass terms. Your material form is alive through natural aggression, the poised, forceful and directed action that is the carrier for creativity.
quotes.translation: 通常有各种不涉及杀戮的身体行动足以解决问题。只要你相信暴力必须以暴力回应，你就在招引暴力及其后果。就个体而言，你的身体和心智成为战场，如同地球的物理身体在集体层面成为战场。你的物质形态通过自然的攻击性——那种平衡、有力且导向性的行动——而充满活力，这种行动是创造力的载体。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (Most intently:) When this happens, the species by default must fall back upon vestiges of old instincts -- that were not geared to operate in conjunction with a conscious reasoning mind, and do not comprehend your experience; that finds your "moment of reflection" an impertinent denial of impulse. So man loses full use of the animals' regulated, graceful instinct, and yet denies the conscious and emotional discrimination given him instead.
quotes.translation: （极其专注地：）当这种情况发生时，物种必然退回到古老本能的残余——这些本能并非为与有意识推理心智协同运作而设计，也不理解你的体验；它们认为你的"反思时刻"是对冲动的无礼否定。于是人类既无法充分运用动物那种有节律、优雅的本能，又否定了赋予他的有意识与情感辨别力。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: A belief must not possess. They were psychically adding to the unfortunate circumstances over which they argued, and both equally were at fault for this reason. A battle recreated in the mind reinforces the original psychic explosion and contemplation of war helps project that disaster into the future. To argue aggressively that war is wrong is ironic stupidity, for such an individual already displays those characteristics that lead to war.
quotes.translation: 信念不应占有。他们灵性上正在加剧他们争论的不幸境况，双方因此同样有错。在心智中重演的战斗强化了最初的心灵爆炸，而对战争的沉思则有助于将这场灾难投射到未来。激烈争论战争是错误的，这是讽刺的愚蠢，因为这样的个体已经展现出导致战争的那些特质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: In the beginning, then, these units operated both as identities or particles, and as waves. The main concentration was not yet physical in your terms. What you now think of as the dream state was the waking one, for it was still the recognized form of purposeful activity, creativity, and power. The dream state continues to be a connective between the two realities, and as a species you literally learned to walk by first being sleepwalkers. You walked in your sleep. You dreamed your languages. You spoke in your dreams and later wrote down the alphabets--and your knowledge and your intellect have always been fired, sharpened, propelled by the great inner reality from which your minds emerged.
quotes.translation: 起初，这些单位既作为身份或粒子运作，也作为波运作。主要聚集尚未以你们术语中的物质形式存在。你现在所谓的梦境状态曾是清醒状态，因为它仍是被认可的有目的活动、创造力与力量的形式。梦境状态继续作为两种现实之间的连接，而作为一个物种，你们实际上是通过先成为梦游者才学会行走的。你们在睡眠中行走。你们梦见自己的语言。你们在梦中说话，后来写下字母表——而你们的知识与智力始终被那伟大的内在现实所点燃、磨砺、推动，你们的心智正是从中涌现。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Irregardless of our particular interests, I look forward to long conversations with you on less formal topics... like yourself, I am independent. We have many fields of common interests... the personality should always be considered in an elemental way as patterns of action. It is never stable. When you attempt to tamper with various levels you change it. When you crack an egg to discover what is inside you ruin the egg. There are other ways to get inside the egg, and they are not too difficult to do... you have the sort of mind, Dr. Instream, that will be of great benefit to us here. We do not need a hammer to crack the eggshell. I am an egghead, but I do not need a hammer to be cracked.
quotes.translation: 无论我们各自关注什么领域，我都期待能与你进行更多非正式话题的长谈……就像你一样，我独立自主。我们有许多共同的兴趣领域……人格始终应被视为行动模式的基本元素。它从不稳定。当你试图篡改不同层面时，你便改变了它。当你敲开鸡蛋想看看里面有什么时，你毁掉了鸡蛋。还有其他方法可以进入鸡蛋内部，而且并不太难做到……因斯壮博士，你拥有那种对我们这里大有裨益的心智。我们不需要锤子来敲碎蛋壳。我是个书呆子，但我不需要锤子来敲开自己。

quotes.text: In those terms overpopulation is a violation. In the cases of both war and of overgrowth, the species has ignored its natural guilt. When a man kills another, regardless of his other beliefs a certain portion of his conscious mind is always aware of the violation involved, justify it though he may.
quotes.translation: 就那些术语而言，人口过剩是一种侵犯。在战争和过度繁衍这两种情况下，该物种都忽视了其自然的内疚感。当一个人杀死另一个人时，无论他有何种其他信仰，其意识心的某一部分总是清楚这其中的侵犯行为，尽管他可能会为之辩护。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: A moment later the line from his poem came to him, and he made the proper connection. The conscious mind was disturbed for a moment but it assimilated the data. The meaning of the light will become even clearer through Ruburt's dreams,* the intuitive continuation of the poem, and physical example.
quotes.translation: 片刻之后，他想起自己诗中的那句诗，并做出了恰当的连接。意识心一时受到干扰，但它吸收了这些数据。那光的意义将通过鲁柏的梦境、诗歌的直觉延续以及物质实例变得更加清晰。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: These are kept separate in various systems of actuality, while still combining in an overall fashion. You understand the cause-and-effect kind of order, but this is built upon the noncausal aspect of significances. In a way the dreams that you recall are like numbered paintings, tailored to fit your own intents and purposes, fitting the contours of your mind so perfectly that you forget the larger experiences from which they were drawn.
quotes.translation: 这些在不同的实相系统中被分开保存，同时仍以整体方式结合。你理解因果式的秩序，但这建立在意义非因果性面向之上。在某种程度上，你回忆起的梦境就像编号的绘画，被裁剪得恰好符合你自己的意图和目的，如此完美地贴合你心智的轮廓，以至于你忘记了它们所源自的更宏大体验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The experience, the subjective universe, the "mind" of All That Is, was so brilliant, so distinct, that All That Is almost became lost, mentally wandering within this ever-flourishing, ever-growing interior landscape. Each thought, feeling, dream, or mood was itself indelibly marked with all of the attributes of this infinite subjectivity. Each glowed and quivered with its own creativity, its own desire to create as it had been created.
quotes.translation: 那体验、那主观宇宙、那一切万有的"心智"，是如此璀璨、如此独特，以至于一切万有几乎迷失其中，心智漫游在这片永远繁茂、永远生长的内在风景里。每一个念头、感受、梦境或情绪，都不可磨灭地烙印着这无限主观性的所有属性。每一个都因自身的创造力而闪烁颤动，因自身被创造后想要创造的欲望而闪烁颤动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Despite this, I'm aware of the fact that I was necessary to the production of Seth's book. He needs my ability with words; even, I think, my turn of mind. Certainly my writing training aids in the translation of his material and helps give it form, no matter how unconsciously this is done. Certain personality characteristics are important too, I imagine -- the agility with which I can switch the focus of my consciousness, for example.
quotes.translation: 尽管如此，我意识到自己对赛斯书的产生是必要的。他需要我的文字能力；甚至，我想，需要我的心智倾向。当然，我的写作训练有助于翻译他的资料，并帮助赋予其形式，无论这过程多么无意识。某些人格特质也很重要，我想——比如我能够灵活切换意识焦点的能力。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth began dictating the book in our next Session 511, January 21, 1970, and finished it in Session 591, August 11, 1971. The intervening sessions did not all involve book dictation, however. Some were devoted to personal matters, some given for specific people who needed help, and some were in answer to philosophical questions not connected with the book. I also took several "little vacations." Despite such layoffs, Seth always picked right up, precisely where he left off dictation.

quotes.text: If this happens, all kinds of old and new religious denominations will war, and all kinds of ideologies surface. This need not take place, for the conscious mind -- basically, now -- having learned to focus in physical terms, is meant to expand, to accept unconscious intuitions and knowledge, and to organize these deeply creative principles into cultural patterns.
quotes.translation: 若此事发生，各种新旧教派将相互征战，各种意识形态也将浮出水面。这并非必然发生，因为意识心——本质上，现在——既已学会聚焦于物质层面，本应扩展以接纳无意识的直觉与知识，并将这些深具创造力的原理组织成文化模式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: If -- now, a brief innocuous-enough example -- you meet an individual often enough and think, "He gives me a pain in the neck," it is surely no coincidence that you find yourself with a painful neck in future encounters with this person. The suggestion is quite a conscious one, however (emphatically), given by yourself and carried out not symbolically but most practically, most literally. In other words, the conscious mind gives its orders and the inner self carries them out.
quotes.translation: 若——现在举个简短无害的例子——你常常见到某人并心想"他让我脖子疼"，日后与这人相遇时发现自己果真脖子疼痛，这绝非巧合。然而（着重强调），这个暗示完全是你有意识给出的，且执行方式并非象征性，而是最实际、最字面的。换言之，意识心下达指令，内在自我便予以执行。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: New paragraph: This is, however, a clear case of the interweaving of probabilities. In this one Joseph can choose whether to buy or not, so there is no coercion (by Stella Butts), for example. Joseph and Ruburt were also shown a second house in Sayre -- one a good deal cheaper, but generally much like the one in which Joseph's mother lived in this life. They saw both houses on the same day. The second, like the first, was for sale because of age. An elderly couple recently moved from the second house to a home for the aged. Again, the "official" mind says, "Coincidence. All of this is quite natural: Many homes are for sale because the elderly can care for them no longer."
quotes.translation: 新段落：但这显然是概率交织的典型案例。在此例中，约瑟可以选择买或不买，因此（斯特拉·巴茨）并无强迫之意。约瑟和鲁柏还看了塞尔的第二栋房子——价格便宜得多，但整体与约瑟母亲今生所住房屋颇为相似。他们同一天看了这两栋房子。第二栋与第一栋相同，皆因屋主年迈而出售。一对老夫妇刚搬离第二栋房子住进养老院。同样，"官方"心会说："巧合。这一切都很自然：许多房屋因老人无力照管而出售。"

quotes.text: (One must exert the downward pressure in a manner directed away from the body while experimenting, in order to maintain the balance and center of gravity, and at least when experimenting this necessity to push down and away from oneself is quite noticeable. When the table is performing well it seems that a direct downward pressure, applied to the edge of the tabletop, is required to overcome the force. The direction of this downward push is quite different, we believe at this stage, from the experimental down and away thrust. But more study is required here, and with this knowledge in mind, we will try to more accurately assess the table's performance at future sessions.
quotes.translation: （实验时必须朝远离身体的方向施力下压，以维持平衡与重心；至少在实验时，这种向下推离自身的必要性相当明显。当桌子表现良好时，似乎需要直接向下按压桌面边缘才能克服这股力量。我们认为在此阶段，这种向下推压的方向与实验时向下推离的动作截然不同。但此处需要更多研究，带着这份认知，我们将在未来聚会中更精确地评估桌子的表现。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: It's not unusual that students should dream of Seth, of course, or that they should dream of me. But certainly Seth has achieved independent status in their eyes and has become a vehicle of instruction even in the dream state. In other words, besides producing the continuing Seth material and this book, Seth has entered the minds and consciousness of many people.
quotes.translation: 学生梦见赛斯或梦见我本不足为奇。但赛斯确实在他们眼中获得了独立地位，甚至在梦境中也成为教导的载体。换言之，除了持续产出赛斯资料和本书之外，赛斯已进入许多人的心智与意识之中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth began dictating the book in our next Session 511, January 21, 1970, and finished it in Session 591, August 11, 1971. The intervening sessions did not all involve book dictation, however. Some were devoted to personal matters, some given for specific people who needed help, and some were in answer to philosophical questions not connected with the book. I also took several "little vacations." Despite such layoffs, Seth always picked right up, precisely where he left off dictation.

quotes.text: The invisible environment within your mind is not as lonely as you might think, and your seeming inner isolation is caused by the ego's persistent guard. It sees no reason, for example, why you should be informed of information that it does not consider pertinent to day-by-day daily activity.
quotes.translation: 你心智中那无形的环境并不如想象中孤独，你表面上的内在孤立实因自我持续警戒所致。例如，它认为你没有理由获知那些它认为与日常活动无关的信息。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the dreaming state, then, the needs and desires of families, communities and countries are well known. The dream state serves as a rich source for the world's knowledge, and is also therefore responsible for the outgrowth of its technology. This is a highly important point, for "the technological world out there" was at one time the world of dreams. The discoveries and inventions that made the industrial world possible were always latent in man's mind, and represented an inner glittering landscape of probability that he brought into actualization through the use of dreams--the intuitive and the conscious manipulation of material that was at one time latent.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，家庭、社区和国家的需求与欲望都清晰可见。梦境状态作为世界知识的丰富源泉，也因此催生了技术的蓬勃发展。这是一个极其重要的观点，因为"外在的技术世界"曾一度是梦境的世界。使工业世界成为可能的发现与发明，始终潜藏于人类心智之中，代表着一种内在的璀璨概率景观——人类通过运用梦境（对曾潜藏材料的直觉与有意识操控）将其转化为现实。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: For the framework is the same framework in which you have your physical existence. We will make you investigators even in your sleep; and you can see now how your very investigations are bound to change the nature of your dreams themselves. Only in this case nothing will be lost, and much gained. It is unfortunate that medical men who deal with the mind as a specialty have not thought of some of these connections more thoroughly.
quotes.translation: 因为这一架构与你们拥有物质存在的架构是相同的。我们甚至会让你们在睡眠中成为探索者；现在你们可以看到，你们的探索本身必然如何改变梦境自身的本质。只是在这种情况下，不会有所损失，反而会收获良多。遗憾的是，那些以心智为专业的医学人士并未更深入地思考这些关联。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Its beliefs about the nature of reality are then given to inner portions of the self. These rely mainly upon the conscious mind's interpretation of temporal reality. The conscious mind sets the goals and the inner self brings them about, using all its facilities and inexhaustible energy.
quotes.translation: 它对现实本质的信念随后被传递给自我的内在部分。这些部分主要依赖意识心对时间性现实的诠释。意识心设定目标，而内在自我则运用其所有能力与无穷能量来实现这些目标。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Continue your combined prayer periods. During the time have Ruburt concentrate upon all those other functions of body, organ and mind, that are in excellent condition, quite obviously filled with vitality. This automatically concentrates his attention upon health, which is what you want.
quotes.translation: 继续你们的联合祈祷时段。在此期间，让鲁柏专注于身体、器官与心智中所有其他功能——这些功能显然充满活力、状态极佳。这会自动将他的注意力集中在健康上，而这正是你们所期望的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: This vastvoid, this infinite mind, came out of another that was greater than itself. (Pause, smile.) The possibilities that have come to reality within this universal system have each given birth to other systems and other realities, as one tree bears a thousand seeds. (Pause.) you yourselves through your own mental actions create realities of which you yourselves are unaware, and you give birth to more than physical children.
quotes.translation: 这浩瀚的虚空，这无限的心智，源自另一个比它自身更伟大的存在。（停顿，微笑。）在这个宇宙系统中已化为现实的可能性，每一个都孕育出其他系统与其他现实，正如一棵树结出千粒种子。（停顿。）你们自身通过自己的心智活动创造出连你们自己都未曾察觉的现实，你们所孕育的远不止是物质层面的子女。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: The living picture of the world grows within the mind. The world as it appears to you is like a three-dimensional painting in which each individual takes a hand. Each color, each line that appears within it has first been painted within a mind, and only then does it materialize without.
quotes.translation: 世界的鲜活图景在心智中生长。你们所看到的世界如同一幅三维画作，每个人都在其中参与创作。其中出现的每一种色彩、每一条线条，都首先在心智中被描绘出来，然后才在外部世界中具象化。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now as far as the species is concerned, all variations are necessary--and it is as if (underlined) in one instance a member of the species--for its own reasons, but also on behalf of the whole--decides to specialize in one particular area, to isolate certain abilities, so to speak, and display them with the greatest tenacity and brilliance, while nearly completely ignoring certain other areas. In your society, however, the capacities of the reasoning mind have been considered in opposition to the intuitive abilities, so that your ideas of what a person is or should be largely ignore the idea of emotional achievement, emotional understanding.
quotes.translation: 就物种而言，所有变异都是必要的——仿佛（强调）物种中的某个成员，出于自身原因，也代表整体，决定专精于某个特定领域，可以说，隔离某些能力，以最大的韧性和辉煌展现它们，同时几乎完全忽略其他某些领域。然而，在你们的社会中，理性心智的能力一直被视为与直觉能力相对立，以至于你们对一个人是什么或应该是什么的看法，很大程度上忽略了情感成就、情感理解的概念。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Ruburt has allowed a portion of his this-life consciousness to go off on a tangent, so to speak, on another path into another system of actuality (i.e., into his psychic library). His life there is as valid as his existence in your world. In the waking state he is able, now, to alter the direction of his focus precisely enough to bring about a condition in which he perceives both realities simultaneously. He is just beginning, so as yet he is only occasionally conscious of that other experience. He is, however, aware of it now in the back of his mind more or less constantly. It does not intrude upon the world that he knows, but enriches it.
quotes.translation: 可以说，鲁柏允许他此生意识的一部分偏离正轨，沿着另一条路径进入另一个实相系统（即进入他的心灵图书馆）。他在那里的生活与他在你们世界的存在同样真实。在清醒状态下，他现在能够足够精确地改变他的焦点方向，从而引发一种同时感知两种实相的状态。他刚刚开始，因此目前只是偶尔意识到那种其他体验。然而，他现在或多或少持续地在脑海深处意识到它。它并不侵扰他所知的世界，反而丰富了它。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 715
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You may have dreams urging you to move in such and such a direction, or pointing out areas in which corrections should be made. Often such dreams bring about behavior changes whether or not you remember them in the morning. You may request dreams in which proper direction is given, and you will receive them. If you ask on the one hand, however, and do not believe in the therapeutic nature of dreams on the other, you will short-circuit any such activity. In such a case you are not being honest with the contents of your conscious mind. Instead you are saying, "I will have a dream to help me, and yet I do not believe I can have such a dream."
quotes.translation: 你可能会有梦，敦促你朝某个方向前进，或指出需要修正的领域。通常，无论你早上是否记得，这样的梦都会带来行为上的改变。你可以请求给予正确方向的梦，并且你会收到它们。然而，如果你一方面请求，另一方面却不相信梦的治疗性质，你就会短路任何此类活动。在这种情况下，你没有诚实地面对你意识心智的内容。相反，你在说：“我会有一个梦来帮助我，但我不相信我能有这样的梦。”
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Even the duration of an event or object in space or time is determined by the intensity of the thoughts or emotions that gave it birth. Duration in space is not the same as duration in time, however, though it may seem that this is the case. I am speaking in your terms now. An event or object that exists briefly in space may have a much greater duration in time. It may have far greater importance and intensity, existing in your memory, for example, long after it has disappeared in space. Such an event or object does not merely exist symbolically within your mind or memory -- but in your terms its actual reality continues as a time event.
quotes.translation: 甚至事件或物体在空间或时间中的持续时间，也由产生它的思想或情感的强度决定。然而，空间中的持续时间与时间中的持续时间并不相同，尽管看起来可能如此。我现在用你们的术语来说。一个在空间中短暂存在的事件或物体，可能在时间中具有更长的持续时间。它可能具有远为重要的强度和重要性，例如，在它从空间消失很久之后，仍存在于你的记忆中。这样的事件或物体不仅仅象征性地存在于你的心智或记忆中——而是，用你们的术语来说，它的实际实相作为时间事件继续存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Television interacts with your lives, but it does not cause your lives. It does not cause the events that it depicts. With your great belief in technology, it often seems to many people that television causes violence, for example, or that it causes a love of overmaterialism, or that it causes "loose morals." Television reflects. In a manner of speaking it does not even distort, though it may reflect distortions. The writers and actors of television dramas are attuned to the "mass mind." They are not leaders or followers. They are creative reflectors, acutely aware of the overall, generalized emotional and psychic patterns of the age.
quotes.translation: 电视与你们的生活互动，但它并不导致你们的生活。它并不导致它所描绘的事件。由于你们对技术的巨大信念，例如，许多人常常认为电视导致暴力，或导致对过度物质主义的热爱，或导致“道德沦丧”。电视反映。可以说，它甚至不扭曲，尽管它可能反映扭曲。电视剧的编剧和演员与“大众心智”相协调。他们不是领导者或追随者。他们是创造性的反映者，敏锐地意识到这个时代整体的、普遍的情感和心灵模式。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 815, DECEMBER 17, 1977
9:22 P.M. SATURDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: Now because your conscious mind, as you think of it, is not aware of these activities, you do not identify with this inner portion of yourselves. You prefer to identify with the part of you who watches television or cooks or works -- the part you think knows what it is doing. But this seemingly unconscious portion of yourself is far more knowledgeable, and upon its smooth functioning your entire physical existence depends.
quotes.translation: 现在，因为你们所认为的意识心智并不知晓这些活动，所以你们不认同自己的这个内在部分。你们更愿意认同那个看电视、做饭或工作的那部分自己——那个你们认为知道自己正在做什么的部分。但你们这个看似无意识的部分要博学得多，你们整个物质存在都依赖于它的平稳运作。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is much more natural to remember your dreams than not to remember them. It is presently in the vogue to say that the conscious mind, as you consider it, deals with survival. It deals with survival only insofar as it promotes survival in your particular kind of society. In those terms, if you remembered your dreams, and if you benefited consciously from that knowledge, even your physical survival would be better assured.
quotes.translation: 记住梦境远比不记得要自然得多。目前流行一种说法，认为你们所认为的意识心只处理生存问题。它只在促进你们特定社会形态的生存时才处理生存问题。就这方面而言，如果你记得梦境，并有意识地受益于那些知识，连你的物质生存也会得到更好的保障。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Many individuals are afraid that they will be swept away by inner explorations, that insanity will overtake them, when instead the physical stance of the body and personality is firmly rooted in these alternate organizations. There is nothing wrong with the conscious mind. You have simply put a lid on it, allowing it to be only so conscious, and no more. You have said: "Here it is safe to be conscious, and here it is not."
quotes.translation: 许多人害怕会被内在探索所淹没，害怕疯狂会降临，而实际上身体与人格的物质立场正是牢牢扎根于这些交替的组织中。意识心本身并无问题。你只是给它加了个盖子，只允许它意识到那么多，不能再多。你说："在这里保持意识是安全的，在那里则不然。"
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Man has been endowed, and has endowed himself, with a conscious mind to direct the nature, shape and form of his creations. All deep aspirations and unconscious motivations, all unspoken drives, rise up for the approval or disapproval of the conscious mind, and await its direction.
quotes.translation: 人类被赋予——也自我赋予了——意识心，用以引导其创造的本质、形状与形式。所有深切的渴望与无意识动机，所有未说出口的驱力，都升起来接受意识心的认可或否定，并等待它的指引。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Some people believe that they must be punished, and so they seek [out] unfortunate circumstances. They [go] to one event after another in which they meet retribution. They may seek out areas of the country in which natural disasters are frequent, or their behavior may be such that they attract from other people reactions of an explosive kind. Often, however, individuals use disasters quite for their own purposes, as an exteriorized force that brings their lives into clear focus. Some may be flirting with the idea of death, and choose a dramatic encounter with nature in the final act. Others change their minds at the last moment.
quotes.translation: 有些人相信自己必须受到惩罚，于是他们寻求不幸的境遇。他们接连遭遇一个又一个事件，在其中承受报应。他们可能选择自然灾害频发的地区，或者他们的行为会招致他人爆发性的反应。然而，人们常常为了自己的目的而利用灾难，将其作为外在化的力量，使生活焦点清晰。有些人可能正与死亡念头调情，在最后一幕中选择与自然戏剧性地相遇。另一些人则在最后一刻改变了主意。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 821, FEBRUARY 20, 1978
9:30 P.M. MONDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: There are alterations taken into your calculations, so astronauts know ahead of time that they can expect to encounter weightlessness, for example. Your ideas and experiences with space and matter, however, are determined by your own sense apparatus. What is matter to you might be "empty space" for beings equipped in an entirely different fashion. Your conscious mind as you understand it is the "psychological structure" that deals with conditions on a physical basis. Sense data are served up, so to speak, more or less already packaged. The greater inner reality of the psyche, however, is as extensive as outer space seems to be.
quotes.translation: 计算中已纳入各种变化，例如宇航员事先知道会遭遇失重。然而，你对空间与物质的观念与体验，是由你自己的感官装置决定的。对你而言是物质的东西，对以完全不同方式装备的存在而言可能是"空的空间"。你所理解的意识心，是处理物质基础上条件的"心理结构"。可以说，感官数据或多或少已经包装好才被呈递上来。然而，心灵的内在实相之广阔，正如外在空间所显现的那样。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Fire has one meaning if you are afraid of it, another if you consider it a source of warmth; and either of these two meanings will also be colored by any of the endless variations of personal events that any individual might have encountered with it. Your own knowledge of dream symbols and their personal meaning is so opaque simply because you are not used to examining them with your conscious mind. You have been taught that it cannot understand. The great interconnections between waking and dreaming experience then escape you. You do not realize the many physical problems that are solved for you, and by you, in your dreams.
quotes.translation: 火若令你恐惧，它便有一种意义；若你视其为温暖之源，它又有另一种意义；而这两种意义还会被任何个人可能与之相遇的、无穷变化的个人事件所渲染。你对自己梦境符号及其个人意义的了解如此模糊，仅仅是因为你不习惯用意识心来检视它们。你被教导说意识心无法理解。于是，清醒与梦境体验之间的伟大关联便从你眼前溜走。你未能意识到，有多少物质问题是在梦境中为你——也由你——所解决的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: At this time of your lives it is important that you act. I am telling you that of the [Elmira] houses in your mind it really makes little difference which one you choose. Neither is perfect. You would find yourselves quite hampered in [any] idealistically perfect environment. You need some give-and-take. Either place could well be made to suit your specific needs, and each reflects strong elements of your personalities.
quotes.translation: 在你们人生的这个阶段，行动至关重要。我告诉你们，在你们心中的[埃尔迈拉]房屋中，选择哪一栋其实差别不大。没有一栋是完美的。你们会发现，在[任何]理想化的完美环境中，自己反而会受到很大束缚。你们需要一些相互妥协。两个地方都能很好地满足你们的特定需求，并且各自反映了你们个性的强烈元素。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 738

quotes.text: (Another one-minute pause.) Give us a moment ... The "unknown" reality sustains you and the web of life as you understand it. Your conscious concepts must enlarge so that the conscious self can understand its true nature. As you think of it, consciousness is barely -- barely -- half developed. It has learned to identify with one small group of neurologically accepted responses. Portions of the brain not used lie latent, waiting for the recognition that will trigger them into activity (intently). When this happens, the mind will become aware of the rich bed of probabilities upon which the ego now rides so blindly.
quotes.translation: （又停顿了一分钟。）给我们一点时间……“未知”的现实支撑着你们，以及你们所理解的生命之网。你们的意识概念必须扩展，以便意识自我能够理解其真实本质。按照你们的想法，意识几乎——几乎——只开发了一半。它学会了认同于一小群神经学上可接受的反应。大脑中未使用的部分处于潜伏状态，等待着能够触发它们活动的认知（专注地）。当这发生时，心智将意识到那丰富的可能性之床，而自我目前正如此盲目地骑乘其上。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: In your terms, until now your consciousness has specialized in neurological patterning. As mentioned (in Session 682), this was extremely important while it learned the art of specialized focus. Now, however, it must begin to recognize that it can indeed expand, and bring into its awareness other quite legitimate realities. The nature of probabilities must be understood, for the time has come in the world as you experience it where the greatest wisdom and discrimination are needed. Your consciousness and neurological prejudice blind you to the full dimension of physical activity. The true implications of physical action are not as yet apparent to you.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: You could have chosen not to develop these latent abilities at all. You could have developed other latent abilities in their stead. You settled upon your particular life situation with certain problems and challenges in mind. In helping Ruburt free and use his own strong spontaneous nature, you also free your own spontaneous self. That particular problem then is also a challenge and a way of development for you both.
quotes.translation: 你们本可以完全选择不去发展这些潜在能力。你们本可以发展其他潜在能力来替代它们。你们选择了特定的生活情境，心中带着某些问题和挑战。在帮助鲁柏释放并运用他自身强大的自发性本质时，你们也释放了自己的自发性自我。因此，那个特定问题对你们两人来说，既是一个挑战，也是一种发展途径。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: When you create a poem or a song or a painting you are in a state of play, of enjoyment, of freedom. You intend to make something different, to produce a new version of reality. You create out of love, for the sake of the experience. At one time or another almost everyone has that kind of experience, but children have it often. They compose songs and music and paintings in their heads. They alter the focus of their consciousnesses frequently. They do not stop to ask whether or not the play is real or pertinent. Physically, play develops their body mechanisms. It also flexes the great capabilities of their minds.
quotes.translation: 当你们创作一首诗、一首歌或一幅画时，你们处于一种玩耍、享受和自由的状态。你们意图创造一些不同的东西，产生现实的新版本。你们出于爱而创造，为了体验本身。几乎每个人在某个时候都有过这种体验，但孩子们经常有。他们在脑海中创作歌曲、音乐和绘画。他们频繁地改变意识的焦点。他们不会停下来质疑玩耍是否真实或相关。从生理上讲，玩耍发展了他们的身体机制。它也锻炼了他们心智的巨大能力。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The brain seeks the richest form of an event. I am speaking specifically of the brain, as separated from the mind, to emphasize the point that these abilities are of creaturehood. The brain's genius comes from the mind, which can be called the brain's biophysical counterpart.
quotes.translation: 大脑寻求事件最丰富的形态。我特意将大脑与心智区分开来谈论，是为了强调这些能力是生物性的。大脑的天赋来自心智，心智可以被称为大脑的生物物理对应物。

quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The ego is not as a rule aware of this broader time experience. The subconscious however is so aware, and the associative processes of the mind can, and do, react to a future event, while the ego is closed off to it. Therefore it is possible for Frederick, this year, to become ill at the smell of a particular perfume because subconsciously he knows that in 1980 his mother will be wearing that perfume when she dies.
quotes.translation: 自我通常意识不到这种更广泛的时间体验。然而潜意识是意识到的，并且心智的联想过程能够、也确实会对未来事件做出反应，而自我对此是封闭的。因此，弗雷德里克今年有可能因为闻到某种特定香水的气味而生病，因为他在潜意识中知道，1980年他母亲去世时会穿着那种香水。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: For such material to be made meaningful to the conscious personality, a training of abilities along the lines of interpretation is definitely required in most cases. Such material is a part of what we termed the initial experience, which is of electrical nature, received through the mind, broken down there as explained, and then received by the brain where it is changed or interpreted to psychological symbol form.
quotes.translation: 为使这类材料对意识人格具有意义，在大多数情况下，确实需要按照解读方向训练能力。这类材料是我们所称的初始经验的一部分，具有电性本质，通过心智接收，按前述方式在其中分解，然后由大脑接收，在那里被转化或解读为心理符号形式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: "Somehow, without being able to see them, I knew that stone or clay steps rose up the back of the tower, clear to the top where the soldier posed. He didn't move. Try as I would, I couldn't make his image any clearer or closer, or induce it to change in any other manner. What I did perceive was remarkably steady and lasted for several minutes, at least. I can still summon it to my mind's eye when I want to. It came to me that the soldier was 43 years old and had two male children -- where they were, I didn't know. Like an echo in the background lingered a woman, but I couldn't get anything about her.
quotes.translation: "不知怎的，虽然看不见它们，我却知道石阶或泥阶沿着塔背向上延伸，直达士兵伫立的顶端。他一动不动。无论我如何努力，都无法让他的形象更清晰或更接近，也无法促使其以任何其他方式改变。我所感知到的异常稳定，至少持续了几分钟。如今我仍能在需要时将其召唤至心灵之眼。我忽然意识到这名士兵43岁，有两个儿子——他们在哪里，我不得而知。背景中隐约萦绕着一个女人，但我无法获取关于她的任何信息。

quotes.text: Try to experience all of your present sense data as fully as you can. This tones your entire physical and psychic organism, bringing all of your perceptions together so that your awareness opens fully. Body and mind operate together. You experience an immediate sense of power because your abilities are directed to the fullest of their capacities. In a physical moment you can act directly on the spot, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 请尽可能充分地体验你当前所有的感官数据。这会调谐你整个身体与心灵有机体，将所有感知统合起来，使你的觉知完全敞开。身体与心智协同运作。你会立即感受到力量感，因为你的能力被导向其最大潜能。可以说，在物理瞬间，你能当场直接行动。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In such circumstances, there are not the great artificial divisions created between the two states of consciousness. The conscious mind is better able to remember and assimilate its dreaming experience, and in dreams the self can use its waking experience more efficiently.
quotes.translation: 在此类情境下，两种意识状态之间不会产生人为的割裂。意识心智能更好地记住并吸收其梦境体验，而在梦中，自我也能更有效地运用其清醒体验。

quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The patterns evolve according to certain laws. They merely reflect the ideas behind them, and these ideas you must realize originate from different sources. They originate in the subconscious, it is true, but before this an idea quality is received by the inner senses. Sometimes this idea quality is received as intuition, where it sparks into the conscious mind. But the conscious ego is the primary manipulator of camouflage patterns and the obvious mover. The actual material from which camouflage patterns are formed is the vitality which exists and which is unconsciously used by your personalities.
quotes.translation: 模式依据特定法则演化。它们仅反映其背后的理念，而你必须认识到这些理念源自不同来源。它们确实源于潜意识，但在此之前，一种理念特质已被内在感官接收。有时这种理念特质作为直觉被接收，它会闪现进入意识心智。但意识自我是伪装模式的主要操控者与显性推动者。构成伪装模式的实际材料是存在的活力，它被你们的人格无意识地运用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Give us a moment.... The out-of-body was the result of a change in attitude, and made possible also because of his (Ruburt's) body's relaxation. He was exercising his consciousness, allowing himself greater freedom. A certain portion of your mind was drifting at the time.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……出体体验是态度转变的结果，同时也因他（鲁柏）身体的放松而成为可能。他正在锻炼自己的意识，允许自己获得更大自由。当时你心智的某部分正在游离。

quotes.text: At the levels with which we are concerned, the body must primarily react to present, immediate, primary existence in space and time. At other levels it is equipped to handle many kinds of data, in that I have mentioned before the precognition of cells. But the body depends on the conscious mind to give it a clear assessment of precise conditions of the space and time it occupies. It depends upon that knowledge.
quotes.translation: 在我们所关注的层面上，身体必须首先对时空中的当下、直接、基本的存在做出反应。在其他层面上，它具备处理多种数据的能力，因为我之前提到过细胞的预知能力。但身体依赖意识心为其提供所占据时空确切状况的清晰评估。它依赖这种知识。

quotes.text: They will then think that there is no real comparison to be made between the two, since each individual dream world would be a conglomeration of diverse individualistic symbols, even if they were projected into a type of universe of which the conscious mind was ignorant.
quotes.translation: 然后他们会认为两者之间没有真正的可比性，因为每个个体的梦境世界都是各种个性化符号的集合体，即使它们被投射到意识心所不知的某种宇宙中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: So the inner senses and the subconscious can do the same thing as far as inner space, and what you would call inner time, is concerned. But this is not amazing, far from it. It only seems strange because you are so familiar with your precious camouflage patterns. Time and space, dear friends, are both camouflage patterns, therefore the fact that the inner senses can conquer time and space is not, after all, so surprising. To the mind with its subconscious, and to the inner senses, there is no time and space, and therefore to them nothing is conquered. The camouflage is simply not present.
quotes.translation: 因此，就内在空间以及你们所谓的内在时间而言，内在感官和潜意识也能做到同样的事情。但这并不令人惊奇，远非如此。它之所以显得奇怪，只是因为你们太熟悉自己宝贵的伪装模式了。亲爱的朋友们，时间和空间都是伪装模式，因此内在感官能够征服时间和空间这一事实，毕竟并不那么令人惊讶。对于拥有潜意识的意识心以及内在感官而言，没有时间和空间，因此对它们来说，没有什么被征服。伪装根本不存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Alice. (Pause.) The name Grossman also comes to mind, but I do not know the connection. (Cecile Grossman was an editor at Prentice Hall then.) Mossman has an uncle, I believe, of whom he is fond, or was fond. The name Albert comes to mind. A 1962 engagement or marriage.
quotes.translation: 爱丽丝。（停顿。）格罗斯曼这个名字也浮现在脑海中，但我不知道其中的关联。（塞西尔·格罗斯曼当时是普伦蒂斯·霍尔的编辑。）我相信莫斯曼有一个叔叔，他很喜欢，或者曾经很喜欢。阿尔伯特这个名字浮现在脑海中。1962年的一次订婚或结婚。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: I have often stated that the mind-body relationship is one system. The thoughts are as necessary to the whole system as the body's cells are. Ruburt correctly interpreted an analogy I gave him in which I compared thoughts to individual cells, and belief systems to the physical organs, which are composed of cells. The organs obviously are stationary in the body, though the cells within them die and are reborn.
quotes.translation: 我经常说，心身关系是一个系统。思想对整个系统来说，就像身体细胞一样不可或缺。鲁柏正确地解释了我给他的一个类比，其中我将思想比作个体细胞，将信念系统比作由细胞组成的身体器官。器官显然在身体中是固定的，尽管其中的细胞会死亡和重生。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (Heartily:) What a transformation! Its volcano, it finds, now gives birth to soil and pollen, its excitement roused in a million different ways. It meets the spirit of the first island that has been living there, and says: "What a change! I would like a still more spectacular display. The flowers are not nearly colorful or wild enough. It is, if you will forgive me, too well-tamed -- yet all in all you've done wonders. Now, however, I'd like a cultural interchange with others still unknown; and if you don't mind I wish you'd go home. (Whispering:) This is, after all, me, and my land."
quotes.translation: （热情地：）多么大的转变啊！它发现，它的火山现在孕育出土壤和花粉，它的兴奋以百万种不同的方式被激发。它遇到了一直居住在那里的第一个岛屿的灵体，说道："多么大的变化！我想要更壮观的展示。花朵还不够鲜艳或狂野。如果你能原谅我，这太驯服了——但总的来说，你创造了奇迹。然而现在，我想要与其他未知的存在进行文化交流；如果你不介意，我希望你回家。（低语：）毕竟，这是我，我的土地。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I see a page number, 373 I believe, and a passage brings to mind hooded figures. Connotations of the Middle Ages, and of a female with a headdress that is pointed, and then a veil. I do not know, perhaps a connection here with the heroine of an opera, or medieval novel. And still speaking of the passage, a room of medieval surroundings such as a castle. These are impressions connected with the page. （Pause.）
quotes.translation: 我看到一个页码，大概是373页，一段文字让我想起戴兜帽的身影。中世纪的联想，一个戴着尖顶头饰的女性，还有面纱。我不知道，也许和某部歌剧或中世纪小说的女主角有关。继续谈这段文字，一个中世纪环境的房间，比如城堡。这些都是与这页相关的印象。（停顿。）

quotes.text: An examination of your conscious thoughts will tell you much about the state of your inner mind, your intentions and expectations, and will often lead you to a direct confrontation with challenges and problems. Your thoughts, studied, will let you see where you are going. They point clearly to the nature of physical events. What exists physically exists first in thought and feeling. There is no other rule.
quotes.translation: 检视你有意识的念头，会告诉你许多关于你内在心智的状态、你的意图和期望，并常常引导你直接面对挑战和问题。研究你的念头，会让你看清自己正走向何方。它们清楚地指向物质事件的性质。物质存在的一切，首先存在于思想和感受中。没有其他规则。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Nevertheless the dream world, the mind, and the basic inner universe do exist ... in what we will call the value climate of psychological reality. This is the medium. This takes the place of what you call space. It is a quality which makes all existences and consciousness possible. It is one of the most powerful principles behind or within the vitality that itself composes from itself all other phenomena.3
quotes.translation: 然而，梦境世界、心智和基本的内在宇宙确实存在……存在于我们称之为心理实相的价值气候中。这是媒介。它取代了你所谓的空间。它是一种品质，使所有存在和意识成为可能。它是生命力背后或之内最强大的原则之一，而生命力本身从自身构成所有其他现象。
