quotes.text: Often you do not trust your imagination, considering that it deals with phenomena that cannot be called fact. Therefore you artificially form a situation in which overall traces must be made. If you are too imaginative, for example, you may not be able to adequately deal with physical life. This applies only in the cultural media in which you presently operate, however. Originally, and in your terms of time, it was precisely the imagination that in its own way set you apart from other creatures, enabling you to form realities in your mind that you could "later" exteriorize.
quotes.translation: 你常常不信任自己的想象力，认为它处理的是不能称为事实的现象。因此你人为地制造了一种情况，必须做出全面的痕迹。例如，如果你太富有想象力，你可能无法充分处理物质生活。然而，这只适用于你目前运作的文化媒介。最初，用你们的时间术语来说，正是想象力以其独特的方式将你与其他生物区分开来，使你能在心智中形成实相，然后“后来”将其外化。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "In the same way the scientific community speaks of grandiose ideals, of man's triumph over the planet and nature. At the same time these ideals further divorce the scientists from daily practical experience with their fellowman; and since they see animals as objects, they're bound to see human life in somewhat the same fashion. The sacrifice of, say, thousands of lives in a nuclear accident almost becomes justified in their minds if it is a means toward the grandiose goal of learning how to 'triumph over nature.' Again, this intent automatically turns them into mechanics.
quotes.translation: “同样地，科学界谈论着宏伟的理想，人类对地球和自然的胜利。同时，这些理想进一步使科学家与同胞的日常实际经验疏离；由于他们将动物视为物体，他们必然以类似的方式看待人类生命。例如，在核事故中牺牲数千条生命，在他们心中几乎变得合理，如果这是学习如何‘战胜自然’这一宏伟目标的手段。同样地，这种意图自动将他们变成了机械师。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Scientists realize that the atmosphere of the earth is a distortion, or has a distorting effect upon their instruments. What they do not understand is that their instruments themselves are bound to be distortive. This cannot be emphasized too strongly. Any material physical instrument will have built-in distortive effects. The one instrument which is more important than any other and which has given you, that is mankind, all its breakthroughs and advance is the brain. Or rather the mind which contains the brain, and which is the meeting place of the inner and outer senses.
quotes.translation: 科学家意识到地球大气层是一种扭曲，或对其仪器产生扭曲效应。他们不了解的是，他们的仪器本身必然具有扭曲性。这一点怎么强调都不为过。任何物质物理仪器都会有内置的扭曲效应。那个比其他任何仪器都重要，并给予你们，即人类，所有突破和进步的仪器，是大脑。或者更确切地说，是包含大脑的心智，它是内在与外在感官的交汇处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: In rudimentary form children's dreams also involve mathematical concepts, so that formal mathematical training falls on already fertile ground. The arts, sciences, agriculture -- all of these reflect natural contours and tendencies inherent in man's mind, as general rather than specific attributes emerging first in the dream state, and then sparking specialized intellectual tendencies in the waking state.
quotes.translation: 儿童梦境在雏形阶段也涉及数学概念，因此正规数学训练其实是建立在已然肥沃的土壤上。艺术、科学、农业——所有这些都映照出人类心智固有的自然轮廓与倾向，这些作为普遍而非特定的属性，首先在梦境状态显现，继而激发清醒状态下的专门化智性倾向。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: I wanted to say a little more about duration, as far as communication from the inner senses are concerned. As I said, duration can be experienced in relation to psychological time and within its framework. The important thing here, and I'm afraid the difficult thing for you here, is the relaxation of the mind from clock time. And this is a freedom which you can and should allow yourself in such instances.
quotes.translation: 关于内在感官沟通所涉及的"存续"概念，我想再多说几句。如我所言，存续可以在心理时间的框架内被体验。此处关键——恐怕对你们而言也是难点——在于让心智从钟表时间中松脱。这种自由，你们在这些情境中能够且应当允许自己享有。

quotes.text: The voice mechanism, unfortunately, is something that we must work with, and to get my own personality across through the female image and vocal chords, certain adjustments must be made. Beyond this, however, as I have mentioned before in class, it is not out of the inner sense of my invisible heart, but out of the depths of your own psychologies that you make me into a wise old man, and project upon me the authority images that lurk in your own minds. I have always tried to keep you from making this error, and sought to release from within yourselves your own abilities.
quotes.translation: 遗憾的是，声音机制是我们必须处理的媒介。要让我的个性通过女性形象与声带传达，必须进行某些调整。然而除此之外，正如我课堂上曾提及的：并非出于我无形之心的内在感知，而是源于你们自身心理的深处，你们将我塑造成智慧老者，并将潜藏于自己心智中的权威意象投射于我。我一直试图阻止你们犯此错误，并引导你们释放自身内在的能力。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (However, from Session 14 [for January 8, 1964], there's other material that can be given here, as well as some that makes an interesting note at the end of this appendix.4 First, from my own note at 11:05: "Jane said that Seth was quite pleased with the new voice, and that she now knows what he's thinking sometimes, even though he doesn't relay it to or through her as part of a message."

quotes.text: This involves efficient, complete use of the outer senses in their perception of camouflage reality, and of joyful, effective behavior and manipulation within that field of camouflage in which you spend a certain level of your existence. When you operate within it you should indeed experience it completely, in as many phases as possible, and be it to a much greater degree than is usually achieved, the conscious mind using itself then in experience, and thus knowing itself. And then the switch to use of the inner senses. The great contrast then refreshes the whole self.
quotes.translation: 这涉及对外感官的高效完整运用——在感知伪装实相时，以及在你们特定存在层面所栖居的伪装领域内，进行愉悦有效的行动与操控。当你们在此领域运作时，确实应当尽可能全面地体验它，达到远超寻常的程度，让意识心在体验中运用自身，从而认知自身。随后转向内在感官的运用。这种巨大反差将使整个自我焕然一新。

quotes.text: You must therefore explore the psyche, the living consciousness. It will lead you to the withinness. This is not an impractical, but very practical, endeavor in all areas. Scientifically, such studies would vastly enlarge your concepts so that a loving technology could follow the most beautiful contours of the mind, rising on the natural mountains of human abilities and then more easily into fulfillment.
quotes.translation: 因此你们必须探索心灵——这活生生的意识。它将引领你们抵达内在之境。这在所有领域都不是不切实际，而是极为务实的努力。从科学角度看，此类研究将极大拓展你们的概念，使充满关爱的科技得以追随心智最美丽的轮廓，沿人类能力的自然山脉攀升，更顺畅地臻于圆满。

quotes.text: Illnesses must be treated primarily however by changing the basic mental habits, for unless this is done the trouble will erupt time and time again in different guises. However, the system obviously has the ability to heal itself, and every opportunity should be given so that it is allowed to do so. To my knowledge nothing is known about the healing effect that often occurs in dreams themselves. For a wholly destructive attitude of mind has been changed overnight in the dream state to a constructive condition, and the whole electromagnetic balance has been changed.
quotes.translation: 然而疾病必须主要通过改变基本心智习惯来治疗，因为若不如此，问题将以不同伪装反复爆发。不过，系统显然具备自愈能力，应当给予它充分机会来施展。据我所知，梦境本身常发生的疗愈效应至今无人知晓。因为全然破坏性的心智态度，曾在梦境状态中一夜之间转化为建设性状态，整个电磁平衡也随之改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The lag is partially the result of cues to action. These gradually lose their strength and as the personality, in a better state of mind, is no longer as sensitive to those cues. For a while it is still somewhat more sensitive, for example, to Moods or depressions that it would ordinarily sail through. The overall condition has indeed remarkably improved, as I told you, and there is no distortion here.
quotes.translation: 这种滞后部分源于行动线索。这些线索逐渐失去效力，而人格在较好的心境下，对这些线索也不再那么敏感。举例来说，对于平常能轻易度过的情绪或抑郁，此时他仍会稍微敏感一些。整体状况确实已显著改善，正如我告诉你的，这里没有任何扭曲。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Before the beginning of the universe, we will postulate the existence of an omnipotent, creative source. (Pause.) We will hope to show that this divine subjectivity is as present in the world of your experience as it was before the beginning of the universe. Again, I refer to this original subjectivity as All That Is. I am making an attempt to verbalize concepts that almost defy the edges of the intellect, unless that intellect is thoroughly reinforced by the intuition's strength. So you will need to use your mind and your own intuitions as you read this book.
quotes.translation: 在宇宙诞生之前，我们假设存在一个全能、具有创造力的源头。（停顿。）我们希望证明，这个神圣的主体性在你们经验的世界中，与宇宙诞生之前同样存在。我再次将这个原始主体性称为"一切万有"。我正在尝试用语言表达那些几乎超越理智边缘的概念，除非理智被直觉的力量彻底强化。因此，当你阅读这本书时，你需要运用你的心智和自身的直觉。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Ruburt was well prepared for the call by then, and for the visit. Now the visit and Ruburt's earlier feelings and thoughts were part of the same event, except that his subjective experience gave him clues as to the inner processes by which all events take place. More is involved than the simple question: Did he perceive the visit precognitively? More is involved than the question: Did he perceive his information directly from the minds of his friends, or from the letter itself, which had already been mailed, of course, and was on its way to Ruburt at the time?
quotes.translation: 鲁柏当时已为那通电话和来访做好了充分准备。现在，这次来访与鲁柏先前的感受和想法属于同一事件，只是他的主观体验为他提供了所有事件发生的内在过程的线索。这涉及的不只是简单的问题：他是否预知了这次来访？也不只是：他是否直接从朋友们的思想中，或从那封已经寄出、正在寄往鲁柏途中的信件本身获取了信息？
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The most rejuvenating idea of all, and the greatest step to any true illumination, is the realization that your exterior life springs from the invisible world of your reality through your conscious thoughts and beliefs, for then you realize the power of your individuality and identity. You are immediately presented with choices. You can no longer see yourself as a victim of circumstances. Yet the conscious mind arose precisely to open up choices, to free you from a one-road experience, to let you use your creativity to form diversified, varied comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 最具恢复活力的理念，也是通往任何真正启蒙的最大一步，就是认识到你的外在生活源自你实相的无形世界，通过你有意识的念头和信念。因为那时你才意识到你个体性和身份的力量。你立刻面临选择。你不再将自己视为环境的受害者。而有意识的心智恰恰是为了开启选择，将你从单一道路的经验中解放出来，让你运用创造力形成多样化、多角度的理解。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The releases in his personality could have been achieved in other, easier methods. Nevertheless without these alterations our work could not be fulfilled in all the ways possible. His rigidity of attitude resulted in a trend for self destruction, tempered it is true, but dangerous. There was a resultant manufacture of various chemicals within his system that tended to reproduce and perpetuate the depressed state of mind. An overproduction of adrenaline that kept him stirred up, but also an overproduction of certain chemicals that physically slowed him down.
quotes.translation: 他的人格本可以通过其他更简单的方式获得释放。然而，没有这些改变，我们的工作就无法在所有可能的方面完成。他僵化的态度导致了一种自我毁灭的倾向，虽有所缓和，但依然危险。这导致他体内制造出各种化学物质，倾向于复制并延续抑郁的心境。肾上腺素过度分泌使他持续亢奋，但某些化学物质的过度分泌又在生理上使他迟缓。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Now he was told under Emotionally charged conditions, as you know, by his mother, that he could or would lose his mind. He tied this in with any strong spontaneous actions on his part, regardless of their nature. An example: once he found it thrilling to ride in an automobile at fast speeds. Now they frighten him. But to him any spontaneity carried the same danger that, say, speeding definitely does.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，他在情绪激动的状况下被母亲告知，他可能会或将会失去理智。他将此与他任何强烈的自发行为联系起来，无论其性质如何。例如：他曾觉得高速驾车令人兴奋。现在这却让他害怕。但对他而言，任何自发性都带有与超速驾驶同样的危险。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: You were mentally wandering through the house, the both of you, and Ruburt caught himself where his mind was--only his physical body was not in the same place. Because this was like a mental doodle, the colors were not complete, the picture was not filled in.
quotes.translation: 你们俩都在精神上游荡于那栋房子，鲁柏觉察到自己心智所在之处——只是他的身体并不在同一位置。由于这就像精神涂鸦，色彩并不完整，画面也未填充完毕。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

source_raw.title: (See the beginning of this Session 909 in Chapter 6 for Volume 1 of Dreams. As Seth, Jane had started delivering the session at 9:05 P.M. on Monday, April 21, 1980.)

quotes.text: Your actions take place with such seeming smoothness that you do not realize the order involved. A volcanic eruption in one corner of the world will ultimately affect the entire earth in varying degrees. An emotional eruption will do the same thing on another level, altering the local area primarily but also sending out its ripples into the mass psychological environment. The psyche's picture of reality, then, would be equally incomprehensible to the conscious mind because of the intense focus upon singularity that your usual consciousness requires.
quotes.translation: 你的行动看似如此流畅，以至于你意识不到其中蕴含的秩序。世界某个角落的火山爆发最终会以不同程度影响整个地球。情感爆发在另一层面也会产生同样效应，主要改变局部区域，同时将其涟漪扩散至集体心理环境。因此，心灵对实相的描绘对意识心智而言同样难以理解，因为你的日常意识需要极度聚焦于单一性。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When we began, he found it difficult to believe that so many answers were available in the conscious mind, and was astonished as he proceeded to discover that this was the case. I will use him here as an example to some extent, to show how a bridge belief appeared to assimilate what seemed to be diametrically opposed ideas. The same procedures will operate regardless of the particular beliefs held.
quotes.translation: 当我们开始时，他难以相信意识心智中竟有如此多的答案，随着探索发现确实如此，他感到震惊。我在此以他为例，展示一个桥梁信念如何同化看似截然对立的观念。无论持有何种特定信念，同样的运作程序都会生效。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: You should be close to this sort of thing. You should pay more attention to the basis on which the company ultimately rests, or the company will be outstripped. Your judgment of people will stand you in good stead here, but you must join all your forces and tell yourself that you have made up your mind, for this is your one chance now, as you have lived your life, to shape a strong area of reality.
quotes.translation: 你应当贴近这类事物。你该更关注公司最终赖以生存的基础，否则公司将被超越。你对人的判断在此将大有裨益，但你必须凝聚所有力量，告诉自己已下定决心——因为这是你此生唯一的机会，去塑造一个坚实的实相领域。

quotes.text: The enlightened conscious mind is always alert for such messages. You can also go steps beyond this into the dream condition itself, requesting certain dreams, certain solutions, and therefore shortening the time, so to speak, that may be involved otherwise.
quotes.translation: 开悟的意识心智始终警觉地接收此类讯息。你甚至可以更进一步进入梦境状态，请求特定的梦境与解决方案，从而缩短原本可能需要的时间。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Instead of a shadowy element, you yourself may feel unsubstantial -- "ghostly," as Ruburt did. Period. Instead of any of those things, the imagined dialogue -- if there is any -- may suddenly change from the dialogue that you remember; or the entire scene and action may quickly alter. Any of these occurrences can be hints that you are beginning to glimpse the probable variations of the particular scene or action. It is, however, the subjective feeling that is the important clue here, and once you experience it there will be no doubt in your mind.
quotes.translation: 你自身可能感觉不实在——如鲁柏那般"幽灵化"，而非成为模糊元素。句号。相较于上述任何情况，想象中的对话——若有的话——可能突然偏离你记忆中的对话；或整个场景与行动迅速改变。这些现象都可能暗示你开始瞥见特定场景或行动的可能变体。然而，主观感受才是此处的重要线索，一旦你体验过，心中便不再存疑。

quotes.text: Fears, sometimes even seemingly irrational ones, can serve to rouse the body if you have been too lethargic, or have been in a rut psychologically or physically. If you trusted your nature you would be able to trust such feelings, and following their own rhythms and routes they would change into others. Ideally even illnesses are a part of the body's health, representing needed adjustments, and also following the needs of the subjective person at any given time. (Long pause.) They are a part of the interplay between the body and mind, or spirit.
quotes.translation: 恐惧——有时甚至看似不合理的恐惧——若你过于懒散，或身心陷入僵局，它们能唤醒你的身体。若你信任自己的本性，便能信任这些感受，它们会遵循自身的节奏与路径，转化为其他感受。理想状态下，即使是疾病也是身体健康的一部分，代表着必要的调整，并顺应着主观个体在任何特定时刻的需求。（长停顿。）它们是身体与心智或灵性之间相互作用的一部分。

quotes.text: All of your physical experience must, of course, be pivoted in the corporeal reality of the body. The energy that moves your image comes from the soul. Through your own thoughts you direct the body's expression, and it can be of health or of illness. Out of a knowledge of the contents of your own conscious mind you can definitely heal most maladies of the body, within conditions to be given later.
quotes.translation: 当然，你所有的物质体验都必须以身体的肉体现实为轴心。驱动你形象的能量来自灵魂。通过你自己的思想，你引导着身体的表达，它可以是健康，也可以是疾病。凭借对自身意识心智内容的了解，你绝对可以在后续给出的条件下，治愈身体的大多数病痛。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Give us a moment, then ... Imagination also plays an important part in your subjective life, as it gives mobility to your beliefs. It is one of the motivating agencies that helps transform your beliefs into physical experience. It is vital therefore that you understand the interrelationship between ideas and imagination. In order to dislodge unsuitable beliefs and establish new ones, you must learn to use your imagination to move concepts in and out of your mind. The proper use of imagination can then propel ideas in the directions you desire.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……想象力在你的主观生活中也扮演着重要角色，因为它赋予你的信念以流动性。它是帮助将你的信念转化为物质体验的驱动机制之一。因此，理解观念与想象力之间的相互关系至关重要。为了摆脱不合适的信念并建立新的信念，你必须学会运用想象力，将概念移入和移出你的心智。恰当地运用想象力，便能将观念推向你所期望的方向。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Small amounts of ordinary stimulators, such as coffee or tea, taken before bed when you are already sleepy, have a beneficial effect in stimulating dream activity and aiding dream recall. Too large a serving, of course, could simply waken you, but small amounts taken if you are already drowsy allow you to take your conscious mind into the dream state more readily, where it can act as an observer.
quotes.translation: 少量常见的刺激物，如咖啡或茶，在睡前你已感到困倦时服用，对刺激梦境活动和帮助回忆梦境有有益效果。当然，过量服用只会让你清醒，但若你已昏昏欲睡，少量服用能让你更轻松地将意识心智带入梦境状态，使其充当观察者。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: This does not mean that you must forever remain in ignorance, but it does mean that there are different kinds of knowledge, and that all of your information does not come by reasoning alone. You grew from a fetus into an adult, for instance, so obviously some part of you does know how to perform such an amazing activity as the growth and care of the physical body. The reasoning mind alone, however, cannot by itself grow even the smallest cell, or activate the life of even one molecule, yet the growth and maintenance of the body is constant.
quotes.translation: 这并不意味着你必须永远处于无知之中，而是意味着存在不同种类的知识，并且你所有的信息并非仅凭推理得来。例如，你从胎儿成长为成人，因此你身体的某部分显然确实知道如何完成诸如身体成长与照料这般惊人的活动。然而，仅凭推理心智，它本身连最小的细胞也无法生长，或激活哪怕一个分子的生命，但身体的成长与维持却是持续不断的。

quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Your question about transmigration (in Session 903 in Dreams) was an excellent one, and did indeed make the matter clear. It is a temptation at times to use more specific scientific terms, but these would be as confusing as the various definitions and classifications (with humor) that you read in the dictionary, so overall we try to hit a "happy medium." (With more humor.)

quotes.text: The fact is that when you insist upon evidence through the outside, regularly accepted senses, that you almost automatically turn off the inner sense apparatus. This is not necessary. Man to a large degree has set up this habit reaction. It is not a natural habit reaction. You must take the inner data at its face value, and this is what you will not do. Once you take this first step of spontaneity, you will actually receive evidence that even your conscious mind will be forced to accept. But the first step of such willingness must be made.
quotes.translation: 事实是，当你坚持通过外部、常规接受的感官来获取证据时，你几乎会自动关闭内在感官装置。这并非必要。人类在很大程度上已养成这种习惯性反应。它并非自然的习惯性反应。你必须按内在资料的字面意义接受它，而这正是你不愿做的。一旦你迈出这自发性的第一步，你实际上会收到连你的意识心智都不得不接受的证据。但必须迈出这意愿的第一步。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The body mechanisms become highly disoriented. The signals to the body are very contradictory, so that after a while, if such conditions continue, you can no longer tell whether you are in actual danger or imagined danger. Your mind then forces your body to be in a state of constant alert -- but more unfortunately, you train yourself to ignore your direct, sensual feedback in the present moment.
quotes.translation: 身体机制会变得高度紊乱。传递给身体的信号极为矛盾，以至于如果这种状况持续下去，过不了多久，你就无法分辨自己究竟处于真实危险还是想象的危险之中。你的心智随后迫使身体处于持续警戒状态——但更不幸的是，你训练自己忽略了当下时刻直接而感性的反馈。

quotes.text: Give us a moment, please. Now there is an exaggerated fear on Ruburt's part of the word arthritic, for in his mind it is connected with his mother's chronic state. He should understand it does not have this meaning to other people necessarily. He feels threatened by the term. (Long pause.) It's being used in the context it was, in your parents home and under those circumstances, was unfortunate.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻。现在鲁柏对"关节炎"这个词存在过度的恐惧，因为在他的心智中，这个词与他母亲的慢性病状况相关联。他应该明白，这个词对其他人未必具有同样的含义。他感到被这个术语威胁。（长停顿。）这个词被用在你们父母家中的那种语境下，在当时那种环境中，确实是不幸的。

quotes.text: Therefore a seeming division occurs, in which a portion of the invisible conscious mind is connected with the physical brain, and a portion of it is free of that connection. That [latter] part forms what you think of as the involuntary system of the body.
quotes.translation: 因此出现了一种表面上的分裂，其中无形意识心智的一部分与物质大脑相连，而另一部分则不受这种连接束缚。那后一部分形成了你们所认为的身体自主系统。

quotes.text: For an exercise, keep notes for a day or so of all the times you find yourself thinking of probable actions,2 large or small. In your mind, try to follow "what might have happened" had you taken the course you did not take. Then imagine what might happen as a result of your chosen decisions. You are a member of the species. Any choice you make privately affects it biologically and psychically.
quotes.translation: 作为一项练习，请用一天左右的时间记录下所有你发现自己正在思考可能行动的时刻，无论大小。在你的心智中，试着追踪"如果当初选择了你未选择的道路，可能会发生什么"。然后想象你已做出的决定可能带来什么结果。你是人类物种的一员。你私下做出的任何选择，都会在生物性和灵性上影响整个物种。

quotes.text: When I speak of levels of the subconscious, I do so only for simplicity's sake, since there is after all no top or bottom to the mind, but only a present point of focus from which viewpoints may be taken, and new perspectives formed. It is theoretically possible, for example, to change the focus, to delve downward as it were two lives back, and look upward from that perspective to the present.
quotes.translation: 当我谈到潜意识的层次时，只是为了简便起见，因为心智毕竟没有顶端或底部，只有当下的焦点，从这个焦点可以采取各种视角，并形成新的观点。例如，理论上可以改变焦点，仿佛向下深入两世之前，然后从那个视角向上看向当下。

Your abilities are developed enough so that these experiments should be successful, with some additional training that can only be achieved through experience. Some hints from me will help, but I would like to give you some idea, you sec, of what to expect.
quotes.translation: 一年前，这些信息对你来说未必实用。但考虑到你心中计划的实验，它们就变得高度实用了。你的能力已发展到足够程度，因此这些实验应该能够成功，只需通过经验获得一些额外训练。我的一些提示会有所帮助，但我想让你对可能发生的情况有所了解，你明白吗。

quotes.text: That earth-god portion of yourself attempts to direct you through probabilities. Again, on deep biological levels beneath normal consciousness, and on psychic levels above normal consciousness, you are aware of the integrity of your being -- but also of your great connection, while living in flesh, with the natural environment of time and space. The earth-god concept can be consciously used, but only to your greatest advantage if you understand the purposes of your conscious mind and its relationship with your biological nature.
quotes.translation: 你自身那部分大地之神试图引导你穿越可能性。在正常意识之下的深层生物层面，以及正常意识之上的心灵层面，你都能意识到自身存在的完整性——同时也意识到当你活在肉身中时，与时空自然环境之间那伟大的联结。大地之神的概念可以有意识地运用，但唯有当你理解了自己意识心智的目的及其与生物本质的关系时，才能获得最大利益。

quotes.text: Now you have some latent inclinations of a musical nature as I mentioned earlier having to do with past-life experiences. Now simply in a spirit of fun, you see, you can use your abilities, and I will give you hints if you request them. You can use your abilities to hear the kind of music with which you were one time acquainted. This music is part of your experience though you have forgotten. Now: when you hear this music, in your mind try to translate it into color and design. And when you think of color, then, senseits motion.
quotes.translation: 正如我先前所提，你有些与前世经历相关的音乐性潜在倾向。现在纯粹为了好玩，你可以运用自己的能力，若你请求，我会给你提示。你可以运用自己的能力去聆听曾经熟悉的音乐。这音乐虽被你遗忘，却仍是你经验的一部分。现在：当你听到这音乐时，试着在脑海中将其转化为色彩与图案。而当你想到色彩时，便去感受它的律动。

quotes.text: Do not be impatient. As you continue with this exercise over a period of time you will be able to go further away, orienting yourself as you grow more familiar with the feeling of your mind. Gradually you will discover that this inner sense data will become clearer and clearer as you move toward another "station." It will represent reality as perceived from a different state of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 不要急躁。当你持续进行这个练习一段时间后，随着你对心智感受越来越熟悉，你将能走得更远，为自己定位。当你朝向下一个"站点"移动时，会逐渐发现这内在感官数据会变得越来越清晰。它将代表从不同意识状态所感知到的实相。

source_raw.title: Each particular "station" of consciousness perceives in a different kind of reality, and as mentioned earlier (in Session 711, for instance), you usually tune in to your home station most of the time. If you turn your focus only slightly away, the world appears differently; and if that slightly altered focus were the predominant one, then that is how the world would seem to be. Each aspect of the psyche perceives the reality upon which it is focused, and that reality is also the materialization of a particular state of the psyche projected outward. You can learn to encounter other realities by altering your position within your own psyche.

quotes.text: The past is obviously built into words in terms of time. When you speak a given word you may not know the history of its changes through the years, yet you speak it perfectly. You seldom realize that the present state of your language, whatever it is, will for others someday seem to be an archaic version. In whatever terms, again, you think of yourselves as being at the top of the mountain. In your terms, language presupposes a particular kind of development of mind, and when you think of language you tie the two together.
quotes.translation: 从时间角度来看，过去显然被编织进词语之中。当你说出一个特定词语时，或许并不知晓它历经岁月变迁的历史，却能完美地说出它。你很少意识到，无论你当前的语言状态为何，对后人而言终将成为古老版本。不论以何种方式，你再次将自己视为站在山顶。就你的角度而言，语言预设了心智的特定发展模式，当你想到语言时，便将两者联结在一起。

quotes.text: An outright lie may or may not be a violation. A sex act may or may not be a violation. A scientific expedition may or may not be a violation. Not going to church on Sunday is not a violation. Having normal aggressive thoughts is not a violation. Doing violence to your body, or another's, is a violation. Doing violence to the spirit of another is a violation -- but again, because you are conscious beings the interpretations are yours. Swearing is not a violation. If you believe that it is then in your mind it becomes one.
quotes.translation: 彻头彻尾的谎言可能是也可能不是一种侵犯。性行为可能是也可能不是一种侵犯。科学考察可能是也可能不是一种侵犯。周日不去教堂不是一种侵犯。拥有正常的攻击性念头不是一种侵犯。对自己或他人的身体施暴是一种侵犯。对他人精神施暴是一种侵犯——但再次强调，因为你们是有意识的存有，所以诠释权在你们手中。咒骂不是一种侵犯。若你相信它是，那么在你的心智中它便成了侵犯。

quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your ideas themselves follow certain laws of creativity. They have their own rhythms. The associative processes of your mind, working through the brain, have great connection with the minute behavior of your cells. As you learn to use your thoughts, or even as they naturally change, resulting alterations take place within the cells. There is an orderly progression, an intimate relationship.
quotes.translation: 你的想法本身遵循着某些创造力法则。它们有自己的节奏。你心智的联想过程，经由大脑运作，与你细胞的细微行为有着巨大关联。当你学会运用自己的思想，或甚至当它们自然改变时，细胞内部也会发生相应的变化。这其中存在着有序的进程与亲密的关系。

quotes.text: This psychic startled the couple by correctly identifying some specific elements of their experiences, so there was some kind of psychic connection also. Again, of course, coincidence. So says the officially organized mind. The rich interweavings of probabilities are apparent in all of your lives if only you stop organizing your perceptions and experience in prepackaged ways (emphatically).
quotes.translation: 这位灵媒准确指出了这对夫妇经历中的某些特定细节，令他们大为震惊，因此确实存在某种灵性连接。当然，这又被归为巧合。官方组织的心智如是说。概率的丰富交织在你们所有人的人生中显而易见，只要你们停止用预先包装好的方式组织你们的感知和体验（强调地说）。

quotes.text: If the dream world and the mind and the inner universe do exist, and if they do not exist in space, and if they do not exist basically in time, though they may be glimpsed through time, then your question will be: in what medium or in what manner do they exist; and without time how can they be said to exist in duration?
quotes.translation: 如果梦境世界、心智和内在宇宙确实存在，且它们不存在于空间中，基本上也不存在于时间里——尽管可能透过时间瞥见它们——那么你们的问题将是：它们存在于何种媒介或方式中？没有时间，又如何能说它们存在于持续中？

quotes.text: Now, creativity has always been the species' closest connection with its own source, with the nature of its own being. Through creativity the species senses All That Is. Creativity goes by a different set of rules, however. It defies categories, and it insists upon the evidence of feeling. It is a source of revelation and inspiration -- yet initially revelation and inspiration do not deal with power, but with knowing. So what often happens in your society when men and women have creative bents, and good minds to boot?
quotes.translation: 创造力一直是人类物种与其自身源头、与其存在本质最紧密的连接。通过创造力，物种感知到一切万有。然而，创造力遵循一套不同的规则。它蔑视分类，坚持情感的证据。它是启示和灵感的源泉——但最初启示和灵感处理的不是力量，而是知晓。那么在你们社会中，当男女拥有创造倾向和良好心智时，常常会发生什么？

quotes.text: Therapeutic systems are an important part of this interrelationship, and they operate constantly. quotes.translation: 治疗系统是这种相互关联的重要组成部分，它们持续运作。从某种角度看，恩宠或启明的状态发生在意识心与其他心灵及身体层面达到最平衡的均衡之处——这是对个体内在完整性及其与整个宇宙关系的生物性和灵性认知。

quotes.text: This type of perception cannot be described until he forms suitable verbal patterns that can come only with further experience. In this, I am a touchstone. He accelerates mentally to a certain degree, and that puts him in touch with me -- an additional energy source. He activates certain portions of the brain that connect it to another mind, that people do not as yet realize they possess.
quotes.translation: 这种感知类型无法被描述，直到他形成合适的语言模式——这只能通过进一步的经验获得。在这方面，我是一块试金石。他在心智上加速到一定程度，这使他与我——一个额外的能量来源——取得联系。他激活了大脑中连接另一个心智的某些部分，而人们尚未意识到自己拥有这个心智。

quotes.text: The perspective from which you watch world events is vital, and it is true that communication now brings to the conscious mind a far greater barrage than before. But it is also a barrage that makes man see his own activities, and even with the growth of the new nationalism in the Third World, those nations begin from a new perspective, in which the eyes of the world are indeed upon them.
quotes.translation: 你观察世界事件的角度至关重要，确实，现今的通讯带给意识心前所未有的信息轰炸。但这种轰炸也让人类看清自身活动，即便在第三世界新民族主义兴起之际，这些国家也以崭新视角起步——全世界的目光确实正注视着他们。

quotes.text: You will accept your present position, whatever it is, as being a part of that direction, and realize that from it can come all the creative elements that you need. Being yourself and trusting in your own integrity, you will automatically help others. It does little good to repeat a suggestion such as, "I am a worthy person. I trust myself and my integrity," if at the same time you are afraid of your own emotions and become upset whenever you catch yourself in what you think of as a negative frame of mind.
quotes.translation: 无论你当前处境如何，都要将其视为该方向的一部分，并认识到从中能生发你所需的一切创造元素。做真实的自己并信赖自身完整性，你自然会帮助他人。若同时畏惧自身情绪，每当察觉自己处于所谓负面心境便惶惶不安，那么重复"我是个有价值的人，我信任自己与完整性"这类暗示便收效甚微。

quotes.text: If the books are children symbolically, then in those same terms his representation of my reality is a far more living, three-dimensional aspect. quotes.translation: 若书籍象征孩童，那么用同样标准衡量，他对我的实相之呈现是更具生命力的立体面向。例如他曾多次思考精神分裂症，却未意识到在此层面——不论我的独立性及其他议题——他正创造着不受时间束缚的人格，将其置于意识心领导下，指派具有重大意义与重要性的任务，而这些任务终将被执行。

quotes.text: Ruburt is coming along well in the gallery situation. You should have a good winter, both of you. If Ruburt can stand it, I would suggest a three-day period after he leaves the gallery, during which he does no writing. Perhaps playing about his apartment while instructing his subconscious to work for him in the interval, and completely divorcing his conscious mind from his writing for that period of time. Such a method will result in maximum use of his abilities, and more practical utilization of energy.

quotes.text: Music is an exterior representation, and an excellent one, of the life-giving inner sounds that act therapeutically within your body all the time. (See Chapter Five.) Listening to music that you like will often bring images into your mind that show you your conscious beliefs in different form.
quotes.translation: 音乐是赋予生命的内在声音之外在呈现——且是绝佳的呈现——这些声音始终在你们体内发挥疗愈作用（见第五章）。音乐是那些更深层声律与动律的有意识提醒。聆听喜爱的音乐常会带来意象，以不同形式展现你的意识信念。

quotes.text: (I nodded.) Use those, and keep them foremost in your mind as you fall to sleep. Even more, inspire your enthusiasm for projecting, for desire is extremely important. Imagine yourself doing so. You may find it of benefit also to imagine a flying dream, or form a dream consciously in which you feel me waiting for you to let go the cares of the day, and ready to help you leave your body.
quotes.translation: （我点头。）运用这些意象，入睡时让它们占据意识最前沿。更要激发你对出体的热忱，因为渴望至关重要。想象自己正在出体。你或许会发现，想象飞翔梦境或刻意编织梦境也有助益——在梦中感受我正等待你放下俗务，随时准备助你离开肉身。

quotes.text: "Your scientists can count their elements.... That is, they will create more and discover more until they are ready to go out of their minds, because they will always create [physical] 'camouflages' of the real [nonphysical] thing. And while they create instruments to deal with smaller and smaller particles, they will actually see smaller and smaller particles, seemingly without end.
quotes.translation: 你们的科学家可以数出他们的元素……也就是说，他们会创造更多并发现更多，直到他们准备发疯，因为他们总是会为真实的（非物质的）事物创造（物质的）"伪装"。当他们制造仪器来处理越来越小的粒子时，他们实际上会看到越来越小的粒子，似乎永无止境。

source_raw.title: 1. In Volume 1 of "Unknown" Reality, I wrote in Note 7 for Session 681 that atoms are "processes" rather than things. The classical conception of the typical atom as being composed of a neat nucleus of indivisible protons and neutrons circled by electrons is largely passé, although for convenience's sake we may still describe the atom that way. (In those terms, the one exception is the hydrogen atom, which evidently consists of but one proton and one electron cloud, or "smear.") For the simple purposes of this note, then, I'm leaving out considerations involving quantum mechanics, which concept repudiates the idea of "particles" to begin with. (And surely that notion involves more than a little of the psychic, or "irrational." What a heretical thought from the scientific viewpoint!) But each atom of whatever element is an amazingly complicated, finely balanced assemblage of forces and particles woven together in exquisite detail--one of the more basic examples of the unending and stupendous creativity, order, and design of nature, or consciousness, or All That Is.

quotes.text: ("Connection with a fabric that somehow seems glued on. Something glued on something, perhaps paper." This is excellent data, and very close indeed to the object. The object is trimmed from the linen canvas I bought at the Art Shop, and this piece of canvas was glued on to large sheets of Masonite so as to have a firm support--the experiment I had in mind which was referred to later.
quotes.translation: （"与某种似乎粘在上面的织物连接。某种东西粘在什么东西上，也许是纸。"）这是极好的数据，确实非常接近那个物体。这个物体是从我在美术店买的亚麻画布上剪下来的，这块画布被粘在大块的美森耐板上，以便有一个坚实的支撑——这就是我后来提到的那个实验构想。

quotes.text: It isn't what you see, and yet what you see is it. Sight itself is it, the thing seen and the perceiver. You cannot stop action even in your mind, for the attempt to stop action is in itself an action. What you term death is an action. It is not the end of acts. The self at death is indeed more active as a rule than before, and the resources of the self are used to greater advantage.
quotes.translation: 它不是你所看到的，然而你所看到的正是它。视觉本身就是它，被见之物与见者。你甚至无法在你的心智中停止行动，因为停止行动的尝试本身就是一种行动。你所谓的死亡是一种行动。它不是行动的终结。死亡时的自我通常比之前更活跃，自我的资源被更有效地利用。

quotes.text: Now. In this existence, when you see a picture in your mind, and when there is strong emotion and vivid desire behind it, it will be constructed. There will be a time lapse within this system, but in other systems there may be no time lapse... and your thought may be instantly transposed into reality. Therefore, now you must learn the nature of your thoughts and how to handle energy.
quotes.translation: 现在。在这个存在中，当你在心智中看到一幅画面，并且背后有强烈的情感和生动的渴望时，它就会被构建出来。在这个系统内会有时间延迟，但在其他系统中可能没有时间延迟……你的思想可能瞬间转化为现实。因此，现在你必须学习你思想的本质以及如何处理能量。

Her own work has been going well for the most part, however--she expects to finish Chapter 12 of God of Jane tomorrow. Because of her concentration on that hook she hasn't done much on her book of poetry, If We Live Again, since late February; and as I mentioned in the Preface for Dreams, she laid aside her third Seven novel, Oversoul Seven and the Museum of Time, in May 1979 when she began God of Jane. quotes.text: Now these previews are played out not only for the mind but for the body as well. In sleep, again, each cell calculates the effect of various probable events upon its own reality. Computations are made so that the body's entire response can be ascertained ahead of time, and the advantages and disadvantages weighed. The body participates in dreaming at the most minute levels.
quotes.translation: 这些预演不仅为心智而演，也为身体而演。在睡眠中，每个细胞同样计算各种可能事件对其自身实相的影响。进行运算以便提前确定身体的整体反应，并权衡利弊。身体在最细微的层面上参与做梦。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the back of your mind is one question that I have avoided for many reasons. One of the main reasons for my avoiding it was the necessity of giving prerequisite material so that the answer would be at least partially comprehensible.
quotes.translation: 你心底有一个我因诸多原因一直回避的问题。我回避它的主要原因之一，是必须提供先决资料，使答案至少能部分被理解。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Now. The mind as opposed to the brain, perceives time in terms of the spacious present. It is true that the mind works on associative principles. Therefore these associations are drawn not only from the past, but also from the future.
quotes.translation: 现在。心智与大脑相对，以广袤当下的方式感知时间。心智确实依据联想原则运作。因此这些联想不仅来自过去，也来自未来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: To refute your reality in time, therefore, results in your being stuck in time and obsessed by it. Accepting your integrity in time allows the body to function until its natural end, in good condition, free from those distorted, invisible concepts about age. If you believe that youth is the ideal and struggle for it while simultaneously believing that old age must involve infirmities, then you cause an unnecessary dilemma, and hasten aging according to the negative aspects of your mind.
quotes.translation: 因此，否认你在时间中的实相，会导致你被困在时间中并为其所困。接受你在时间中的完整性，能让身体以良好状态运作至自然终点，免于那些关于年龄的扭曲、无形观念。若你相信青春是理想并为之奋斗，同时相信老年必然伴随衰弱，便会造成不必要的困境，并依你心智的负面面向加速衰老。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now in his own mind Ruburt has been highly critical of that neighbor, and so have you at times. There is one area you see where he (Leonard) is thus far entirely blocked, for he cannot love another person wholeheartedly, nor imagine himself in that position. This lack is always with him, and it is caused by a particular shallow area in his personality that is not developed.
quotes.translation: 现在，鲁柏自己心里对那位邻居一直非常挑剔，你们有时也是如此。你们看到有一个领域，他（伦纳德）至今完全受阻，因为他无法全心全意地爱另一个人，也无法想象自己处于那种境地。这种缺失始终伴随着他，这是由他个性中一个未发展的特定浅薄区域造成的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You can request that the thought content of your mind be translated into an intense image, symbolically representing individual thoughts and the overall mental landscape, then take out what you do not like and replace it with more positive images. This does not mean that this inner landscape must always be completely sunny, but it does mean that it should be well balanced.
quotes.translation: 你可以要求你心灵的思想内容被转化为强烈的意象，象征性地代表个体思想和整体心灵景观，然后取出你不喜欢的部分，用更积极的意象替换。这并不意味着这个内在景观必须始终完全晴朗，但它确实意味着它应该保持良好平衡。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There is a strong and rather aggressive nature beneath the surface personality of the questioner, of which the questioner is frightened, to some extent therefore a divided self. The male is passive and of a submissive mind, and given to an obsession with over consideration for security. He will always seek security, but he enjoys being the object of a chase of this nature.
quotes.translation: 提问者表面人格之下有一种强烈且相当侵略性的本性，提问者对此感到害怕，因此在某种程度上是一个分裂的自我。男性是被动的、顺从的心灵，并且沉迷于过度考虑安全。他总是寻求安全，但他享受成为这种性质追逐的对象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The receptivity is of a creative nature, you understand as if you were clearing land so that a primitive airplane could land, or setting a beacon in a window. In other words your state of mind can help guide him to you, much to his surprise and delight and gratitude, for he did not count on that boost.
quotes.translation: 接受性具有创造性的本质，你明白这就像清理土地以便原始飞机降落，或在窗户上设置一个信标。换句话说，你的心灵状态可以帮助引导他到你这里，令他大为惊讶、喜悦和感激，因为他没有预料到这种助力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Close your eyes. Imagine a photograph of yourself (in parentheses: Yes, we are finally back to photographs).3 In your mind's eye see the photograph of yourself on a table or desk. If you are working mentally with a particular snapshot, then note the other items in the picture. If the photograph is strictly imaginary, then create an environment about the image of yourself.
quotes.translation: 闭上眼睛。想象一张你自己的照片（括号内：是的，我们终于回到照片了）。3 在你心灵之眼中，看到桌子上或书桌上你自己的照片。如果你在心灵上处理一张特定的快照，那么注意照片中的其他物品。如果照片纯粹是想象的，那么围绕你自己的形象创造一个环境。

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Dictation: There are vast distances of a psychological and psychic nature separating my reality from your own. To some degree Ruburt's mind is the connective between us. As I speak in sessions, therefore, I am not entirely myself.
quotes.translation: 口授：我的实相与你们的实相之间存在着心理和灵性性质的广阔距离。在某种程度上，鲁柏的心灵是我们之间的连接。因此，当我在课中说话时，我并不完全是原来的自己。

quotes.text: It could not be accomplished by the conscious mind -- though the conscious mind can indeed will the process to occur. (A most important point to remember.) The knee will continue to improve, and the finger also (on the left hand).
quotes.translation: 这无法通过意识心完成——尽管意识心确实可以意愿这个过程发生（这是最需要记住的一点）。膝盖会继续好转，手指（左手）也会如此。

source_raw.title: (I talked about the first session for Jane's symptoms -- the private portion of Session 208, for November 15, 1965 -- yet she said that for her the whole thing really started the day before we went to a party in June 1966 regarding How to Develop Your ESP Power. Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: For all my humor concerning the particular communication to Philip, I wanted him to be conscious of hearing me. Actually this caused an interruption of action, in order to make the source of the communication stand out in his mind. Otherwise he would have merely automatically recorded my warning, and reacted to it smoothly without any realization on the part of the ego.
quotes.translation: 尽管我对给菲利普的特定传讯开了些玩笑，但我希望他能有意识地听到我。实际上这造成了行动的暂时中断，目的是让传讯的来源在他心中凸显出来。否则他只会自动记录我的警告，并毫无自我觉察地顺利做出反应。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: Obviously the conscious mind is a phenomenon, not a thing. It is ever-changing. It can be concentrated or turned by the ego in literally endless directions. It can view outward reality or turn inward, observing its own contents.
quotes.translation: 显然意识心是一种现象，而非实体。它不断变化。自我可以将其聚焦或转向几乎无穷无尽的方向。它可以审视外在实相，也可以转向内在观察自身内容。

quotes.text: A board is neutral, messages in own mind are not. She is cooperating and your two attitudes give excellent balance. She will grow used to it. Individual attitudes change with various approaches, and you will try other ways also.
quotes.translation: 木板是中性的，但自己心中的讯息并非如此。她正在配合，你们两人的态度形成了绝佳的平衡。她会逐渐习惯。个体态度会随着不同方法而改变，你们也会尝试其他方式。

quotes.text: You have a storm. Weathermen speak of local conditions and merging air currents -- but my journeys are in a realm where consciousnesses merge. I do not know if I have specifically mentioned it, but you should understand my constant state of growth, expansion, and development. Recall Ruburt's episodes with the [psychic] library.1 Those are examples of far lesser versions of my activities, and yet in those terms, and using an analogy, I travel to many great universities of the mind.
quotes.translation: 你们正经历一场风暴。气象学家谈论局部条件和气流交汇——但我的旅程处于意识交融的领域。我不确定是否特别提过，但你们应该理解我持续成长、扩展和发展的状态。回想一下鲁柏与[灵性]图书馆的经历。那些只是我活动的微小版本，但用类比来说，我游历了许多心灵大学。

quotes.text: You form the fabric of your experience through your own beliefs and expectations. These personal ideas about yourself and the nature of reality will affect your thoughts and emotions. You take your beliefs about reality as truth, and often do not question them. They seem self-explanatory. They appear in your mind as statements of fact, far too obvious for examination.
quotes.translation: 你通过自己的信念和期待编织经验的结构。这些关于自我和实相本质的个人观念会影响你的思想和情绪。你将关于实相的信念视为真理，往往不加质疑。它们看似不言自明，以事实陈述的形式出现在你心中，明显到无需审视。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is because of this instantaneous creation and projection of inner reality into form that you experience time within the physical system--to train you literally, to give you time to learn to handle your own creations. Projection experiments should only be adopted therefore when you are in a peaceful state of mind, as Ruburt should know after his creature experience.
quotes.translation: 正是由于内在实相被瞬间创造并投射为具体形态，你才在物质系统内体验时间——这实际上是为了训练你，给你时间学习处理自己的创造物。因此，投射实验只应在心境平和时进行，鲁柏在经历过他的造物体验后应当明白这点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Jointly, each generation has its own purpose. It is this -- to perfect inner knowledge, and to materialize it as faithfully as possible outward into the world. The changing physical scene throughout the centuries, as you know them, represents the inner images that have flickered through the minds of the individuals who lived within the world through the various ages.
quotes.translation: 每一代人共同拥有其自身目的——即完善内在知识，并尽可能忠实地将其物质化呈现于外在世界。你所知的数世纪以来不断变化的物质场景，代表着曾生活在不同时代的个体们脑海中闪现过的内在意象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: As the mind developed, the species could hand down to its offspring the wisdom and law of the elders. This is still being done in modern society, of course, when each child inherits the beliefs of its parents about the nature of reality. Apart from all other considerations, this is also a characteristic of creaturehood. Only the means are different with the animals.
quotes.translation: 随着心智的发展，物种能够将长者的智慧与法则传递给后代。当然，现代社会仍在延续这一模式——每个孩子都继承父母关于实相本质的信念。撇开其他因素不谈，这也是生物性的特征之一，动物只是采用不同的方式。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The physical organism itself is so equipped. Blood pressure rises in whole populations -- stress signals in terms of hormones are activated, but you are not taught to recognize these natural signals. There is a give-and-take between all portions of nature. You are as natural as an animal, and as "tuned in" to the deep rhythms of the earth -- those that you consciously perceive and those that are perceived by your body consciousness, but are screened out by the "official mind."
quotes.translation: 物质有机体本身具备相应机制。整个人群的血压会升高——荷尔蒙层面的压力信号被激活，但你们未被教导识别这些自然信号。自然界的各个部分之间存在相互交流。你与动物同样自然，同样"调频"至地球的深层韵律——那些你意识感知到的，以及被身体意识感知却被"官方心智"过滤掉的韵律。

quotes.text: And here a word about this material. Ruburt's mind is an excellent one, and well given to serve our needs at this time. There is a reciprocal agreement here, and a give and take that is unlike your friends idea of invasion.
quotes.translation: 关于这份资料需说明一点：鲁柏的心智极为出色，此刻非常适合服务我们的需求。这里存在互惠协议与双向交流，这与你们朋友所理解的"入侵"概念截然不同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The belief in guilt therefore would be a cementing structure that would hold together other similar core beliefs, and add to their strength. You must understand that these are not simply dead ideas, like debris, within your mind. They are psychic matter. In a sense then they are alive. They group themselves like cells, protecting their own validity and identity.
quotes.translation: 因此，对罪恶感的信念会成为巩固结构，将其他类似的核心信念凝聚在一起并增强其力量。你必须明白，这些并非如碎片般散落于心智中的僵死观念，而是心灵物质。从某种意义上说，它们是鲜活的——会像细胞般自我聚合，保护自身的有效性与独特性。

quotes.text: (11:32.) Illnesses usually represent unfaced problems, in your terms, and these dilemmas embody challenges meant to lead you to greater achievement and fulfillment. Because body and mind operate so well together, one will attempt to cure the other, and will often succeed if left alone. The organism has its own beliefs in health that are unconscious on your part.
quotes.translation: (11:32分) 疾病通常代表未面对的问题，用你们的说法，这些困境体现了旨在引导你们走向更大成就与圆满的挑战。由于身体与心智配合得如此完美，一方会试图治愈另一方，若不加干预往往能成功。有机体自身拥有你们意识层面所不知的健康信念。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Within the mind the dream has an electrical reality. Within the brain the dream has an electric and psychological reality; a much weaker electrical reality. That is, it still exists as an electrical reality, but it is not recognized as such by the brain or the psychological awareness of an individual. To the individual the dream has only a psychological reality.
quotes.translation: 在心智中，梦境具有电性实相。在大脑中，梦境具有电性与心理实相——但电性实相较弱。也就是说，它仍以电性实相存在，但大脑或个体的心理意识并未将其辨识为电性实相。对个体而言，梦境仅具有心理实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Atoms perceive their own positions, their velocities, motions, the nature of their surroundings, the material that they compose. [Your] world did not just come together, mindless atoms forming here and there, elements coalescing from brainless gases--nor was the world, again, created by some distant objectified God who created it part by part as in some cosmic assembly line. With defects built in, mind you (with some humor), and better models coming every geological season.
quotes.translation: 原子感知自身的位置、速度、运动、周遭环境的性质，以及它们所构成的物质。你们的世界并非偶然聚合而成——没有心智的原子四处拼凑，元素从无脑的气体中凝聚；同样，世界也并非由某个遥远客体化的神像宇宙流水线般逐一创造。请注意（带着些许幽默），每个地质季节都会推出更完善的型号，且缺陷早已内置其中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He felt bewildered in a world of opposites. Conflicting beliefs were uncritically accepted. (Pause.) The conscious mind will always attempt to make sense out of its beliefs, to form them into patterns and sequences. It will usually organize ideas in as rational a way as possible, and dispense with those that seem to contradict the overall system of its beliefs.
quotes.translation: 他在充满对立的世界中感到困惑。相互冲突的信念被不加批判地接受。（停顿）意识心智总会试图理解自己的信念，将其组织成模式与序列。它通常会尽可能理性地组织观念，并剔除那些与整体信念系统相矛盾的部分。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If you form a guilt in your mind, then it is a reality for you, and you must work it out. But many of you form guilts for which there is no adequate cause, and you saddle yourselves with these guilts without reason. In your dimension of activity there appear to be a wild assortment of evils. Let me tell you that he who hates an evil merely creates another one.
quotes.translation: 若你在心中形成罪疚，那对你而言就是实相，你必须化解它。但许多人形成的罪疚并无充分缘由，却毫无道理地将这些罪疚强加于己。在你们的活动维度中，似乎存在着形形色色的邪恶。让我告诉你们：憎恨邪恶者不过是在制造另一种邪恶。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You often avoid this natural treatment, however, and run from frightening conscious thoughts that would in their turn lead you to the source of "negative" beliefs, where they could be faced; you could then travel through them, so to speak, into feelings of joy and victory. Instead, for example, many of you accept the way of drugs, where such feelings and thoughts are thrust upon you, or forced out of you while you are denied the stabilizing comforts of the conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 然而你们常回避这种自然疗法，逃离那些本可引导你们找到"负面"信念根源的可怕意识念头——在那里你们本可直面它们，然后穿越它们，可以说，进入喜悦与胜利的感受。反之，例如许多人接受药物之道，让这些感受与念头被强加于身或强行驱离，同时被剥夺了意识心智带来的稳定慰藉。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Nor is its reality in space annihilated as long as it exists within your mind. Let us take a very simple example. A child has been told not to play with a doll. The order is disobeyed. The child, wittingly or unwittingly, breaks the doll, and it is finally thrown away. The doll exists in time quite vitally as long as the child or the adult-to-be remembers it.
quotes.translation: 只要它存在于你的心智中，它在空间中的实相也不会被消灭。让我们举一个非常简单的例子。一个孩子被告知不要玩一个娃娃。命令被违抗了。孩子有意或无意地打破了娃娃，最终它被丢弃了。只要孩子或未来的成人记得它，这个娃娃在时间中就依然生动地存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In this existence you are physically oriented. Surely then the conscious physically oriented mind is the one that is meant to make deductions about the nature of physical reality. Otherwise you would have no free will.
quotes.translation: 在这个存在中，你们是物质导向的。那么，当然，有意识的物质导向心智才是那个被用来对物质实相的本质进行推论的心智。否则你们就不会有自由意志。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "The name Normandy comes to mind, and the name Abelard. The dream came to remind Ruburt of those connections, but also to remind him that his life even then was enriched by a long-held love relationship. The two corresponded frequently, met often, and in their ways conspired to alter many of the practices that were abhorrent yet held as proper church policy.
quotes.translation: “诺曼底这个名字浮现在脑海中，还有阿伯拉尔的名字。这个梦是为了提醒鲁柏那些联系，但也提醒他，即使在那时，他的生命也因一段持久的爱情关系而丰富。两人频繁通信，经常见面，并以他们的方式合谋改变了许多令人憎恶却被视为教会正当政策的做法。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth's comments of February 17 also reflect upon Jane's own ideas as she described them just 11 days ago; see the excerpts in Note 4. Then in this Chapter 9 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 920.

quotes.text: I do not intend to let Ruburt in on any of my plans or intentions, as far as demonstrations are concerned. It is much better all around if he be kept clear of such knowledge. The last session with your friend went off extremely well, and I hope that he follows my advice. If Ruburt had known what was in my mind, he would have been nervous and self-conscious.
quotes.translation: 就示范而言，我不打算让鲁柏知道我的任何计划或意图。让他远离这类知识对各方面都好得多。上次与你朋友的课程进行得非常顺利，我希望他能听从我的建议。如果鲁柏知道我的想法，他会紧张且不自在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: "In childhood we have little past time to remember. We seem to come from darkness, taking our parents' memories on faith for proof that there was time before our birth. As we grow toward old age, we have past time to play with--we know where we came from in usual terms--and the darkness that once seemed to stretch behind our source or origin seems to be our destination. Certainly an examination of the mind and reality from the standpoint of old age will be invaluable."
quotes.translation: “在童年，我们几乎没有过去的时间可以回忆。我们似乎从黑暗中而来，凭借父母的记忆作为信仰，证明在我们出生之前有时间存在。随着我们走向老年，我们有过去的时间可以玩耍——我们以通常的术语知道我们来自何处——而曾经似乎延伸到我们源头或起源背后的黑暗，似乎成了我们的目的地。当然，从老年的角度审视心智和实相将是无价的。”
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: ("A main turnabout, or a connection with Maine." The turnabout connection is correct. In the letter used as object, I inform Dr. Lodico that I've changed my mind about sending the abstracts to the Mount Savior exhibit, and plan to submit other kinds of paintings instead. This makes the abstracts available for the doctor's inspection, and perhaps purchase.
quotes.translation: （“一个主要的转变，或者与缅因州的联系。”转变的联系是正确的。在用作物件的信中，我通知洛迪科医生，我改变了关于将抽象画送到救世主山展览的想法，并计划提交其他类型的画作。这使得这些抽象画可供医生检查，或许还能购买。）

quotes.translation: 然而，我曾说过，意识心智只是整体自我的一小部分，因此通过梦境接收到的信息会自动被采取行动或吸收，无论意识是否认知到。

However she has no written record of the dream, since this was before she had cultivated the habit of keeping a dream notebook. As mentioned many many sessions ago, however, her poem The Fence, written in May 1963, clearly foreshadows the Seth material, dealing with [but not always by outright name] such subjects as reincarnation, dreams, unperceived worlds, etc. [See the poem on page 28, Session 5, in Volume I of The Early Sessions]

quotes.text: They represent a present unique beyond telling, alive in each consciousness, more important than you recognize. There are no real rules to be followed that will bring you into such an encounter with the present moment of reality -- only a trust in the nature of your being. And that trust is within you whether or not you recognize it, for it gives you your present experience; and no matter how your mind questions, it rides securely in the great creativity of the soul.
quotes.translation: 它们代表了一种无法言喻的当下存在，在每个意识中鲜活，比你认识到的更为重要。没有真正的规则可以遵循，让你与现实的当下时刻相遇——只有对你存在本质的信任。而那份信任就在你之内，无论你是否认识到它，因为它赋予你当下的体验；无论你的心智如何质疑，它都安稳地乘载于灵魂的伟大创造力之中。

quotes.text: As far as Ruburt's personal subconscious mind is concerned, neither of you have to fear any material in it that might be, or present, dangerous, misguided, egocentric to the extreme, or darkly-inhibited phenomena.
quotes.translation: 就鲁柏的个人潜意识心智而言，你们俩都不必害怕其中可能存在的任何材料，这些材料可能是危险的、误导的、极端自我中心的，或是阴暗压抑的现象。

quotes.text: Our young friend here has many questions. His mind is buzzing with them. He is so impatient. There is one question however that I will indeed answer now, and it has to do with the delight with which he views fire.
quotes.translation: 我们这位年轻的朋友有许多问题。他的心智充满了这些问题。他如此不耐烦。然而，有一个问题我现在确实会回答，这与他看待火焰时的喜悦有关。

quotes.text: Messages from the inner self if strong enough will pass through the conscious barriers, and the conscious ego is only too happy to receive them. But such intuitions have usually first appeared in dream form, appearing to the ego later; and many such messages appear in dreams ahead of time, to be released to the conscious mind when situations demand it.
quotes.translation: 来自内在自我的讯息如果足够强烈，会穿过意识的屏障，而意识自我也非常乐意接收它们。但这样的直觉通常首先以梦境形式出现，后来才显现给自我；许多这样的讯息会提前在梦中出现，在情况需要时释放给意识心智。

quotes.text: Ruburt did not have to do anything in particular, for example, of a conscious nature, except to state his intentions, and the body's healing mechanisms immediately quickened. This is because he began to take the pressure off, so to speak, and really began to understand the abilities and limitations of the rational mind in its relationship to the body.
quotes.translation: 例如，鲁柏不必刻意做任何特别的事情，只需表明他的意图，身体的疗愈机制便立即加速。这是因为，可以说，他开始卸下压力，并真正开始理解理性心智在身体关系中的能力与局限。

quotes.text: If you remembered such a dream, therefore, you might think that it was precognitive, and that the event would become physical. Instead, the whole portent of the dream event would be an educational one, bringing your fear into clear focus. In such cases you should think of the dire dream situation as a shadow, and look for its source within your mind.
quotes.translation: 因此，如果你记得这样一个梦，你可能会认为它是预知性的，而事件将变成物质实相。相反，这整个梦事件的预兆会是教育性的，将你的恐惧带入清晰的焦点。在这种情况下，你应该把可怕的梦境视为一个阴影，并在你的心智中寻找其根源。

quotes.text: There is little real danger of this, but a state of dissociation is a state of dissociation, and this must be kept in mind. There is however a distinction here which he is already beginning to recognize. It is simply that he becomes vulnerable, or more sensitive, to inner data in a dissociated state, regardless of what causes the state to come about.
quotes.translation: 这几乎没有真正的危险，但解离状态就是解离状态，这一点必须牢记。然而，这里有一个他已经开始认识到的区别。简单来说，无论是什么原因导致解离状态，他都会在这种状态下变得脆弱，或对内我资料更加敏感。

quotes.text: The conscious mind is meant to make clear judgments about your position in physical reality. Often false beliefs will prevent it from making these, for the egotistically held ideas will cloud its clear vision.
quotes.translation: 意识心本应对你在物质实相中的位置做出清晰的判断。错误的信念常常会阻止它做出这些判断，因为自我所持有的观念会遮蔽它清晰的视野。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: I quite realize that many of my statements will contradict the beliefs of those of you who accept the idea that the conscious mind is relatively powerless, and that the answers to problems lie hidden beneath.
quotes.translation: 我很清楚，我的许多陈述会与那些相信意识心相对无力、问题的答案隐藏在潜意识中的人们的信念相矛盾。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The ego is your idea of your physical image in relation to the world. Your self image is not unconscious, then. You are quite aware of it, though often you reject certain thoughts about it in favor of others. False beliefs can result in a rigid ego that insists upon using the conscious mind in one direction only, further distorting its perceptions.
quotes.translation: 自我是你对自己与世界相关的物质形象的概念。因此，你的自我形象并非无意识的。你相当清楚它，尽管你常常拒绝关于它的某些想法而偏好其他想法。错误的信念可能导致一个僵化的自我，它坚持只在一个方向上使用意识心，进一步扭曲了它的感知。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There have been tyrannies propagated for various reasons by the race of man upon itself. One of the greatest, however, is the idea that the conscious mind does not have any touch with the fountains of its own being, that it is divorced from nature, and that the individual is therefore at the mercy of unconscious drives over which he has no control.
quotes.translation: 人类种族曾因各种原因对自己施加过暴政。然而，最大的暴政之一是认为意识心与自身存在的源泉毫无接触，认为它与自然分离，因此个体受制于自己无法控制的无意识驱力。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It is only when you dam them up that they appear to be opposed to the intellect, or overwhelming. It is of the utmost importance, however, that you understand the power and directing nature of your conscious mind, for otherwise you will believe yourself to be forever at the mercy of conditions and situations over which you feel you have no control.
quotes.translation: 只有当你们压抑它们时，它们才会显得与理智对立或难以承受。然而，至关重要的是，你们必须理解自己有意识心智的力量和引导性质，否则你们会相信自己永远受制于那些感觉无法掌控的环境和处境。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: We are making some simplified statements here to make the idea clear. Data, valid data, which is not materialized as physical form, at any given time still exists as potential physical form. Some such data will be materialized in your future, as for example plans existing only in the mind will later be carried out and utilized within physical form.
quotes.translation: 我们在此做一些简化的陈述以阐明这个观点。数据，有效的数据，在未物质化为具体形态时，在任何特定时间仍作为潜在的物质形态存在。其中一些数据将在你们的未来被物质化，例如仅存在于心智中的计划，日后将在物质形态中得以实施和运用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The board is a matter of formality, actually exceedingly important in that it renews contact in a familiar manner, and also I have always been partial to formality to some extent. The board gives us each a breathing spell, and is a method of saying good day or good evening or tipping one's hat. I'm also of the opinion that small ritual tends to emphasize data in the mind, and set it off to advantage, in the same way that good cuisine is set off by fine dishes.
quotes.translation: 这个板子是一种形式上的仪式，实际上极其重要，因为它以熟悉的方式重新建立联系，而且我一直以来在某种程度上偏爱形式。板子给我们每个人一个喘息的机会，也是一种说早安或晚安或脱帽致意的方式。我也认为，小小的仪式有助于在心智中强调数据，并使其凸显优势，就像精致菜肴需要精美餐具来衬托一样。

quotes.text: ("A written note, with an appeal for an answer, or implied request." I believe this is a reference to the letter Bill Ward sent me with the art work I received Sunday, October 23. quotes.translation: （"一张书面便条，附有对答复的恳求或隐含请求。"我相信这是指比尔·沃德寄给我的那封信，连同我在10月23日周日收到的艺术作品。再次参见第116页的注释。同时请记住，作为物件的账单代表了我为了完成比尔寄来的工作而购买的铅笔和纸笔头。

source_raw.title: (To my surprise last Friday, October 21, I received a call from an old fiend, Bill Ward, with whom I used to do comic books about 1940-2. He asked me to help him, probably on a regular basis, with some work, and I said yes. The work, involving inking, arrived Sunday. Wendell Crowley is a boy hood friend of Wards, and also an old friend of mine; he was my editor in New York City for some years after World War 11. I was working with him in the early 1950s. Also, see Session 290.

quotes.text: The mind breaks the impulse down into more specific terms, collects or attracts within separate fields those impulses within the same general range, and then forms from them a new electrical pattern, composed of impulses now more meaningful to the individual, because they have been somewhat deciphered and put together in a more recognizable form.
quotes.translation: 心智将冲动分解为更具体的术语，在各自领域内收集或吸引同一大致范围内的冲动，然后从中形成一种新的电性模式，由对个体更具意义的冲动组成，因为这些冲动已被部分解读并以更可识别的形式组合在一起。

quotes.text: If your Friday get-togethers have become boring, it is to a large extent because you have both allowed them to do so, and not taken the lead in directing the conversations along more responsible lines. For these young people can offer much. They look at things with fresh minds, and their questions can lead both of you along new lines of thought.
quotes.translation: 如果你们周五的聚会变得无聊，很大程度上是因为你们两人都允许它们如此，而没有主动引导对话走向更负责任的方向。因为这些年轻人可以提供很多。他们以清新的心智看待事物，他们的问题可以引导你们两人沿着新的思路前进。

quotes.text: When people profess an interest in the nature of dreams, they usually have certain set questions in mind, such as: "How real are dream events?" "What do dreams mean?" "How do they affect daily life?" Each person is aware of the astonishingly intimate nature of dreams. Despite this, certain symbols seem to be fairly universal in your experience.
quotes.translation: 当人们宣称对梦的本质感兴趣时，他们心中通常会有一些既定问题，比如："梦中的事件有多真实？""梦意味着什么？""它们如何影响日常生活？"每个人都意识到梦具有惊人的亲密性。尽管如此，某些符号在你的经验中似乎相当普遍。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your experience will follow your concentration and belief and expectation. The mind is a great discriminator. It can use its reasoning to bring about almost any possible experience within your framework.
quotes.translation: 你的体验将追随你的专注、信念和期待。心灵是一个伟大的辨别者。它能够运用其推理能力，在你的框架内带来几乎任何可能的体验。

quotes.text: At the same time all beliefs are communicated to others, not only through quite unconscious bodily mechanisms, but telepathically. You will always try to correlate your ideas with exterior experience. (Pause.) All of the abilities of the inner self will be brought to bear to materialize the image of your beliefs, regardless of what they ought to be. The "proper" emotions will be generated, bringing about those body states that exist in your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 同时，所有信念不仅通过完全无意识的身体机制，还通过心电感应传达给他人。你总会试图将你的想法与外在经验相关联。（停顿。）内在自我的所有能力都将被调动起来，以具体化你信念的形象，无论这些信念应该是什么。"适当的"情绪将被激发，带来那些存在于你意识心灵中的身体状态。

quotes.text: The brain organizes activity and translates events, but it does not initiate them. Events have an electromagnetic reality that is then projected onto the brain for physical activation. Your instruments only pick up certain levels of the brain's activity. They do not perceive the mind's activity at all, except as it is imprinted onto the brain.
quotes.translation: 大脑组织活动并翻译事件，但它并不启动它们。事件具有电磁实相，然后被投射到大脑上以进行物理激活。你的仪器只能捕捉到大脑活动的某些层面。它们完全无法感知心灵的活动，除非心灵活动被印刻在大脑上。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: You are Joseph, the Joseph you see in your mind, the blueprint. Wall or walls are the divisions between your various personalities, and also represent the times of incarnations. Sometimes you can see over the wall, sometimes it is 1 wall, and sometimes it is many. To the （wl no） whole Joseph there is no wall, but unity. I will call you Joseph.
quotes.translation: 你是约瑟，你心中所见的那个约瑟，那个蓝图。墙或墙壁是你不同人格之间的分隔，也代表转世的时间。有时你能看到墙的另一边，有时是一道墙，有时是许多道墙。对于（wl no）完整的约瑟来说，没有墙，只有统一。我将称你为约瑟。

(and this is more important than) 10 pages of notes and dates that you cannot check, since the lives were so long ago. There were however some records... not papers... that exist along the way, and sometime if it will ease your mind we will endeavor to give them to you.
quotes.translation: 你们的内在自我会消化我所说和将要说的话……（这比）十页你们无法核对的笔记和日期更重要，因为那些生命发生在如此久远的年代。不过沿途确实存在一些记录……不是纸张……有时如果能让你们安心，我们会尽力提供给你们。

quotes.text: Too many complicated issues are connected here, so that I must at best simplify. It is as if man said: "Now what about this idea? What can we do with it? What will happen if we toss it out into reality, physically? How far can we go with any of the great social, scientific, religious ideas that are so peculiarly the offshoots of man's mind?"
quotes.translation: 这里有太多复杂的问题相互关联，所以我必须尽量简化。仿佛人类在问："这个想法如何？我们能用它做什么？如果我们把它抛入物质现实，会发生什么？那些如此独特地源自人类心智的伟大社会、科学、宗教理念，我们能走多远？"
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The conscious mind does not even know, and cannot of itself command, the legs to walk across the floor. Is it any wonder, then, that the conscious mind does not know how it creates the dream universe?
quotes.translation: 意识心甚至不知道，也无法自行命令双腿走过地板。那么，意识心不知道它如何创造梦境宇宙，这又有什么好奇怪的呢？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The role-playing in the dream drama would be one in which you creatively worked out the problems that caused the imbalances to begin with. Dreams of a strongly aggressive nature in this context may be very beneficial to a given individual, allowing the release of usually inhibited feelings and freeing the body from tension. By such constant dream therapy, both body and mind regulate themselves to a large degree. So your flesh is affected by your dreams.
quotes.translation: 在梦境戏剧中的角色扮演，会让你创造性地解决最初导致失衡的问题。在这种情境下，具有强烈攻击性的梦境可能对特定个体非常有益，允许释放通常被压抑的情感，使身体摆脱紧张。通过这种持续的梦境治疗，身体和心智在很大程度上自我调节。所以你的肉体受到梦境的影响。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Basically, events are not built one upon the other. They grow out of each other in a kind of spontaneous expansion, a profusion of creativity, while the conscious mind chooses which aspects to experience -- and those aspects then become what you call an objective event.
quotes.translation: 基本上，事件并非一个建立在另一个之上。它们以一种自发的扩展、丰富的创造力相互衍生，而意识心则选择要体验哪些面向——这些面向随后成为你所谓的客观事件。

quotes.text: With many psychics, the ability is so automatic that you can learn nothing about the process as it occurs. Now I shall try to point out impressions that are incorrect when I am able to. You will be able to see how extrasensory perceptions merge with personal associations, and watch the mind as it actually learns to perceive the difference.
quotes.translation: 对于许多通灵者来说，这种能力是如此自动化，以至于你无法了解其发生的过程。现在，我会在可能时指出那些不正确的印象。你将能够看到超感官知觉如何与个人联想融合，并观察心智实际上如何学会辨别差异。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: This had no connection. The Gallaghers were at a party that evening. They were somewhat on Ruburts mind, and the name Terwilliger is a subconscious designation he uses for them. A distortion of the words Tim and Gallagher, simply because Tim has a particularly Gaelic connotation for him. More so than Bill, which is our Jesuit's first name.
quotes.translation: 这毫无关联。加拉赫一家当晚正在参加派对。鲁柏心中隐约挂念着他们，而特威利格这个姓氏是他潜意识中为他们设定的代号，源自蒂姆与加拉赫两个词的变形——只因蒂姆这个称呼在他心中带有特别强烈的盖尔语色彩，甚至超过我们那位耶稣会士的本名比尔。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: He spoke honestly when he said that he considered it intellectual suicide to accept even the possibility of personal survival of death. The conflict brought him to a point where he simply could go no further intellectually, for the intellect would not follow where the intuitions led. This caused him to think of our sessions in stereotyped ways, putting an either-or aspect to our relationship, and closing his mind to my own explanation of my existence.
quotes.translation: 他坦言，即便只是承认死后人格存续的可能性，在他看来也等同于智力上的自杀。这种冲突将他逼至智识的绝境——当直觉引领的方向无法被理性追随，他便只能以刻板模式看待我们的课程，将我们的关系置于非此即彼的对立中，从而封闭了心智对我存在本质的阐释。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: （More inner-sense delineations are to come, according to Seth. With a list of the above categories in mind however, it is clear which senses and laws apply to the material in the sessions following, up until now. I have often meant to remind Seth to mention the specific senses along with the material under discussion but have not done so.
quotes.translation: （据赛斯所言，后续将会有更多内在感官的阐述。但若牢记上述分类清单，便能清晰看出直至此刻的课程材料中，哪些感官与法则正在运作。我屡次想提醒赛斯在讨论具体材料时标注对应的感官，却始终未能付诸行动。）

quotes.text: It is not as important as you may think, since oftentimes you act with more wisdom automatically, and the conscious mind is extremely slow to assimilate knowledge that the intuitions know by heart.
quotes.translation: 这并不如你想象中那般重要，因为你们时常在无意识中展现出更睿智的作为，而显意识消化直觉早已熟稔的知识时，其速度之缓慢令人惊叹。

source_raw.title: （As Seth states, Jane did have a dream about the idea the following night, September 11,1963. She recalls it quite easily. However she has no written record of the dream, since this was before she had cultivated the habit of keeping a dream notebook. As mentioned many many sessions ago, however, her poem The Fence, written in May 1963, clearly foreshadows the Seth material, dealing with [but not always by outright name] such subjects as reincarnation, dreams, unperceived worlds, etc. [See the poem on page 28, Session 5, in Volume I of The Early Sessions]

quotes.text: If you keep this in mind, generally speaking, then you will find yourself able to do so within a brief time. It is a pleasant and easy way to achieve a projection, and with some experience you will discover that you can maintain good control, walk out of your apartment, and outside. You may then attempt normal locomotion, or levitation.
quotes.translation: 若你谨记此点，一般而言，便能在短时间内达成目标。这是实现投射的愉悦捷径，稍加练习后你会发现，自己既能保持良好控制，又能步出公寓走向户外。届时你可尝试正常移动，或体验悬浮之态。

quotes.text: There are a few points that I would like to make of a general nature. Ruburt has been reading Jung, though not consistently. The libido does not originate in the individual subconscious of the present personality. It originates instead in the energy of the entity and inner self, and is directed by means of the inner senses, outward so to speak, through the deeper layers of the individual subconscious mind, then through the outer or personal layers.
quotes.translation: 我想就几个普遍性问题稍作说明。鲁柏近来虽未持续研读，却一直在翻阅荣格著作。力比多并非源自当前人格的个体潜意识，而是发端于存有与内在自我的能量，经由内在感官向外传导——可以说，它穿透个体潜意识的深层结构，再流经外在或人格表层。

quotes.text: This can be compared and correctly to errors in a painting. When you are in a poor state of mind, you automatically affect the others you meet, negatively. You then react to their behavior and complete the circle. Now this leads into a highly charged Emotional environment, which is the cause of the depressions of which you have spoken.
quotes.translation: 这可以恰当地比作画作中的错误。当你处于糟糕的心境时，你会自动负面地影响你遇到的其他人。然后你对他们行为的反应又完成了这个循环。这会导致高度情绪化的环境，这正是你所说的抑郁的成因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Now. It is a big universe, and we do not travel in the same circles. When I have information for you from him, then I pass it on. Since he seems to keep you in mind sufficiently enough to give the messages, you can presume that he will continue to do so; and when he clearly communicates with me, then I will question him in that regard.
quotes.translation: 宇宙浩瀚，我们并不在相同的圈子里活动。当我从他那里得到给你的信息时，我会传递给你。既然他似乎足够挂念你而传递讯息，你可以假设他会继续这样做；当他与我清晰沟通时，我会就此向他提问。
